Name: The Quarter
Author: Radinsa Dharmadasa
Review: Food was really nice and the staff were lovely. We got the pork belly and lamb ribs for main and the tiramisu and mudcake for dessert. A thing to note is the dessert portion sizes are massive so you can definitely share one between people. My mum and I got one each and ended up taking half of both home.

Name: The Quarter
Author: Cass C
Review:
With so many options in Degraves, I decided to try The Quarter, but unfortunately, it didn’t quite live up to expectations. I ordered the pulled pork eggs Benedict and the New York bagel, but both dishes were missing key elements. The eggs Benedict was supposed to come with crunchy crackling, which was absent, and the New York bagel didn’t have the mayo as advertised on the menu.

On the bright side, the eggs were cooked well, and the Hollandaise sauce was tasty. However, the service left something to be desired—although our waiter was good the service from the food runners felt a bit abrupt, which added to the overall disappointment.

With so many great spots nearby, I might explore other options next time.

Name: The Quarter
Author: Parttimepiggy
Review:
One thing I love about Melbourne is the hidden laneway gems like these ⭐️ you have to check this place out if you haven’t already!

What we ordered:
🍃 Zucchini Flowers $26
🤍 Scallops Linguine $34.50
🍅 Rigatoni all'Amatriciana $27
🤎 Tiramisu $16.50

The food and the ambience here makes for the perfect hang out spot whether you're having a date night or catching up with friends & family. They even had live music on when we visited on a Thursday evening and it was such a vibe!

Onto the food, everything we ate was yum but my favourite dish was definitely the rigatoni. I'm not normally a big fan of tomato / red sauce base pastas but wow this one was sensational. The pasta was perfectly al dente and the bits of pancetta were pan fried to perfection.

Also if you come here make sure to leave some room for their dessert! I was pleasantly surprised at how big the tiramisu serving was and it was soooo delicious. I like that it wasn’t overly sweet and it was actually the perfect size to share between two.

You can find this charming restaurant on Degraves Street which is just a two minute walk away from Flinders Street Station.

Name: The Quarter
Author: Rhys G
Review: Beautiful cafe, well designed. The atmosphere is perfect. The staff and their attention to their customers is exceptional and the food is 10/10. I highly recommend the "Quarter Eggs Benny". The pulled pork is exceptional.

Name: The Quarter
Author: Vivian William
Review: The chorizo omelette was delicious. Eggs benedict was also very unique and tasty. Coffee was nice. Good little cafe for brunch.

Name: Manchester Press
Author: Amy (thehungrymelbgirl)
Review:
Nestled deep inside Rankins Ln, Manchester Press draws a consistent crowd with its bagels and warehouse vibes.

The last time I dined here was 7 years ago. The chorizo bagel I had back then was well put together and very tasty. The lack of customer service was, however, quite off-putting.

We recently tried Manchester Press again, and we can't fault the warm staff at all. Unfortunately, it was the food that didn't quite hit the mark this time.

We ordered the Bacon and Maple Bagel ($24.50), which on paper sounds like the perfect combination of sweet and savoury. However, it was actually quite difficult to get all the flavours together as it was quite textural. We also weren't sure whether the blueberry bagel was the best flavour to compliment this creation.

We fared a bit better with the Philly Steak Bagel ($23.80), although the chef may have gone a bit overboard with the chipotle mayo and the overall result was not that aesthetically pleasing.

Hojicha Latte ($6.50) is on its current special menu for those wanting to try something different.

It was overall a slightly underwhelming experience, but I am hoping it was only because we went on a bad day...

Name: Manchester Press
Author: Pauline
Review:
It’s been many years since I visited Manchester Press.

🥯🫐 I’ve had very fond memories of their blueberry bagel served with the best mascarpone cream mixed with berries and pistachio, so I decided to visit them again after at least 5 years. It most definitely did not disappoint even though the way they whip the cream is now a little different.

I highly recommend the mixed berry bagel if you want a sweet brunch.

🍳 We also had their Egg Benedict and it was superb with a generous serving of bacon.

🍯 Another bagel we tried was their honey ricotta toast, I would have preferred they served a bigger portion of ricotta rather than thinly spread it on the bagel. I wouldn’t mind paying more for it too. I suppose this highlights the bagel rather than the topping.

Name: Manchester Press
Author: Emily Holm
Review:
This place used to be one of the *it* places in Melbourne. And now…. It’s fine?
Our coffee was still pretty good actually. But our bagels didn’t look or taste nearly as exciting and they used to.
Don’t get me wrong… they were absolutely fine. Just nothing special. The smoked salmon looks unhappy and tasted like supermarket salmon, and the blueberries were sparing.
There was nothing super wrong, but also nothing super right.

Name: Manchester Press
Author: Ariaan
Review: Service and atmosphere is amazing here! Staff is always so friendly and it’s so cozy inside. I ordered an oat mocha and ham and cheese toasted sandwich takeaway. Oat mocha tasted bland and watery. Toasted sandwich was crunchy and buttery however there wasn’t a lot of cheese and the ham was very salty. The other sandwiches on their menu look amazing though so definitely recommend trying one of those instead! Bagels looked delicious!

Name: Manchester Press
Author: Cindy Ramli Chang
Review:
Brunch date with the one and only on our wedding anniversary day ❤️
That day I decided that I do not like bagels 🤣
The sandwich was beautiful
Bagels topping was good but I just figured that I do not like that texture. So dense 🤣
But apparently “Good bagels are denser than most bread,”
Well guess I just don’t like it 🙈

The place was buzzing with people enjoying their food. Young, old, kids, adult, they all enjoying the vibes there.
Will come back for more sandwiches 😁

#bulgogi_n_rendang

Name: Proud Mary Coffee
Author: Siren (Siren)
Review:
Great little Cafe with a warm vibe. It feels full but you always have space to move and feel comfortable. The decor adds to this
Food was fast and delicious. The Hash just needed a touch more salt.

Name: Proud Mary Coffee
Author: manooch
Review: If you want to have different experiences with morning coffee definitely you need to get there and get them a go. They have got a specific menu just for coffee that brings you knowledge about the variety of coffee beans.  Also, they got delicious breakfast and very helpful staff.

Name: Proud Mary Coffee
Author: Tristan
Review: My experience at Proud Mary Coffee was a bit underwhelming, especially after hearing so many high compliments. While they seem to know their coffee well, my soy flat white was disappointing—the foam on top ruined the taste and didn’t look appealing either. The kimchi avo on toast tasted great, but the kimchi wasn’t the traditional Korean spicy kind, and while the toppings were delicious, the bacon felt overlooked. I admire their experimental twist on classic brunch dishes, but the execution still needs some polishing. Considering this place is a bit expensive, I did expect the dish to be perfect. I’ll give this place another chance.

Name: Proud Mary Coffee
Author: Derek Hum
Review:
Great place for coffee and a meal. Dishes are unique and delicious. The tiramisu hotcakes was a nice dessert which we shared - might've been too much for one I think.

Piccolo was good and the cold drip was refreshing, if a bit small.

Name: Proud Mary Coffee
Author: Simon T
Review: Excellent spot for coffee and breakfast. Be sure to ask for today's coffee recommendations. Always busy and vibrant

Name: Riché - Cafe Richmond
Author: Tolga O
Review:
We had a fantastic experience at Riche, and highly recommend it. The menu has plenty of creative options that let it stand out from your generic local cafe. The food is well, portioned and tastes great.
The atmosphere is energetic, so I'd highly recommend a booking if coming on the weekend.
My partner had the pumpkin king, and along with my Riche burger, we enjoyed both meals and left full.
On the drinks side we enjoyed a black sesame latte (surprisingly enjoyable), and a berry smoothie (tasted healthy and light).
We'd definitely come again.

Name: Riché - Cafe Richmond
Author: Ivan Lu
Review: I visited Riché Cafe on a weekday morning, and it was moderately busy. I ordered a soy latte, which had nice latte art and tasted great. However, I also tried the Prawny Business dish. Unfortunately, there were only 3-4 small shrimp, and they were a bit overcooked and dry, almost like they had been pre-cooked and then re-fried. On a positive note, the service was excellent; the staff checked in on me several times to ensure everything was okay. Overall, it was a mixed experience.

Name: Riché - Cafe Richmond
Author: Nana Pun
Review: This restaurant is one of the best brunch places I’ve ever visited. We must go again; the food is incredibly delicious with a fusion design that offers a fresh take on classic dishes. The bread, French toast, and pumpkin base are all wonderfully soft, and the seasoning enhances every flavor. Words can’t fully describe how much we enjoyed the whole experience. Highly recommended!

Name: Riché - Cafe Richmond
Author: William Hua
Review:
A lovely cafe with a wide variety of food options, a clean and aesthetic interior, as well as friendly service staff.

The speed of deliver is also something I must mention, all the dishes we ordered come out in around 10-15 min, despite the cafe being relatively lively at the time.

The price was definitely reasonable as well. So deffs worth a try or making this place a regular spot if you live close by!

Name: Riché - Cafe Richmond
Author: Matt
Review: Food was OK, but nothing special. You can find a better burger almost anywhere, but the chips were good. Long black and curry laksa also nice but quite average.

Name: The League of Honest Coffee
Author: Sonja Pitt
Review:
Pretty abysmal food and coffee on a Saturday morning. Thought they’d have more specialty coffee type stuff, but coffee served in a bowl certainly isn’t it… Did someone forget to wash the mugs or cups??

Only a few toasties left at 10am, no kitchen…. Had one tomato and cheese and one ham and cheese croissant. Gas station quality. Basically no ham/cheese/tomato in them at all. Not nearly enough for breakfast for one person. Some thick cut tomatoes and less stingy on the ham and tomato would have made up for the lack of quality elsewhere.

Filter coffee was OK, but probably under extracted. Lighter roast or better selection would have been better.

Maybe come here if you’re after just a quick to-go coffee, will probably be better than a takeaway elsewhere… But pretty sad for dine-in.

Name: The League of Honest Coffee
Author: Abizer Mohmedi
Review:
Had an honest “bowl” of Flat White to start my day, with a delicious Chk-Avocado-Spinach toastie
The coffee was well made with perfectly steamed and textured milk. The bean flavour was coming through so well 🤎😍
The toastie had some peppercorn kernels adding the right amount of heat at intervals. With the cheese and spinach, it was very flavoursome. I could have done with more avocado though- it was hard to find/taste.
This is my second trip here, while visiting the city and it’s becoming my go-to café! ☕️
Well done, guys ✨🌟

Name: The League of Honest Coffee
Author: Lucy Bui
Review: Delicious hot chocolate, a little rich for my liking but was very well made. We ordered a chicken sandwich which was also great, a little salty but very enjoyable. I was disappointed that they did not have any matcha but the lovely staff and cozy atmosphere definitely made up for it. Excited to return and try out more of the menu!

Name: The League of Honest Coffee
Author: John Domingo
Review:
Ordered an oat mocha, iced latte, mushroom toastie, and cheese & tomato toastie. The cup for the mocha was really cute! The coffee was amazing. The toasties were pretty filling.

The iced latte was on the smaller size, but would probably just need to order a bigger cup next time.

Name: The League of Honest Coffee
Author: IL Deko
Review: This cafe is just opposite to the hotel so it’s the most convenient when you need that caffeine in the morning. It’s super duper busy with the working crowds coming in for their cuppa and you would think the coffee must be great. Unfortunately NOT the case, the long black is under extracted and taste awfully sour. Definitely won’t drop in again.

Name: Station Street Cafe
Author: Andy T
Review: "I absolutely love this cafe! The atmosphere is cozy and welcoming, perfect for catching up with friends and family. The staff is incredibly friendly and attentive, always ready with a smile. The coffee is Exceptional! Food was absolutely delicious, and they offer a wide variety of options to suit every taste. I can't wait to return!"

Name: Station Street Cafe
Author: Serena C
Review:
Really good coffee and tasty food. Owners and staff are very friendly and will remember you by name. Great family Cafe to visit on the weekends 😊
Will visit again!

Name: Station Street Cafe
Author: MIRACLE MIRIIN
Review:
I was there this morning to get some coffee, muffin and banana bread. The barista is very unwelcoming and not even greeting.
No smile No service No communication Not even thank you.
And the worst part is the coffee.
I asked for mocha strong chocolate and look what I got. The coffee is not even full and how on earth the Mocha has no froth?
This is not even considered a Flat White.
This is unacceptable at all.
I pay around $16 for this?
And yes she ruined my morning. 😕

Name: Station Street Cafe
Author: Ean Sugarman
Review:
Interesting Breakfast cafe but nobody there and service felt indifferent.
On the outside this looks like a great place. Food was timely and tasty but not hot.
Maybe it was empty because it was lunchtime but my young-ish server who was efficient and courteous didn’t seem to want to be there.
Food good, service appropriate but not warm and atmosphere fine but no one there. I would go back to see if anything changed.

Name: Station Street Cafe
Author: Two Sheds
Review:
Station St Cafe

3.7

Cute cafe right beside the station as the name suggests.  Surprisingly large and not unpleasantly decorated with occasional splashed of greenery.  Good music playing.    Overall, there’s a good relaxed feel here.  Quick stop only today as I was time poor.  The coffee here was ace.  Rich, silky and powerful.  Just as I like it.  A good tasty reasonably priced egg, cheese and bacon muffin was had as well.  Service was friendly and cheerful.  On what I saw today, I’m not surprised this cafe is well regarded.

Name: Golden Mug Cafe
Address: Ground Floor of Novotel Hotel, 399 Little Lonsdale St, Melbourne
Author: Elaine V. Reglos
Review:
Went to dine in with my grandma and auntie as they were on a tourist visa. We spotted this cozy cute cafe and tried some of their dish.

The amount of the food is huge and delicious. Coffee is very good as well, we have experience the best customer service as well her name’s Vanessa. Will definitely come back for another experience!

Name: Golden Mug Cafe
Address: Ground Floor of Novotel Hotel, 399 Little Lonsdale St, Melbourne
Author: Jennie
Review: The steak at this restaurant is an unforgettable delight. Each bite of the steak is tender, juicy, and perfectly grilled, with a delicious flavor. The prawn salad is refreshing and delicious, with fresh prawn complemented by crisp lettuce and salad dressing, bringing a refreshing seafood flavor. As for the garlic cheese bread, it's a wonderful combination, with a soft texture and a surface sprinkled with rich cheese and garlic flavor, each bite is a delight. Overall, this meal is satisfying and a feast for the taste buds.

Name: Golden Mug Cafe
Address: Ground Floor of Novotel Hotel, 399 Little Lonsdale St, Melbourne
Author: Hara
Review: I had high expectations, but my experience was disappointing. I ordered the bacon eggs benedict, and the eggs were too sour. Is this really bacon? There wasn't even a hash brown! The place felt busy but didn't deliver on quality. I won't be coming back

Name: Golden Mug Cafe
Address: Ground Floor of Novotel Hotel, 399 Little Lonsdale St, Melbourne
Author: Yosy Christy Natalia
Review:
Perfect spot to grab your late afternoon coffee cuz they open until 6pm!

Comfortable sitting table on the Ground floor of Novotel hotel. They have so many various of foods (breakfast, lunch and dinner menu). Coffee is also great!

Highly recommend!

Name: Golden Mug Cafe
Address: Ground Floor of Novotel Hotel, 399 Little Lonsdale St, Melbourne
Author: Sylvia Liu
Review: We had a delightful brunch at this spot, enjoying a variety of dishes including chili scramble, egg royal, seafood penne pasta, and a fresh fruit bowl. The drinks were also standout, with both the chai latte and iced taro being refreshing and flavorful. Everything was delicious.

Name: George Street Cafe
Author: Erica Kingham-Bradley
Review:
Stopped in for a Saturday lunch; what a hidden gem. The food came very quickly despite being busy.
Everything from the drinks to the chips were delicious. The staff were very friendly.

Will definitely be back!

Name: George Street Cafe
Author: Mel
Review: Beautiful coffee and breakfast close to Quest East Melbourne. Absolutely recommend this lovely coffee shop. The latte was perfect and smashed avo on toast with poached eggs was just the way I like it, runny yolk with cooked whites.

Name: George Street Cafe
Author: David Spencer
Review: Generously sized breakfast offerings but they were a little underwhelming in flavour.  The service is cheerful and prompt.  Decent coffee.  Would score them three and half if I could.

Name: George Street Cafe
Author: Paul Richards
Review: A nice cafe, just a short walk from Wellington Parade. The beef brisket toastie/burger (a lunch special) was very good, as was the mango smoothie. I'll be back!

Name: George Street Cafe
Author: Tanja Genovese (muppet)
Review:
We absolutely love George Street Cafe. It's become our new fortnightly place to dine. The food is always fresh and delicious, and the coffee always tastes great.

The atmosphere is great, and Michael and his team are amazing and friendly. We always leave smiling and happy.

Name: Crest Café
Author: Christopher Lai
Review:
Great food, excellent coffee
We've been here a few times now.
Food is always good and coffee is to our liking.

They have a kids menu which is always a bit plus.
They also sell candy which is fun for the kids.

I just wish this place is warmer in the winters and cooler in the summer but that is just a minor thing.

We will be back. Definitely the best cafe in the knox area!

Name: Crest Café
Author: Claudine Faerie
Review:
Nice cafe, tucked away in the side streets of suburbia. Coffees and hot drinks were good! Menu had lots of favourites and some items with a fun twist! Our food was well presented and delicious and we predominantly enjoyed our meals...The green hashbrown dish was yummy ( but came served with a hair and the fritters were cold inside)
We did have difficulties making our reservation ( getting someone to answer the phone took 5 tries over 2 days)

Name: Crest Café
Author: MTWTFSS
Review: Hidden gem in the neighbourhood! Coffee is always done well! The cafe is kids and pets friendly! Very thoughtful staff! Our favorite cafe!

Name: Crest Café
Author: King Katt
Review: The corn fritter was good, and the coffee was nice too. The poached egg can be improved.  However, one of the female staff members was not friendly.

Name: Crest Café
Author: Shayan Soroush
Review:
Small cafe near Knox shopping centre, talked away in the local streets and if you don’t look close you’ll think it’s an ordinary house, cute atmosphere.

I ordered the golden latte with tumeric, almond milk and cinnamon. It had such a rich and spicy flavour, highly recommend.

The staff were very attentive and friendly 🙏

Name: Two Birds One Stone Cafe South Yarra
Author: Weiqi Wang
Review: The dining experience was excellent. We ordered two dishes: Fresh Chili Scrambled Eggs and Sauteed Mixed Mushrooms. The Fresh Chili Scrambled Eggs were different from what you usually find elsewhere; they used fresh chilies, and the eggs were incredibly tender. The Sauteed Mixed Mushrooms featured a variety of mushrooms served with two poached eggs on top of a slice of toast, which had a firm and crispy texture—healthy and delicious. Both dishes were very generous in portion, leaving us feeling full. We'll definitely be back next time.

Name: Two Birds One Stone Cafe South Yarra
Author: Melody
Review: Great dining experience! Both meals are delicious! The shape is also very good-looking ~ Lamb pasta recommended! The lamb stew is very soft, rich sauce wrapped in the pasta, really delicious! Another dessert is also a surprise, the sweetness is just right! My friend loves hot chocolate. It's delicious! Service and dining environment are very good! Will come again!

Name: Two Birds One Stone Cafe South Yarra
Author: Suzanne Kehl
Review: We were here for brunch and it was pretty good. Our group had a mixture of eggs & bacon, pancakes and scrambled eggs with kids meals.  Everything was delicious. Coffee was good too. A fairly busy place. The only negative was the booths are quite tight to get into. My daughter is pregnant and couldn't get in at all so had to sit on a stool. Service was good so overall a lovely place to go for brunch.

Name: Two Birds One Stone Cafe South Yarra
Author: Shayini Rattambige
Review: I was craving to get a french toast on a Saturday and found this place. Honestly,  the atmosphere is great, it's a cosy cafe with great design. It was busy on a Saturday, I recommend that you reserve in advance to get a better seat. Staff were too busy to actually provide good service. Food was mediocre, I'd go again when it's less busy to check it out. Prices are high for the quality of food. I got 2 coffee's, a french toast and my husband got a full breakfast, the breakfast dish was actually a good portion. We paid $75.

Name: Two Birds One Stone Cafe South Yarra
Author: Daisy W
Review: Coming back to Two Birds One Stone Cafe in South Yarra after years was so nice. It’s still a perfect spot for brunch with friends. The Breakfast Board gave me with a serious case of food envy as I ordered the grilled salmon. The pancakes, were just as good as I remembered - fluffy and perfectly cooked. The service was also excellent as they accommodated me with the wheelchair. Two Birds One Stone remains a top choice for brunch in South Yarra.

Name: Foundry Lane Cafe
Author: XZ C
Review:
It's a delightful experience that I purchased coffee from this Cafe.

Friendly staff and very warm welcoming environment, especially in a chilly morning, this coffee and her beautiful smile made my day.
Thank you so much Elle. :-)

Strongly recommend to people who working or traveling near 150 Lansdale St in city. The waiting area in the lobby is also very impressive.

Name: Foundry Lane Cafe
Author: My Uno Support
Review: Great coffee, delicious pastries & friendly service

Name: Foundry Lane Cafe
Author: Jason Berek-Lewis
Review: The best coffee I've had in the city in years. The strong piccolo or 3/4 latte is beautifully balanced, perfectly smooth and outstandingly delicious. The small bikkie on top in the icing on the cake.

Name: Foundry Lane Cafe
Author: Boris
Review:
The coffee and food are photo prefect
The taste is even better then how good it looks or smells
Staff are friendly and happy to take on a request if one is made.
The atmosphere makes you feel like your on top of the world
Clean, elegant and spacious
Cafe has dine in if your not in a rush

Name: Foundry Lane Cafe
Author: M Zubair
Review:
I had to visit this coffee spot after seeing the positive reviews and decor.
The service was fantastic and my cappuccino was of a high standard. Great coffee hardware behind the counter and an experienced barista made the investment worthwhile. Definitely somewhere to purchase great coffee.

Name: ONA Coffee Melbourne | Cafe Brunswick
Author: Cambell Charrier
Review: Such an amazing experience all around!!  Delicious food, even better coffee, and the best people.  I attended a latte art class with Victor and he was a great teacher.  Super patient and knowledgeable, and taught a really fun class!  Devin was also super nice and helpful when selecting coffee and giving recommendations for brunch.  11/10 experience!!

Name: ONA Coffee Melbourne | Cafe Brunswick
Author: Tianzhuo Jing
Review:
ONA Coffee Melbourne is an absolute gem in the heart of Brunswick’s iconic café district. ☕️I recently visited and was blown away by the incredible food and coffee. I ordered the beef burger, which was exceptional. The beef was top-notch, cooked to perfection, and came with crispy chips. My partner had the smoked salmon, and the salmon was fresh and flavorful.

for drinks, I went with the customer favorite from the menu, and it did not disappoint! The special blend, which combines the unique flavors of  pistachio coffee, was outstanding. The barista crafted an iced coffee that was truly one-of-a-kind. The flavor was rich and smooth—something you won’t find anywhere else. 🥳

The atmosphere is also fantastic, surrounded by lush plants 🌱 with a cozy outdoor seating area that’s perfect for relaxing.

I highly recommend this place for anyone looking for great coffee and food in Melbourne!

Name: ONA Coffee Melbourne | Cafe Brunswick
Author: Andy Tran
Review:
Beautiful and spacious Cafe in Brunswick.

Food was really tasty and with an open kitchen was able to see the chef work their magic and the food coffee was really nice.

Highly recommend if you are in the area !

Name: ONA Coffee Melbourne | Cafe Brunswick
Author: Pesco Palate
Review:
Love this cafe! The staff is always really nice. Spacious, and nice decor and menu too!

Booked a few times for large groups of around 7-8 by emailing. Service is always efficient despite our group size or how busy it is.

Didn’t take photos but their latte art is great. Especially loved the poodle! Potato rosti and kale were particularly good.

Highly recommend you stop by and try something from their tasty and interesting menu.

Name: ONA Coffee Melbourne | Cafe Brunswick
Author: Liz L
Review: Spacious warehouse turned cafe with bright decor. The service was prompt and friendly. The coffee was great, was impressed with the variety available and really enjoyed my drink. The food was delicious, the flavours and textures all blended well together. It was a nice place for a brunch catch up.

Name: Rathdowne Street Cafe
Author: Xiaohan Pan
Review: Great experience here! The service is 5-star for both humans and fur friends. The coffees and food are delicious with generous servings. Will definitely visit again!

Name: Rathdowne Street Cafe
Author: Aaron Soroka
Review: Such an unassuming cafe surrounded by some more popular, known places that you wouldn't give much thought too as it's not much to look at. However, the service here is top notch, super friendly and accommodating. The food on offer is more than you'll see on the menu and it offers a great variety of dine in and takeaway options. Good is full of flavour and good portions. Fresh juices and smoothies and coffees too. Worth a visit

Name: Rathdowne Street Cafe
Author: Mohamed Irba
Review:
Stopped here for breakfast mid week and had a lovely time. Staff were really nice and welcoming and it was nice seeing different options rather than just egg and bacon dishes.

We ended up with the falafel plate and foul medammus (fava beans) which were tasty.

Great place and will be back.

Name: Rathdowne Street Cafe
Author: Nazifa Ajayoglu
Review: This café is a gem. Eating al fresco under the  tree canopy in beautiful Rathdowne St. North Carlton, is a truly Melbourne experience. Alex and Mary are wonderful warm hosts who make you feel part of their extended family. I have also found that they are happy to accommodate my needs, as I suffer from food allergies and because their food is freshly prepared they know all the ingredients present. Every meal I have had there is delicious, fresh and reasonably priced. And off course the coffee is up to Melbourne's exacting standards. Thank you Alex and Mary.

Name: Rathdowne Street Cafe
Author: Sam Khalil
Review: One of the best dishes I’ve ever had. The hash brown is to die for. So fresh definitely recommend.

Name: Oh Happy Day Cafe
Address: Tribecca Village Shopping Centre, Shop 11/2 Kenswick St, Point Cook
Author: Aravindh Vijayakumar
Review: Went on Father’s Day without booking, which was a bad choice. Coffee was amazing, one of the best I had though my friend ordered extra hot but didn’t get it hot. Great options for breakfast and it tasted good.

Name: Oh Happy Day Cafe
Address: Tribecca Village Shopping Centre, Shop 11/2 Kenswick St, Point Cook
Author: E
Review:
Where do I start!
It’s been such a hot streak with dining because this cafe / restaurant has ticked all my boxes once again!

Quiet and tucked away in deep point cook just off point cook road is this beautiful cafe with wonderful welcoming staff.

I ordered a make your own breakfast, scrambled eggs on sourdough bread along with smoked salmon and this bar far was such beautiful creamy eggs! Had a cheesy aftertaste along with what I think is a herb that seasoned the plate. A wonderful compliment to the eggs!
Not much to say about simple scrambled eggs but I want to highlight them noticeably better!
Their observant nature lead to them bringing my partner a new pair of eggs after his poached eggs came out every so slightly over done.

He had no problem about it but it seemed they have noticed. Such hospitality!

The waitress who took care of us, Alex! (Went back to ask and again AMAZING service and food’), was a beautiful woman! Such a refreshing personality! Beautiful to interact with, helped us with a bee too.!

Nonetheless, this cafe needs more traction , it’s all round a 10!

Name: Oh Happy Day Cafe
Address: Tribecca Village Shopping Centre, Shop 11/2 Kenswick St, Point Cook
Author: He-man R
Review: We visited Oh Happy Day Café on Father's Day and enjoyed their coffee. We also tried the chilli scrambled eggs with mushrooms for breakfast, along with some almond croissants. Overall, it was a good experience.

Name: Oh Happy Day Cafe
Address: Tribecca Village Shopping Centre, Shop 11/2 Kenswick St, Point Cook
Author: Catherine Naranjo
Review:
I can honestly say is been the best and most consistent cafe/restaurant I've been to.

I have tried 90% of the menu and oh man all the food is fantastic but not only the food, but the people also are so amazing and welcoming they are true to their name, and every time I go ( quite often) they make my day HAPPY, also their coffee is one of my favourites, I drink long blacks no sugar, they have the perfect formula.

I'm so excited to be able to go in the evening, every meal you make guys is absolutely amazing.

I remember taking the photos when is too late and I'd eaten mostly already, meals are so good that I can't hold my excitement when the food arrives.

Also, prices are really fair

Name: Oh Happy Day Cafe
Address: Tribecca Village Shopping Centre, Shop 11/2 Kenswick St, Point Cook
Author: Vanessa Bechara
Review:
Amazing food and drinks. Usually attend for breakfast or lunch.
Just found out the apple juice is made fresh from actually apples! Just amazing!

Name: Cafe Court
Author: Debajyoti Saha
Review: Went for brunch on a Sunday morning and this place surprised us with the amazing food and the beverages. The place is nice and cozy with good decors. We ordered some freshly squeezed juice and an iced mocha and both were really good. For food we ordered the traditional Benedict and Chilli Scrambled eggs. Both the dishes looked visually pleasing and the taste was even better. Would highly recommend this place for brunch!

Name: Cafe Court
Author: Megan Dunne
Review:
Busy little cafe, open on Sunday, we had a large family group of 8 and weren’t sure how we would go getting a table but luckily there was one big enough for us inside!
The food was delicious, I had the brisket bun, tried my brother’s omelette, others had poached eggs, bowl of chips, smashed avocado, and the double cheeseburger.
The service was really great, professional friendly flexible and accommodating. We had a really lovely catch up and will definitely be returning when in Melbourne again.

Name: Cafe Court
Author: D Cummins
Review: We were pleasantly surprised with this cafe!  The pancakes were absolutely delicious and not too sweet, I highly recommend!  My partner had their original omelette and said it was good but needed a little more flavor.  Service was fast and friendly.   I only took a picture of the pancakes as it was very picture worthy!  Go!  See for yourself!

Name: Cafe Court
Author: Jasmine Drake
Review: Cool unique decor and plenty of seating. Nice and open plan. Service is impeccable. Staff noticed we only had one menu and gave us another without us even asking. They brought cutlery to our table and the food came out hot fresh and quick. Flavours of the chorizo, mushrooms and relish are amazing in the big brekkie. Nice hollandaise sauce on the Benedict.  Poached eggs to perfection. Coffee is very strong. They can do iced lattes even though its not on the menu, they have flavored syrups but they are quite weak so would reccomend extra if you prefer more sweetness

Name: Cafe Court
Author: Alexis Soo
Review: This cafe in Melbourne is fantastic for brunch! The food is delicious and beautifully presented, with generous portions that are sure to satisfy. The service is excellent, making the whole experience even more enjoyable. Plus, the menu is highly customizable with vegan and gluten-free options available. Highly recommended for anyone looking for a great brunch spot!

Name: CIEL Cafe
Author: Shirley Seow
Review:
This is such a nice cafe at Southbank! Was on holiday in Melbourne  and found this cafe when walking back to the apartment.
The staff here are very friendly and helpful. Not forgetting to mention, coffee and food are above my expectations. Ordered the Eggs on Toast and Chicken burger. And they tasted so good. Love it and will definitely be back again

Name: CIEL Cafe
Author: Tobi Jacobs
Review:
**Review for Ciel Cafe, Southbank**

I recently had the pleasure of enjoying breakfast at **Ciel Cafe** in Southbank, and it was an experience worth raving about! Nestled in a spacious ground floor location, this cafe truly is a hidden gem in the local area.

I opted for the **Breakfast Burger**, which was an absolute delight. The burger featured a perfectly cooked egg with a runny yolk, pork and fennel sausage, bacon onion, and coffee jam, sauerkraut , ripe house burger sauce and swiss cheese, all sandwiched between glossy milk buns. Each bite was a burst of flavor, and the ingredients were clearly fresh and of high quality. The burger was priced at a reasonable $20

To complement the burger, I ordered a side of their **homemade hash browns**. These golden-brown delights were crispy on the outside and fluffy on the inside, making them the perfect accompaniment. The side of three hash browns cost **$6**.

To top it all off, I enjoyed a large **Cappuccino** with intricate latte art. The coffee was rich and smooth, providing the perfect start to my day. The cappuccino was priced at **$5**.

What truly set this experience apart was the **extremely friendly and welcoming service** by the staff. From the moment I walked in, I was greeted with warm smiles and attentive service. The staff made sure I felt right at home and were more than happy to accommodate any requests.

The cafe itself boasts a modern and stylish design with large windows that allow natural light to flood the space, creating a bright and welcoming atmosphere. The decor is minimalist, with clean lines and neutral colors, giving the space a sophisticated and elegant feel.

Overall, Ciel Cafe in Southbank is a must-visit for anyone looking for a delicious breakfast in a welcoming and stylish setting. I can't wait to return and try more of their offerings!

Name: CIEL Cafe
Author: Lore Gutierrez
Review: Ciel Cafe is such an amazing spot! The service is excellent, always friendly and attentive, which makes the experience even more enjoyable. The food is delicious, with a great variety of fresh and beautifully presented options. The place also has a super cute vibe, perfect for relaxing or catching up with friends. Definitely a place I would come back to again and again. Highly recommended!

Name: CIEL Cafe
Author: Graeldon
Review:
Hidden Gem!
The food and drinks here are fantastic and really reasonably priced - I'm super happy that I stumbled upon this place, and it deserves being 10 times busier.
The food came out pretty quickly, and I was impressed by the quality. The potato served with the eggs benedict was crispy on the outside but fluffy and creamy on the inside. The bacon was high quality, and the eggs were just right.
I also tried the breakfast roti 2.0, and the roti was better than I've had in some Indian restaurants. The pork belly was quite fatty but full of flavour and well balanced by the pickled veg.
Both dishes were really thoughtful and balanced, and I felt like the chef(s) really care about the quality of the dishes they create.

I can't wait to go back and try more of the menu, it was simply fantastic.
Thank you CIEL Cafe!

Name: CIEL Cafe
Author: Sayada Korkitkwamdee
Review: Excellent service even though it was a busy period, food and coffee was so nice and reasonable price. I would recommend a chilli scrambled eggs with croissant. I will definitely come back again.

Name: Moonlight Cafe - Broadmeadows Central
Author: Allannah Gibbs
Review: We are on holiday from Nth Qld my dad and I. We have never had such AMAZING and FRIENDLY service in our lives. Such a wonderful experience and 110% recommend. I don't often review places I go but this service was personal, kind and so warm. From being bumped into around the shopping centre to then being spoken to and served with such a warmth, made the shopping trip well worth it. Thank you, thank you, thank you.

Name: Moonlight Cafe - Broadmeadows Central
Author: Lidia Charles
Review: The best coffee ever ♥️♥️ I love u guys I can't start my day without their coffee

Name: Moonlight Cafe - Broadmeadows Central
Author: Mo Shaz
Review:
Title: A Coffee Lover's Paradise

This CoffeeShop is a true haven for any coffee enthusiast. From the moment you take your first sip, you're transported to a world of rich, aromatic perfection. The coffee here is nothing short of amazing - it's clear that every cup is a labor of love. The beans are clearly of the highest quality, resulting in a brew that's smooth, full-bodied, and incredibly satisfying. Whether you're a fan of a classic espresso or a specialty latte, each cup is a testament to their dedication to the craft. The baristas are true artists, mastering the art of the perfect pour and creating intricate latte art that's almost too beautiful to disturb. This CoffeeShop has truly set the standard for what exceptional coffee should be. Don't miss out on this caffeinated masterpiece!

Name: Moonlight Cafe - Broadmeadows Central
Author: Creative Shopfitting
Review:
We have known khan and his wife for many years after being regular customers. His coffee, food and customer service are excellent! khan asked us to refit his existing store and we have just finished. I’d recommend this cafe to anybody within the Broadmeadows shopping centre precinct.

Thank you.

Name: Moonlight Cafe - Broadmeadows Central
Author: Richard T.Whybrow
Review:
Delicious dinning or takeaway food, excellent drop in for a coffee with friends for a meet between shopping or just for a chat. Location is easy to find and very spacious in the bright and open central atrium.  It’s relaxing to just sit and enjoy the bright open cafe space with a delightful beverage.

Service is quick and efficient with cool designs displayed on the top of your hot drink.

Sweet and savory items to try with a good range and selection.

No problem with mobility impaired seating or access and plenty of seating available.

Name: Three Drops Coffee & Sandwich
Author: Phillip Janicki
Review: A small little cafe with minimal outdoor seating only, hence the lower score for atmosphere but the service was good and helpful. Most importantly, the food was unreal and reasonably priced. We got the bacon K-Sandwiches and they are seriously unreal. The flavours are amazing. I only wish I got double bacon to take it to the next level but that’s hindsight for you and still can’t really fault it. Definitely recommend checking this little beauty out.

Name: Three Drops Coffee & Sandwich
Author: Payel Gayen
Review:
Absolutely delightful!
This spot serves some of the best sandwiches in Melbourne and is the perfect place to satisfy your breakfast cravings.

We ordered the classic bacon sandwich, the roasted chicken sandwich, and mocha. The mocha was quite good, and the chicken sandwich was perfectly prepared and tasted fantastic. However, the true standout was the classic bacon sandwich, which was mouthwateringly delicious and filled with fluffy eggs. Simply lovely!

There are some outdoor seating options, and the service was quick and prompt. I highly recommend this place to everyone!

Name: Three Drops Coffee & Sandwich
Author: Daniel Symons
Review: Delicious Korean sandwich, excellent coffee. Lovely service. Well worth a visit if you're after something unique or just plain yummy!

Name: Three Drops Coffee & Sandwich
Author: Lollie Pop
Review: Their bulgogi sandwich is light, flavourful …. dances in your mouth type of texture and flavour. It’s not something I’ve tasted before and it’s absolutely moorish and delightful. Generous portion also. It’s more of a takeaway shop. They do have tables on the walkway next to the road and you can’t sit there when it rains. That’s the only downside. Otherwise, it’s an excellent little cafe.

Name: Three Drops Coffee & Sandwich
Author: Liam
Review:
Cool hole in the wall establishment. I had the ham and cheese toasties, which were delicious and reasonably priced. I’d recommend for anyone going through the city trying to find a bargain!

They also had a few tables on the side of the street, where I parked up for a bit. Good if you’re travelling and looking for a bit of a pit stop.

Name: Down The Rabbit Hole Cafe
Author: manooch
Review: We were here for brunch. We ordered Shrooms and Hole also we ordered a latte and capo. The coffee was good but the meals were ordinary if I look optimist. And the other thing about this place is if you want to have a quiet moment this is not the one that you are looking for at weekends. Any way could be my chance at that moment. Good staff.

Name: Down The Rabbit Hole Cafe
Author: Samantha Kaspers
Review:
Down the Rabbit Hole was fantastic! We went for the first time a couple of weeks ago and absolutely LOVED the food! From the moment we were greeted at the door by the friendly Jo, to the quick service with our meals, the whole experience was great. And the food…so good!! I want to go back just to try more things on the menu! We had the Lil bit Posh, Shrooms & a big Brekky. No one was disappointed!

I highly recommend Down the Rabbit Hole if you’re in the area.

Name: Down The Rabbit Hole Cafe
Author: Joyce K
Review: Great atmosphere, delicious food presented beautifully. Lovely staff. Gorgeous place for brunch and hanging out with friends!

Name: Down The Rabbit Hole Cafe
Author: Mina
Review:
This is a great place with a steady buzz of activity.

The atmosphere is comfortable, and the staff were attentive and on top of the service.

The coffee was spot on, and the food was excellent.

Overall, I had a wonderful experience!

Name: Down The Rabbit Hole Cafe
Author: Lau Lime
Review: My partner and I went over the weekend to down the rabbit hole. We both enjoyed a lovely seat just outside the window of the shop under the heaters due to it raining. The food was amazing and yummy. The staff were all friendly.

Name: Gellibrand Cafe
Author: Stefan Heaton
Review: Great set-up and yummy food. I tried a variety of their breakfast menu items and they were all really nice. The highlights were the Smashed Avocado and Kids Waffles. Would definitely go back here.

Name: Gellibrand Cafe
Author: Vidya Bharti
Review: Team member were welcoming, we came at 3 and generally the kitchen closed at 3 but they offered us to take our order at the very last moment, above all the food was really amazing (can’t describe in words).

Name: Gellibrand Cafe
Author: Liz L
Review: Pretty chill and family friendly cafe. The food and coffee were good and the service was friendly. It took us a while to order but it was busy however once we ordered, the food didn't take long. Overall, it was a good experience. #Reservoircafe #goodcoffeee

Name: Gellibrand Cafe
Author: Thoughtful Critique
Review:
This cafe is an absolute delight! A hidden gem that stole our hearts from the moment we walked in.

The All Board and Spanish Omelette were a taste sensation, surpassing all expectations.

What truly set this place apart was the extraordinary care and attention from the team. They went above and beyond, ensuring every moment of our brekky date was unforgettable. It wasn't just a meal; it was a culinary adventure crafted with love. Hats off to the incredible team for turning a simple breakfast into a dazzling, memorable affair!

Name: Gellibrand Cafe
Author: Tarren Anderson
Review: I took my grandmother out here today & everything about the place was fantastic! Our waitress Piper was very attentive, informative regarding the menu & super friendly. We will definitely be back!

Name: Beach Cafe Seaford
Author: bpraewest
Review:
Stopping by to have lunch at this place and meet a friend. The atmosphere is nice. There are a lot of tables you can sit indoor or outdoor to get great views of the sea.

There are a lot of menu selections. Desserts and ice cream also available.
From the restaurant you can walk down to the Seaford pier and the beach.

Name: Beach Cafe Seaford
Author: Vish SID
Review: This charming café offers an interesting and delightful breakfast menu, full of vibrant colors, rich flavors, and enticing aromas. The service is exceptional, with warm and welcoming staff that make you feel valued. Located just off the pier, the café boasts a superb location, allowing guests to sit out on the decking and enjoy a stunning view. The only downside is the lack of dedicated parking for customers, requiring you to pay for council parking, which costs about $6. Despite this, the overall experience is well worth the visit.

Name: Beach Cafe Seaford
Author: Jackki Retired from the kitchen
Review:
Heading down the boardwalk & setting my eyes upon the beautiful crystal clear water gave me holiday vibes.
Vibing the hustle and bustle of this trendy little cafe, The sound of chitter chatter & the smell of freshly brewed coffee is my kinda day.
The pastries in the cabinet were calling my name but couldn't go past the French toast.
Lovely alfresco dining facing the beach & outdoor dining in front.
I must recommend booking before dining as to not be disappointed.
Everything was delicious.

Name: Beach Cafe Seaford
Author: Ghazal Arash Rad
Review: Atmosphere was great, especially where we sat on balcony with the bay view, menue choices were not too varient for brunch and prices maybe a bit more than other places but we had a great time in a sunny day with friends there.

Name: Beach Cafe Seaford
Author: Lisa Sutherland-Fraser
Review:
What a hidden gem of a place. Right on Seaford Pier but hidden behind the changing rooms.

Outdoor seating with dogs allowed. Glad they had lovely warm blankies to use on the day we went as it was overcast and cool.

Lovely warm attentive service 5/5!

Menu had so much inviting food it was hard to decide but the lentil and veggie stew was perfect. Warm, luscious and filling. Pieces of warmed & oiled pita with a spicy topping were just what my cold body needed. 5/5

Almond mocha was ok but then I’m extremely picky. Cannot complain one but really.

Will be back lovely people! 😎

Name: All Things Equal Cafe
Author: Deborah Travers
Review: How exceptionally wonderful is this cafe. The team that served were patient, kind and friendly. Our food was served quickly and was really delicious. I ate granola with poached pears and coconut yoghurt while my friends enjoyed mushrooms on toast and shakahuka. The chai on oat milk was really hot and tasty. All the food was fresh and without sugar! Yay!

Name: All Things Equal Cafe
Author: Yosy Christy Natalia
Review: Very nice cafe with a beautiful concept itself. The coffee is really good but the salmon bagel that I ordered wasn't that good, it was too much cream cheese and too sour. I definitely come here again to try another ones

Name: All Things Equal Cafe
Author: S F
Review: Great service, quick and the coffee was above par (I drink long black - was delicious and not bitter!) Also I could hear every word my friend was saying - refreshing!

Name: All Things Equal Cafe
Author: Carmel Gerdsen
Review:
Just enjoyed the avacado on toast with and added poached egg.
I really enjoyed the crispy radish that was on top of the avacado.
Not what I was expecting, but it was delicious.
And I always enjoy their chai latte and another favourite in the coconut cake - always yum!

Name: All Things Equal Cafe
Author: Andres Trujillo (Consciousness_888)
Review: I have been living in Melbourne for the past 6 years, and today I experienced discrimination for the first time. I went for brunch with my friends, and when we arrived at the restaurant, we were treated with contempt. However, the group behind us was clearly given preferential treatment, which is not how I have been treated at other places I've visited here. Based on my personal experience, I would not recommend this place if you are not a local in the area.

Name: Golden Child Cafe
Author: art3mis
Review:
My boyfriend took me to this place for breakfast twice and the food is incredible!!!
I’ve never had a chilli scrambled eggs so creamy and yummy! And the pulled pork Benedict is delicious !!
We’ve gone around 12pm-12:30pm and it tends to be quite busy. We’ve been lucky to eat breakfast outside both times we’ve visited, but it’s good to book a table before hand to make sure you get a spot.
Will definitely go again for brunch 😋

Name: Golden Child Cafe
Author: Emily May
Review: Three of us came to Golden Child Cafe on a Saturday morning for brunch. I ordered a ham cheese tomato croissant and a chai latte. The food was pretty tasty and the service was very good, but just be aware of the weekend surcharge. Nevertheless, we enjoyed our time at this cafe as it has a very warm feeling when you step inside. Thank you :)

Name: Golden Child Cafe
Author: Betty Damianidis
Review: The dishes here look like a work of art, and they are packed with delicious flavours. Service was quick. Easy parking.

Name: Golden Child Cafe
Author: Vahid Shahani
Review:
Beautiful family owned business. They were very friendly and welcoming. I loved their Salmon Bagels and all type of coffees. I had Almond latte in the early morning and Soy latte with my breakfast. However, I think they could make a bit better with Atlantic Salmon Benedict as you could smell of the non pleasant fish from the food (need some herbs). Also, the menu was not really clear about some items which we asked the staff and smash Avocado was not really smashed.
Finally, we enjoyed our breakfast at this place.

Thank you 😊

Name: Golden Child Cafe
Author: Vincent Wang
Review:
The food served were great, but quite unimpressionable to be fair. They serve Ona specialty coffee here but my skinny latte wasn’t particularly fragrant; perhaps under-extracted the shot.

There’s nothing wrong with the smashed pea and halloumi, nor with the pulled pork Benedict. In fact they were good, but for the price and 10% weekend surcharge, I found myself wanting more for the portion and taste.

Name: Brown Sugar Cafe
Author: Eddie Khays
Review:
Nestled in the heart of Melbourne, Brown Sugar Cafe is a true hidden gem that combines a cozy atmosphere with outstanding food and impeccable service. From the moment you step through the doors, you're greeted with a warm, inviting ambiance that feels like a home away from home. The rustic decor, with its charming wooden tables and ambient lighting, sets the stage for a relaxed dining experience.

The coffee at Brown Sugar Cafe is nothing short of exceptional.  The baristas here clearly know their craft, and the result is a consistently excellent brew that keeps patrons coming back for more.

But what truly sets Brown Sugar Cafe apart is the service. The staff (Alba and Rin) are friendly, attentive, and genuinely care about ensuring a pleasant dining experience for every guest. Their warm hospitality makes you feel like a valued friend rather than just another customer.

In summary, Brown Sugar Cafe is a must-visit for anyone in Melbourne looking to enjoy great food, excellent coffee, and top-notch service in a welcoming environment. Whether you're a local or just passing through, this cafe will quickly become a favorite spot.

Name: Brown Sugar Cafe
Author: sina najarha
Review: Brown Sugar Cafe offers a delightful experience across the board. The atmosphere is warm and inviting, perfect for a casual meal or a cozy catch-up. The service is attentive and friendly, making you feel right at home. As for the food, it's absolutely delicious! Each dish bursts with flavor and is prepared with care. The portion sizes are generous, ensuring you leave satisfied without breaking the bank. Overall, Brown Sugar Cafe strikes a perfect balance of quality, quantity, and affordability, making it a must-visit for anyone craving a memorable dining experience.

Name: Brown Sugar Cafe
Author: Travel Gen
Review: Brown Sugar Cafe in the Block Arcade offers a delightful coffee experience in beautifully renovated premises. The ambience combines modern comfort with the historic charm of the arcade, making it a perfect spot to relax. The coffee was excellent, brewed to perfection, and the service was warm and welcoming. Whether you're looking for a cozy place to enjoy your coffee or just passing through the arcade, Brown Sugar Cafe is well worth a visit.

Name: Brown Sugar Cafe
Author: Pia Linn Theissing
Review:
Lovely cafe in Melbourne, definitely worth a try!
It’s located in a cute laneway at the Block Arcade right in Cbd. The staff is super friendly, amazing coffee and cozy atmosphere.
I tried the Risotto of the day and it’s one of the best vegetarian risottos I’ve ever had!!
Don’t miss out on that!

Name: Brown Sugar Cafe
Author: Talei K
Review: The baked polenta and mushroom dish was lovely- polenta was creamy and smooth with a crunchy edge. There was a mountain of kale...I like kale but couldn't get through half of it. Hubby enjoyed the French toast, not so much the coffee. The coffee was not to my liking at all, but exchanged for tea that was fine. When paying the bill realised they charge the tax on top, so another $7. A pleasant cafe

Name: Krimper Cafe
Author: Patrick Yap
Review: Found our way to this little gem in between the small streets of Melbourne.  It was a lovely surprise and the entrance to this cafe was beautifully decorated.  It already gave you the good vibes before entering the cafe.  Inside, it was warm and beautiful.  The food is also excellent and the staff was friendly.  I would give it a 4 thumbs up!  Worth the visit.

Name: Krimper Cafe
Author: Jay W
Review:
A well established cafe in a beautiful hidden alley way.

The flat white and long black tasted great.

The atmosphere is a highlight of this establishment. Despite being cold outside, it was cozy, low light ambience and beautiful decor inside.

The serving staff were stressed and looked overworked due to the volume of customers coming in. More front staff would benefit this place, as it took a while to clean tables despite people waiting and also once seated it look a while for staff to take orders.

Name: Krimper Cafe
Author: Hanny Pan
Review: I've been there many times!! Whenever my friends ask me to recommend a cafe, I always think of this place first!! It's hidden behind a wooden door, and when you open the door, it's a factory style, very spacious, very beautiful, and a very unique decoration atmosphere!! The key is that the food is really good, and my favorite two dishes are in those picture that I post!! Every time I come, I will order a cup of iced long black, which is stronger and more sour than other coffees!!

Name: Krimper Cafe
Author: Yaz Oz
Review:
Enjoyed breakfast here complimented by very friendly service. Service was delivered quickly with a smile.
Food was delicious and coffee was good. All very reasonably priced. Surprised to get 2 hash browns with extra order.  Would be happy to return again and again.

Name: Krimper Cafe
Author: Heather Cusdin
Review: We had a lovely brunch @ Krimper Cafe..this is a very popular cafe on weekends, so expect to wait, but……  worth the wait! Delicious food, great coffee and attentive service… the building is beautifully renovated and the walk through Guilford  laneway to reach this cafe, is amazing… great part of Melbourne to visit 😊

Name: Long Street Coffee
Author: Dianna Goodman
Review: 5 star coffee @ Long St! I asked Google for 'best coffee near me ' and it didn't disappoint. Thanks Google + thanks Long St cafe 😊

Name: Long Street Coffee
Author: M VDM
Review: This is a lovely little local. The two running it do an amazing job, with such warmth and energy. No wonder its a fave with the regulars. The Spanish omelette is my absolute favourite. Smokey, cheesy, delicious! The basketball hoop outside is great to have while waiting. Love the plants too!

Name: Long Street Coffee
Author: Ricky
Review: Nice little cafe off Hoddle St. Friendly staff and good coffee. Just need to be aware finding a parking space might not be easy.

Name: Long Street Coffee
Author: Michelle Davidson
Review: Just here for coffee and it’s really good! Visited each day I’ve stayed in the city for work.

Name: Long Street Coffee
Author: Vijay Viswanathan
Review:
Banging Music, Exceptional Service, and pipping hot Chai rivaling the best, drank streetside in India.

Exceptional Coffees and Brunch plates too.

Beautiful Melbourne Cafe inconspicuously hiding in a grafitti-filled laneway beside North Richmond Station

Name: Cafe Rubix
Author: D C
Review: New to the area this quickly became our weekly brunch destination. Friendly staff, great food and a rotating specials menu which keeps things interesting.

Name: Cafe Rubix
Author: Joel D
Review: Coffee is smooth as with friendly service and a great local place. keep up the good work. Loved the eggs benny. Thanks

Name: Cafe Rubix
Author: Bo Cui
Review: Met a friend here for a cup of coffee and ordered my favourite latte and tasted smooth, rich and well-balanced. I’d give their coffee for a 4.8/5. Service is attentive and welcoming. Didn’t try their food, maybe next time? 😊my personal rating for this place is 4.8/5

Name: Cafe Rubix
Author: Manu Mathews
Review:
Latte was a bit too watery..
Scrambled eggs were cooked to perfection..
Pancakes were good

Name: Cafe Rubix
Author: Jess Parisi
Review:
Fantastic 10/10 Cafe not only the food was excellent but so was the overall atmosphere.
Service was also exceptional, all staff were very friendly and attentive. Especially Finn he gave us excellent service and a good chat. Highly recommend, will definitely be coming back.

Name: Bezirk Cafe
Author: E
Review:
My winning streak is over!

The location is beautiful, right on the corner of Altona Beach, cramped between homes.
However, the food subpar at best.
I ordered the chilli scrambled eggs based off the reviews I have read myself , I only saw good things until it was presented in front of me.
At first glance, it’s presented beautifully but the sourdough was wholegrain and nothing on the menu stated I was getting wholegrain sour dough so that ruined everything! The eggs were absolutely flavourless and stringy. From past experience, scrambled eggs are fluffy and creamy, delicate on the tongue but these were like chewy stringy chicken to describe them.

My partner ordered the burger to which was not even served with a side, it didn’t say there was but to be given a small fist sized burger for their price point that had a sweet bun and tasted like “American food”, I expected more!

Such a disappointment especially with the good reviews from others!

If anything it has the atmosphere of casual boring dining. Amazing staff!
At least the beach is there!

Name: Bezirk Cafe
Author: Wolly Bumpernickle
Review: How hard is it to deliver a full cup!  I paid for a large 😳 Half the cup was empty!!!  I took the life off and this is what I saw.  If you want a full cup I advise you not to get a drink from them and this is not the first time

Name: Bezirk Cafe
Author: Sherilyn Harris
Review:
A lovely cafe almost beachside at Altona at end of Millers Rd. Had the Monk bowl with grilled chicken and a green tea. Delicious nutrient dense meal! Attentive service from all the staff. Sitting in the sun today was a delight. ThankYou!

Update August 2024. Back again 4 months later. It’s now a superfood green bowl poached egg (I had mine with grilled chicken). Another delicious bowl of goodness. ThankYou!

Name: Bezirk Cafe
Author: Abhinav Bidani
Review:
Outstanding food and outstanding service.

6 of us turned up and the waiter took his time to organise the tables outside and quickly sorted us our order and made some recommendations. Despite being flat out, we appreciated his patience with us.

We order led the Cubano Benedict, haloumi toastie, fried chicken burger, chilli eggs - it was all first class and we all were very satisfied with our order. We also got a range of coffees which were delicious.

Name: Bezirk Cafe
Author: Maria Diana Syrakis
Review: Stopped in a few weeks ago for brunch with my family. We sat outside in the sun and enjoyed a beautiful meal and coffee. The service was really quick and friendly. Our boys really enjoyed their toasties. Such a nice place tucked away in Altona away from the main strip. Parking was easy on the street in front of the cafe. We will definitely be back again.

Name: Fifteen Pounds Cafe
Author: Prajwal pahari
Review: I recently visited this cafe and I must say, it was absolutely fantastic! The atmosphere was cozy and welcoming, and the staff were incredibly friendly. I loved the spacious sitting area and the beautiful backyard. The food was delicious and the portion sizes were just perfect. I highly recommend checking out this cafe!" 🌟👍

Name: Fifteen Pounds Cafe
Author: Cathy N
Review: Good ambience great location, portion size was generous. Coffee was even better.

Name: Fifteen Pounds Cafe
Author: Goli
Review:
The coffee was truly exceptional, boasting a delightful blend of flavors and a stunning display of latte art. Our choice of avocado toast with poached eggs was delicious. The service was efficient.
The interior was really noisy. We look forward to returning on a warmer day to enjoy the more quiet ambiance of the outdoor seating area.

Name: Fifteen Pounds Cafe
Author: RJ Houston
Review:
I have been going here every weekend for 3 years because the brunch menu was awesome. The staff are great. The venue is great.

They recently changed their menu and dropped their great breakfasts. While I am sure there are very real cost-of living and business reasons for leaning things down, this was an abrupt change, and still super disappointing. I noted the usual busy and full restaurant was almost empty this morning when I visited… not sure the full cost-benefit was considered, particularly as our party or 6 today went down the road and spent $250.

Why would you make such an abrupt decision like this without consulting your loyal customers?

Name: Fifteen Pounds Cafe
Author: Donelle Crocker
Review: I love it here! All the yummy vegan and gluten free treats, beautiful coffee and friendly faces! The best

Name: Mr Grumpy's Cafe
Author: PJ
Review: 10/10 will and would recommend. The atmosphere was fantastic, great music, calm environment. Staff were incredibly friendly and helpful when I asked about what they recommended and dietary options. The food came out fast and was incredible!! And so was the coffee! Mr grumpy’s cafe - I walked in grumpy and came out full of amazingly tasting, great ingredient food, significantly happier than when I walked in!! I love the art here as well, so interesting to look at! And the music is great! The cheesy omelet was really one of the best omelettes I’ve ever had in my life and I’m not stranger to omelettes!! So cheesy and so fluffy and incredibly tasty. I practically licked my plate clean 😂 now I’m gonna go on with my day with a pep in my step! It’ll be hard not to come here every day now 😂

Name: Mr Grumpy's Cafe
Author: Riley Jo
Review:
The food and coffee are so nice! I understand why their rating is so good! The staff are all friendly and attentive!
There was road work in front of cafe so the mood was a bit distracted when I visited and the interior was filled with all artificial plants(if you want to go to a pretty and photogenic cafe) But the food and coffee are nice! Recommend to try!

Name: Mr Grumpy's Cafe
Author: Matthew Ainge
Review:
Came in here after a few recommendations and I have to say. What a place, it may not look too much from the street inside you are greeted with great customer service.

Cappuccino: not too strong and the milk wasn't scorched, overall a smooth and pleasant drink.

Breakfast Burrito: What a way to start the day! A combination of eggs, bacon, spinach and bit of mushrooms coupled with a sweet sauce to counter the saltiness of the bacon. The humus type mixture lining the bowl added a great pop of sesame.

Would I go back? Yes!

Name: Mr Grumpy's Cafe
Author: Nuwanga Mahadiulwewa
Review: "Mr Grumpy's Cafe: A quirky & charming Springvale spot serving up delicious brekky & brunch options. Friendly service, cozy atmosphere & a dash of gruff humor make for a memorable experience. Great coffee, tasty eats & a fun vibe - even Mr Grumpy can't hide a smile!"

Name: Mr Grumpy's Cafe
Author: Tharindya Illangathilake
Review:
I recently visited Mr. Grumpy’s Cafe and was thoroughly impressed with the entire experience. The food is exceptional and truly worth every penny. I ordered the granola bowl, and it was, without a doubt, the best I’ve ever had—rich, filling, and absolutely delicious. The turmeric juice was another standout; its unique flavor and freshness are unmatched by any other juice I’ve tried.

The service was outstanding as well. The staff were incredibly friendly, professional, and welcoming, and the speed of service was remarkable. Every aspect of my visit, from the quality of the food to the attentive service, was top-notch. Mr. Grumpy’s Cafe has certainly earned a spot on my list of must-visit places. I highly recommend it for anyone looking for a fantastic meal and a great overall dining experience.

Name: Aunt Billies Cafe
Author: Prem Gautham
Review: An awesome cafe for food and drinks. We randomly selected this cafe for lunch and we were not disappointed. Their service was excellent with all the staff being very nice and were taking orders in spite of being very busy. We loved all the dishes we ordered and the outstanding was the French toast. The kids chicken fingers with chips had only two pieces of chicken which we found to be very less. Otherwise the taste was awesome. We would definitely visit this cafe again.

Name: Aunt Billies Cafe
Author: Sophia
Review: This is my second visit to this cafe, and the experience was just as delightful as the first. The staff are consistently friendly, and the owner was incredibly patient in answering our questions when we were ordering. The seafood risotto and prawn tacos were absolutely delicious, and the lamb shoulder was generously portioned. The fact that there's no surcharge on weekends was a pleasant surprise. The ambiance of the cafe is wonderful, making it a great place to enjoy a meal. Thanks for making my brunch so enjoyable :)

Name: Aunt Billies Cafe
Author: Eugenia Ngai
Review:
Loved it!!!!!
One of the best seafood risotto I’ve ever had in my life!!
It’s very flavourful, filling and very very delicious.
We also had the chicken salad bowl. I was so pleased that the chicken breast was tender and juicy !!!
We also had a side of chips. It’s very big in portion.

I’d recommend this to a friend 100%. It’s so amazing. defo would come back again.

Name: Aunt Billies Cafe
Author: art3mis
Review:
Me and my boyfriend went to this place on Sunday morning. We got there at around 11am, and it wasn’t too busy so getting a table wasn’t an issue. We sat outside in a covered area and it was quite nice.
I got the Beef Ribs Benedict and my boyfriend got the Billy Burger.
My dish was absolutely delicious (I’ve gotten it before so I knew it was good haha) and my boyfriend’s was good. The burger patty was a bit overcooked, and there weren’t any onion rings even when the menu said there was. But it wasn’t horrible, so we didn’t raise the issue to the waitressses.
Other than that, it was quite a lovely brunch.

Name: Aunt Billies Cafe
Author: Billy Li
Review: A great local Cafe in the heart of Blackburn! All day breakfast and lunch menu available. Pet owner can bring their little one to enjoy the sun in the outdoor area. Beside your normal coffee, tea and juice they've also got some interesting smoothies. I've ordered one with coconut juice and was very refreshing. Weekend can get very busy as it's right next to the church. Highly recommended to come and try for yourself!

Name: Rathdowne and Reeves Cafe
Author: Nicholas Cahyono
Review: A great place to enjoy some breakfast or afternoon tea! Friendly and quick service along with a welcoming staff. The food in itself is superb, the Belgian Waffles especially is amazing! The owners themselves are incredible as well! Both lovely and awesome! This cafe, I must highly recommend!

Name: Rathdowne and Reeves Cafe
Author: A. Doushenka
Review: Been coming here for ages. Service is top notch and the food is consistently delicious and beautifully presented. Coffee is as good as it gets. For years I didn’t want to give this review as I don’t like my favourite places becoming too popular, but I keep getting told I’m being silly 😊. Thanks Trina and Damian for the many yummy meals!

Name: Rathdowne and Reeves Cafe
Author: Isabella Nightshade
Review: The food is heavenly, the atmosphere is beautiful and the staff are top notch! It feels like outside in the building! They've got great chef's specials to keep things interesting as well. And the coffee, leave the best for last. Well rounded and rich with just the right acidity levels. The presentation of everything really puts the cherry on top. Highly recommend!

Name: Rathdowne and Reeves Cafe
Author: Nancy Varkey
Review:
My first taste of food in Melbourne. The most perfect poached eggs I have ever eaten in my life.  🌟
Super tasty and super awesome plating👌👌 Love, love, love this little cafe in Carlton and will highly recommend it.  Menu is stupendous. Perfect dishes - all made from scratch and are all super delicious.  Serving portion is quite generous and filling. Prices are quite reasonable.
Very, very clean. Beautiful interiors.  Spaced-out seating. Clean washroom. The owners & the staff are so friendly and kind and make you feel relaxed and welcome instantly.
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Name: Rathdowne and Reeves Cafe
Author: Ann Cox (Annie)
Review: Fabulous cafe with indoor / outdoor garden.  Beautiful food 😋, friendly staff and EXCELLENT ACCESSABILTY.  Wheelchair friendly ♿️ ✅️  parking - sometimes have to drive around the block - nice garden walk at the back of building.

Name: Twenty & Six Espresso
Author: Kiah W
Review: I don’t think I’ve ever taken pictures of food before this moment. 10/10 fish tacos and 10/10 limited iced strawberry matcha latte. Also fam’s latte art was exceptional

Name: Twenty & Six Espresso
Author: Hiromi
Review: Pulled pork benny and latte were delicious. Cute latte art made me happy.  Cozy and nice atmosphere. Friendly staff. Highly recommended.

Name: Twenty & Six Espresso
Author: Hanmo Li
Review:
Brunch at @twentyandsix from a few months ago: good brunch staples in a nice and cosy shop!
.
Zucchini and corn fritters with bacon: yummy fritters!.
Smashed avocado with poached egg
Matcha latte

Name: Twenty & Six Espresso
Author: Habibah Ghazali
Review: Love this cafe. The coffee always good and not overpriced. The food that we have tried are all yummy! The owner and his staff are very nice and friendly too! The best coffee in North Melbourne.

Name: Twenty & Six Espresso
Author: Olga Balaganskaya
Review:
It's one of my favourite cafes. I love this place for their delicious and healthy dishes and a nice, quiet atmosphere.
Place features:
- Food and drinks.
- Breakfasts and lunches.
- Original serving.
- Moderate prices.
- Dogs friendly.
- Indoor and outdoor areas.
- Friendly stuff.

Name: Anda cafe
Author: May Yap
Review: Highly recommend the Philly Cheesesteak and Chai latte here. It is my usual order! I tried their new tomato soup and cheese toastie recently and can see why it’s so popular. Service is always friendly and helpful.

Name: Anda cafe
Author: Lilgem
Review: Coffee is excellent, food amazing! Takeaway window is quite busy, a testament that it’s a favourite for the Melburnians early morning coffee fix. Will definitely be back.

Name: Anda cafe
Author: Maryanne Young
Review:
The best egg and bacon muffin I've ever had,  the special sauce is special! I added my hashbrown into the muffin after I took the photo.

Mum said her toasted ham and cheese croissant was very nice.

Both of our coffees were pretty good

The 4 for atmosphere was just because it was super noisy when we were there

Name: Anda cafe
Author: Cher
Review:
Place gets really crowded but managed to get a table for breakfast.

Tried their chai latte & sausage and egg muffin.

And i must say i didn’t expect much as I was thinking of the one from Mcd, but wow the sausage and egg muffin is AMAZING. the sauce was yummy and the sausage was so juicy and tasty, while the egg was cooked with the yolk being abit raw so it was runny (love runny yolks).

Chai Latte was also really enjoyable, they put alot of spices and the milk and spices balanced really well.

Would recommend!

Name: Anda cafe
Author: Natalie Thaivaranone
Review: I tried sausage and egg muffin as I read through all the reviews here, turned out as great as everyone had mentioned. To my experience, hash brown was sooooooooooo good that I regretted ordered only one! (as I took a delivery). Vanilla bean iced latte was impressive! (I added extra shot of coffee, perfect to my liking! not too strong, not too sweet, love it. 😊👍🏻🙏🏻

Name: Cafe Claremont
Author: Britney Pham
Review:
Delicious brunch here, conveniently next to the station but just around the corner to escape the noise. The staff were lovely (albeit quite busy) and the special was advertised to me, which was worth every penny. The space was a bit tight so is more suited to individuals or smaller groups, but very much worth a stop if you are looking for a simple Melb suburban breakky.

Ordered: chilli scrambled eggs on croissant + flat white

Name: Cafe Claremont
Author: Liza Bun
Review: A very cosy local cafe shop located next to the train station! Very affordable! Great to go for a quick lunch and coffee! The staffs are all very friendly and welcoming. Not too crowded during the lunch time so feel free to check it out!

Name: Cafe Claremont
Author: JEFFREY D
Review:
A wonderful boys lunch on a sunny Saturday. We sat inside after being greeted warmly by Gabby one of the waitresses and a senior staff member. We ordered two wonderful Chai lattes and a coffee. For lunch, I had a salmon pokey bowl, and it was delicious with soba noodles and vegetables, my two mates had green eggs ( omelette ) and scrambled eggs with mushrooms and crispy bacon. Yes .. awesome service, huge menu choices and also light options as well  such as focaccias pies and toasties.
Thanks
We shall be back!

Name: Cafe Claremont
Author: Hormesis
Review:
Great food, warm welcome, friendly staff and nice owner. We have tested the tasty asian salad from the special menu and the superfood salad with pulled lamb. The last one has a lack of seasoning, but the dishes were good, with a good mix of ingredients and well presented.
Menu attached.

Name: Cafe Claremont
Author: Michael Hurley
Review: We moved into Claremont Avenue over 20 years ago, and the vendor of our house recommended that we visit Cafe Claremont. We have been made to feel very welcome by the staff, and have been extremely well fed and watered ever since. All ages are catered for. It’s a home away from home!

Name: Little Rogue.
Author: reham abdullah
Review: Little Rouge Café offers a cozy atmosphere with friendly service, perfect for a casual coffee break. I enjoyed their chocolate banana bread, which was moist, rich, and had the perfect balance of sweetness. Paired with a smooth flat white, it made for a delightful treat. The café is a great spot for a quick snack or a relaxed coffee moment. Would definitely recommend for anyone looking for a little comfort food and great coffee!

Name: Little Rogue.
Author: A S
Review:
I find myself returning to this cafe, day after day, time after time.
The staff are so friendly and take time, when asking about the coffee.
The place is usually full and yet I always find a place to sit and read or write and look outside to the spectacle of life.
Young and old, walking along the small alley this cafe is located in.
Dogs waiting for their owners and dog owners waiting for their matcha latte: modern poetry.

Name: Little Rogue.
Author: Tan P
Review:
Hidding in small corner that we nearly miss it. I believe if you really want to find, you will with your ❤️.

Love their matcha and flatwhite.

Food is good too. We had ham & cheese toasted croissant & matcha cheese cake. Yummy!

Lovely & friendly vibes tho limited sitting area.

Overall experience was good.

Name: Little Rogue.
Author: Hermione Le
Review: Small but really nice coffee and a small place to chill for working and reading. Limited seats so should only sit for short time. Very busy coffee shop. Their matcha latte and sakuratte were amazing. This shop and the opposite bakery Bakemon belong to the same company.

Name: Little Rogue.
Author: Dita W
Review: The BEST place for matcha latte! Always loved the atmosphere, and they even sell their own matcha powder to take home. Plus, the bakery right across is a fantastic bonus. Only few seats available which is not really an issue as we normally do takeaway.

Name: Koko Lime Cafe
Author: Posy Rose
Review: Fantastic service  and delicious drinks.  Lovely food. We had a great time. Thanks

Name: Koko Lime Cafe
Author: Oliver
Review: Really good food and coffee! Hidden gem on the foot of Dandenong ranges. The price to quality ratio is great. Service is quick and friendly. The choices and dishes have a great variety, not a mundane menu. Must try!!

Name: Koko Lime Cafe
Author: MissBollywood4eva
