Review: Went here for brunch & tried multiple options which were all delicious. Had the Chicken, Avocado, Spinach & Aioli Focaccia which was cripsy & full of fresh ingredients. The Koko Omelette was also flavoursome & satisfying. The butter, cheese & coleslaw with Hot Potatoes was also nice with the butter melting in your mouth. The Special Winter Baked Eggs were the highlight, baked in a pan with a tomato, potato & feta gravy was full of tangy flavour bursting with a variety kf spices (not spicy). It also comes with crispy serving of bread on side to dip into the gravy to enjoy. The Spanish Omelette with chorizo was also good & perfectly balanced with the variety of ingredients used. The cappuccino was also good, not too strong, not too light. Coupled with the quick & friendly service, it hits all the right boxes. Highly recommend this cafe, seems to be a popular spot for local brunches.

Name: Koko Lime Cafe
Author: Kristdel Bolog
Review:
We had a team lunch here on the fly and weren't disappointed. The food was really yummy and homely and came out quickly. Price was reasonable and staff were friendly.

The portions were really large and the burger and omelette both came with chips which were flavourful and crispy.

Name: Koko Lime Cafe
Author: Louis Lau
Review: Good cosy little cafe in the neighborhood. Serving great variety of food and beverages. Generous portion, good price and friendly services.

Name: Bellboy Cafe | Brunswick Cafe
Author: Sherelle Shea
Review: Best bagels of my life.  So much flavor.  Bagels were softer than I am used to and so lovely to eat. Coffee was fab too.

Name: Bellboy Cafe | Brunswick Cafe
Author: Sophia Sakellaropoulos
Review:
What a Sunday delight!!

We ordered the crab roll and the fritters with the side of halloumi  everything was really tasty. Coffee was great too.

Such a nice space especially in the sun, we will definitely go back.

Name: Bellboy Cafe | Brunswick Cafe
Author: Cait Y
Review:
Bellboy Cafe offers a delightful dining experience with its flavourful Chermoula Chicken and freshly roasted coffee beans. The cafe's aesthetic charm, featuring high ceilings, ample natural lighting, and cozy booths, creates an inviting atmosphere. The food surprises with a perfect balance of flavours—sweet, sour and salty—exceeding expectations.

Moreover, what truly sets Bellboy apart is its exceptional customer service, embodied by Emilio, the host. Emilio's genuine warmth and attentiveness, coupled with insightful recommendations about Melbourne, enhanced the overall experience.

Additionally, Bellboy is pet-friendly, providing a little kennel and dog bowls for furry companions to rest comfortably. Whether you're craving great food or exceptional hospitality, Bellboy comes highly recommended.

Name: Bellboy Cafe | Brunswick Cafe
Author: Olivia Cowan
Review: Very friendly service. Food was top-tier, so fresh, the bagel options are so creative and burst with flavour. Great coffee! (Would have loved a lactose free milk option though!)

Name: Bellboy Cafe | Brunswick Cafe
Author: Vincent Dang
Review: Stopped here for quick brunch. Surprisingly, food was delicious and cheap. Coffee was decent. Very friendly and prompt service, nice atmosphere. I will definitely come back.

Name: Quarter Two Cafe
Author: Penelope Stark-Wolff
Review: What a great new little place! It was quite busy and the owner was just lovely with ensuring we could get a table, along with the others waiting. Coffee was great, the breakfast is affordable and in my opinion a perfect portion size. Friendly staff who were prompt and attentive. I also bought 2 boxes of pastry’s because they were staring at me and they were an absolute delight, particularly the custard filled Italian sweet and carrot cake, YUM! Well done to the owners and staff, I’ll definitely return.

Name: Quarter Two Cafe
Author: Lucy Vassallo
Review: Great vibe at this place. Excellent service and generous food portions. We had the Ricotta. hot cake and Mortedella Benedict. Fresh and yummy. Price point is pretty good too! Busy but not too busy to provide you with a smile and great service!!

Name: Quarter Two Cafe
Author: Aryan Sinanan
Review: Having been through a number of menu items over a few visits, I can say with confidence this spot is worth your time. The staff are super friendly, there are some great breakfast options (my preference remains the chilli scrambled eggs) and the coffee is excellent. A few extra points for opening at 5.3a, makes it super easy to get in early and have a quiet breakfast and a coffee before you start your day.

Name: Quarter Two Cafe
Author: Melissa Pagano
Review: Ive been coming here weekly since it’s opened because of the beautiful service and iced lattes. My young daughter also loves coming here as the staff always engage with her and serve her delicious babyccino’s. The food is on point and can’t wait to try more dishes on the menu!

Name: Quarter Two Cafe
Author: Bruce Foot
Review: We drive from Footscray for the coffee. Great cafe in an old church. Good range of light food/panini’s and toasties. We shared the steak roll which was substantial and cooked to medium rare, with a huge serving of fries. Very good

Name: Parkers Cafe
Author: Vicky K
Review: The coffee is amazing. Its spot on every time i order. The staff are always friendly and welcoming. My absolute favourite is the super moist carrot cake they usually have at the counter. Its insanely yummy. Highly recommend Parkers Cafe. Its my go to place for coffee any day of the week.

Name: Parkers Cafe
Author: Dudu Gee
Review:
This store is racist. Or can you explain this?
My colleague told me that the matcha at the corner is good. I went to the wrong Parkers Cafe and asked can Ihave two cups of matcha. Before I finished speaking, she turned her head away and told me with a red face that they only had one cup left. But I saw whole box of matcha powder. Then the matcha looked like milk.

Name: Parkers Cafe
Author: Adam Brown
Review:
Really lovely, unassuming cafe. Prompt and friendly service. Seating outside was great on a nice day, under cover and spaced fairly generously.

Coffee was delicious if slightly on the smaller side. All day breakfast and plenty of nice sounding lunch options. Food came out quite quickly and was well presented. Good mix of flavours and generally balanced.

Can't fault this place. Looking forward to going back again. Very dog friendly too.

Name: Parkers Cafe
Author: David Creedon
Review: I waited so long (Friday 14/6/24, 9.45am) for a bowl of oats w dried fruit that I asked waitress (only after customers who’d arrived later had already been served food) if they were still growing the oats! Staff obviously lacked interest/training/both. Very expensive too, though quite nice, admittedly. Only 2 stars overall, primarily cos the company was good 🥴

Name: Parkers Cafe
Author: Caoimhin O'Feinneadha
Review:
Very slow service, and then some of our order never arrived. No apology from the wait staff when we mentioned it.
Witnessed an Uber driver being told he has a15 minute wait, at which point he bolted.
We had stopped coming for the same reason, but decided to give it another go. Same same.
If the kitchen can’t service that many tables, don’t have them.
The 10% Sunday surcharge was an unpleasant surprise, and as is does not appear to be displayed anywhere, never mind displayed as prominently as the prices themselves, it is illegal.

Name: House Blend
Author: Angus Adams
Review:
House Blend on King Street is a hidden gem in Melbourne!
Jared, the owner, is an absolute legend—always welcoming and great to chat with. The atmosphere is cozy and relaxed, perfect for catching up with friends or getting some work done. The coffee is consistently top-notch, and the food never disappoints. Whether you’re a regular or popping in for the first time, House Blend makes you feel like part of the community. Highly recommend giving it a visit!

Name: House Blend
Author: Ruby Zhang
Review: The coffee, food and service here is great!! Glad that it's 4min walk away from where we were staying:)

Name: House Blend
Author: Karen Kwok
Review:
I owe you a review boss 🫡

Been a regular here since I landed in Melb a few months ago. The coffee to go is great here and the pastries are on point too. Really appreciate the warm hospitality and hope to be back soon!

Name: House Blend
Author: Matias Alvarez
Review: Amazing place! Excellent coffee and sandwiches as well as the chocolate croissant. The owner was super friendly and really take care of the costumers 🙌🏼

Name: House Blend
Author: Therese Ang
Review: Vibe was warm and friendly thanks to the owner and staff; great latte and pastries!

Name: For Change Cafe, Brunswick
Author: Joanne Collier
Review: We visited the Brunswick store whilst over from New Zealand,  by far the best coffee we had when visiting,  the Rhubarb Danish was also amazing. The staff are so friendly and knowledgeable of the area. We can't wait to visit again and try one of the delicious looking toastie sammies they sold. Highly recommend this cafe when in Brunswick, right by the train station. Dog friendly as well.

Name: For Change Cafe, Brunswick
Author: Alannah Andrews
Review: I come here almost every weekday on my way to work. There are plenty more places about - but none compare. Marmaduke and Paige (pseudonyms but you’ll work it out) are just delightful, and their coffee always spot on.

Name: For Change Cafe, Brunswick
Author: Jesse Stevens
Review:
I eat here all the time. Their toasties are next level and they're so lovely also.

Highly recommend the tofu toastie with jalapenos. Super crispy and tasty every single time with the the coleslaw on there too.

Always greeted with a smile and I've noticed the whole neighbourhood pops in almost daily.

Grab a coffee and food here and sit in the park nearby for an excellent lunch

Name: For Change Cafe, Brunswick
Author: Courtney Gregg
Review: Love this place! Serving up more than just great coffee—it brews up hope and positive change in the community. For Change Cafe: where the coffee’s hot, but the trainers? Even hotter, thanks to the incredible job they do. The coffee’s strong, the cause is stronger, and if you’re not sipping here, what are you even doing in Brunswick?

Name: For Change Cafe, Brunswick
Author: John S
Review: Fantastic little coffee shop. The staff are super friendly, the coffee is excellent and the food tasty. My dogs love visiting the cafe where they get home made treats and lots of attention.

Name: Otherside Coffee
Author: Scarlett H
Review: A cozy gem with a welcoming vibe! The bright smiley coffee cups add a cheerful touch, perfectly matching the rustic, relaxing atmosphere. Great spot to unwind with a delicious brew surrounded by thoughtful details like potted plants and exposed brick.

Name: Otherside Coffee
Author: Benjamin Lewis
Review: Cute and cool little cafe spot in Melbourne cbd, totally shocked and very happy with the prices. So affordable right now. No extra charges for alternate milks or decaf 😌🚶🏻‍♂️ thank you, spreading the word and will be back

Name: Otherside Coffee
Author: Joweli
Review: A cozy joint for a quick coffee break. What sets this apart from the many other cafes in town is their very reasonable prices without compromising on quality or quantity.

Name: Otherside Coffee
Author: Brittany C
Review: Superb, cheap coffee in Melbourne. Who knew that was a thing! So excited to see a cafe that had cheap coffee AND it wasn't cheap and bad! Great coffee, happy wallet and happy staff

Name: Otherside Coffee
Author: Chris Price (Pricey)
Review:
This needs to be more of a PSA than a google review but the coffee here is very good and ONLY $3.40 for a small plus $0 for alternative milk! What a steal, thank you Otherside for keeping it real.
There also an extensive selection of health grab and go food options available, what a little gem 💎

Name: Mid Square Coffee
Author: Matthew Aitken
Review:
I had to visit this local coffee spot after seeing the positive reviews and decor. It's well tucked away just off Swanston however when you know, you know. Was greeted quickly however the staff member seemed a little disinterested to see customers.

The service was fantastic however and my oat latte was of a high standard. Great coffee hardware behind the counter and an experienced barista made the investment worthwhile. Definitely somewhere to purchase great coffee.

Name: Mid Square Coffee
Author: Jack Wan Kyal
Review: I always come to this coffee shop for an almond latte and skinny latte at times. Rest assured, the coffee was served even with insane amount of coffees lining up for coffees and a busy cafe. Nonetheless, the almond latte here is blended and mix to perfection. You can literally distinguish the taste between the almond milk and the coffee beans, a perfect barista and blending skills. Jeff and Jess here will look after you and ensure your coffee experience in Carlton is quiet and delightful while ensuring you leave the cafe with tranquility and happiness.

Name: Mid Square Coffee
Author: Samantha T
Review: The chicken slaw toastie was amazing. Crisp bread, juicy chicken, and crunchy slaw. Extra points for the yummy drinks we got, honey lemon ginger and oat chai.

Name: Mid Square Coffee
Author: Jay Xiang
Review: Friendly and nice customer service. Awesome coffee and food! Toasties are so good as well as iced latte. Very unique cafe.

Name: Mid Square Coffee
Author: Yos William
Review: Had a meeting here and ordered their ice chocolate. It was pretty good.

Name: Coffee Place 189
Author: Marianne Tijs
Review: Like a little neighbourhood living room. People coming in and out, chatting with the lovely owners and each other. Heaps of magazines and books and great coffee. Loved the atmosphere!

Name: Coffee Place 189
Author: Adrienne Zervos
Review: Beautiful coffee, friendly owners - feels like being in a country town. Wish we could transport this cafe to Sydney!

Name: Coffee Place 189
Author: Casper Wauters
Review: Amazing little coffee cafe, with great coffee and great atmosphere. Like sitting in your living room having a chat with all customers!

Name: Coffee Place 189
Author: Michele Elkington
Review: Beautiful little cafe, intimate and cozy atmosphere, fresh baguettes that are shockingly cheap, good coffee and lovely service

Name: Coffee Place 189
Author: Alex Platt
Review:
First time today! Great little spot with friendly service and fresh bread / baguettes and pasta.

Will return :)

Name: Little Bang Espresso
Author: Lisa Waller
Review: Little Bang is the best cafe at RMIT Melbourne. The coffee is consistently excellent and the staff are talented, fast and friendly. It’s a nice room for casual meetings and the music is always good. Hard to go past.

Name: Little Bang Espresso
Author: N J
Review: The most balanced mouthful café Latte I’d ever have in Melbourne cbd!!!! I have been here many times! The sourdough sandwiches have never gone wrong, excellent staffs and skilled professionals barista!!!! If you wanna find the excellent coffee this place is A MUST!

Name: Little Bang Espresso
Author: Jessica Loeding
Review:
Little Bang Espresso at RMIT is a gem on campus, offering a cosy environment that is warm and inviting. The thoughtfully arranged seating areas create an atmosphere that is both calming and conducive to focus. One of the standout features of Little Bang Espresso is the staff. They are not only friendly and approachable but also exuded a genuine warmth.

The efficiency of service here is commendable. Despite the steady stream of students and staff stopping by for their caffeine fix, drinks are prepared speedily without compromising on quality. Whether you're in between classes or on a quick break from work, with drinks made with such efficiency, you won’t have to wait long to savour your beverage.

The menu boasts a wide range of beverages, from classic hot drinks to refreshing juices, catering to every preference. On my recent visit, I opted for a chai latte, and I was very impressed. The warmth of the drink made it a comforting choice on a chilly day.

Complementing their drink menu is an impressive selection of pastries. These treats pair wonderfully with any of their drinks and are a great option for a light snack or indulgent treat. During my visit, we found a cosy corner table that became the perfect spot to unwind. It’s little details like these – the comfortable seating, the inviting decor, and the thoughtful layout – that make Little Bang Espresso a standout on campus. Whether you’re a student, a staff member, or simply passing by, a visit to Little Bang Espresso is bound to brighten your day.

Name: Little Bang Espresso
Author: Marklin Ho
Review:
12 Oct 2022 ☘️ Had our very first meal of Australia in this inviting and relaxing cafe 😋 straight after landing in Melbourne Airport

Thank you Ms. April and your colleague for a positive first impression 🙏🏻🌸 excellent service!

My mum and I are so glad to have chosen Victoria as our first holiday destination via air travel in 2022 ❤️ which is perfect considering we had such a fulfilling vacay two years ago soaking in the sights and sounds of Melbourne :)

Name: Little Bang Espresso
Author: SOYEON YU
Review: Visited two times during my stays in Melbourne. Nice place to have a morning coffee! Not only the coffee but also banaba bread was so good !!

Name: First Love Coffee Collins St
Author: Barnaby Lim
Review: This is a regular spot for good coffee and a croissant. There's a nice lawn outside to sit on on a nice sunny day. There's even sparkling water on tap! Conveniently located in front of the W hotel. Don't forget the point card that gets you free 10th coffee!

Name: First Love Coffee Collins St
Author: Michiel Harberts
Review: Coffee was pretty good. Service a little slow, people in front of me chose another place. Double shot latte hit the spot.

Name: First Love Coffee Collins St
Author: Sambo Da Cambo
Review:
Check out how artistic the barista is over here in their lattes, can you see the Swan 🦢  and the Lilly in these photos?
Came to check out this Cafe cause I needed a Matcha Latte afternoon hit and I was satisfied 😋 ☕
There is sparkling and straight tap water available for customers.

Name: First Love Coffee Collins St
Author: XZ C
Review:
Wonderful place for a test after busy work. Tasty coffee, cozzy music and friendly staff make my day.

Share with my friends this gorgeous store in heart of Melbourne CBD under W hotel in Collin St.

Absolutely great experience and would come back. Strongly recommend!!!

Name: First Love Coffee Collins St
Author: Steve Hui (IFLYFLAT)
Review: Perfect indoor and outdoor ☀️ spot with great coffee ☕😍.

Name: Coffee Society
Author: Simon Dormer
Review:
Second time here in a week, great interior and place to catch the morning sunshine.

Great range of food too.

For those wanting to work, plenty of space and sockets under the seats for you power. Toilets are near the lifts.

Name: Coffee Society
Author: Rahul Rao
Review: Nice little cafe with very good coffee. I ate the breakfast burger, which was just perfect. The service is very quick and friendly.

Name: Coffee Society
Author: Tailini Diehl
Review:
Really good coffee and food.
Staff friendly and the atmosphere is comfortable and calm.
Nice music/relaxing

Name: Coffee Society
Author: Jora Shen
Review: The place was quite busy, but the food was the absolute best. The serves were huge and I could not even finish. The lady who served me was very friendly and helpful.

Name: Coffee Society
Author: Greek Aliti
Review: Cool little place, great flavours and friendly atmosphere. Very easy to miss if you’re driving past searching for something to eat or drink

Name: B3 Cafe
Author: P F
Review:
Wow never realised this place existed until a friend recommended it. Truly great value! I paid only $8 for a chicken schnitzel baguette. Got it toasted and didn’t have to wait a very long time even though it was very busy at lunch.

It tasted great and had good texture with the mix of crunchy schnitzel and soft baguette. I shall be back!

Name: B3 Cafe
Author: Nicky O'Brien
Review: Always delicious. Have come here for years to grab a quick work lunch. Eggplant schnitzel is incredible. So fresh, so filling. And an absolute bargain 🤤

Name: B3 Cafe
Author: Aaditya Beri
Review:
This is a small place that closes at 4 PM on weekdays and does not open on weekends. So for someone like me who doesn't work in the city having a Baguette from here was a rare occasion. But whenever I do, I get a baguette from here, I enjoy it thoroughly.
The size of the baguettes is huge. They have 2 sizes, a big one and a really big one costing $5 and $7 resp. I love the chicken schnitzel Baguette from here which has a lot of chicken in it and tastes good. They sell quite a lot of baguettes everyday so you can expect them to be made fresh everyday. I have recommended this place to many of my friends who have enjoyed the baguettes from here. This place is great value for money.

Name: B3 Cafe
Author: GTNathan Gardiner
Review:
Best baguettes in Melbourne.
Fantastic selection of fresh rolls with great fillings. If you want them heated up just ask, the staff are always friendly & willing to help.

Name: B3 Cafe
Author: Kirk Hogan
Review: I am in MELBOURNE a few times a month for work. This is the only place I visit for lunch. Tandoori Baguette (toasted) this the best. Fresh, warm, great flavour and good price! Do yourself a favour and treat yoirself!

Name: The Corner House Cafe and Shisha
Author: Frances Salazar
Review: It was pleasant dining in for brunch today with my children. Had the fisherman’s plate and definitely enjoyed it. the best part is also the iced coffee… looks so good and taste so great! will definitely go back! I love the lady from the counter she’s nice too.

Name: The Corner House Cafe and Shisha
Author: Ankush Arora
Review: Best Shisha in the town. Food quality was excellent 🤞The ambiance of this Place is simply fantastic! The Owner was friendly and attentive, always making sure me and my friends were comfortable and had everything I needed. In addition to the great drinks and music 🎶 ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Name: The Corner House Cafe and Shisha
Author: Cassalamski
Review: A very cute cafe located in the middle of a residential area. They have things for sale inside the cafe. Very relaxing atmosphere. Food and coffee are very good. Only thing I can pick on is it's a big hot inside due to glass ceiling and around. Overall it's a very nice place for brunch and coffee.

Name: The Corner House Cafe and Shisha
Author: Fiona S
Review: Our local cafe that I truly cannot fault. It’s the best tasting coffee in the area. The food tastes as good as it looks every time. Our go-tos are the Mediterranean big breakfast and smashed avo. Outdoor seating, pet friendly. I love that they also have gifts and flowers for last minute gifts. Thanks guys!

Name: The Corner House Cafe and Shisha
Author: Rahul Puri
Review: What a great cafe filled up with a wonderful ambience, nice lovely people serving food with a smile. Loved our coffee as well, which was perfectly made, couldn't have asked for more on a cold winter morning! Keep it up guys, would love to come back again!!

Name: No22 Cafe
Author: Shannon Mauri
Review:
Absolutely gorgeous little find for brunch ❤️
Amazing focaccia, the owners are beautiful authentic Italians who are so kind, friendly and passionate about their food. The flour is Italian and sits so light in your stomach. I had the vegan one, it was delicious. Hubby was brave and went with "what mood chef was in" he loved it! We had amazing coffee and looked over the street. Just perfect 👌
Thank you for your kind hospitality and warm welcome Cafe 22, we will recommend you to all we know xx

Name: No22 Cafe
Author: Azharul Islam
Review: Amazing place; cute small setup with a top notch food. The service was like a 5-star hotel. We felt well treated. The bread was delicious and the combination was worth trying. I highly recommend this place people who love to taste authentic Italian focaccia bread. And the flour is imported all the way from Italy.

Name: No22 Cafe
Author: Foodanddrinkmelbourne
Review:
@n22cafe is lovingly run by husband and wife team, Tano and Jess. Located on Flinders St in CBD Melbourne, their cafe showcases authentic Italian focaccias, straight from their home region of Puglia.

We had:
🇮🇹 Autentica with Mortadella, Stracciatella cheese and crumbled pistachios
🥦 La Campana with Southern Italian style sausage and Friarielli
🚀 Tartufata with Prosciutto, black truffle, Stracciatella cheese and rocket
☕️ Skinny Latte and Skinny Piccolo

My favourites were the Tartufata and Autentica, and hubby adored the La Campana. The focaccias are so light and fresh, and the truly Italian ingredients take them to the next level. They are fully loaded and full of flavour!

Tano and Jess are amazing hosts with an obvious passion for what they do. And with so many focaccia options, there is definitely something for everyone.

Name: No22 Cafe
Author: Luka Owens
Review: The most delicious sandwiches, and probably the best coffee I’ve had in years. The owners were so delightful to talk to. Highly recommend getting their recommended sandwiches, we had the authentic sandwich which was just insanely good. Thanks No22!

Name: No22 Cafe
Author: Eddie Whiteway
Review:
Here in Melbourne on holiday, stopped here almost in disbelief as it had a 5 star average... but it definitely earns it!
The owner greeted us warmly from the moment we walked through the doors and explained the menu. He was very friendly, had good reccomendations, and was very warm - he came by our table often to chat and ensure our experience was going well. Was also very accommodating to dietary requests!

The food was very authentic and delicious, and the coffee was good.

This place didn't just feel like a meal but like an experience. Could not more highly reccomend it! Will be back next time we are in Melbourne!

Name: Industry Beans Lt. Collins
Author: Thomas Zulliger
Review:
Always worth a visit.
Nice flat white.
Love when they have free sparkling water to compliment your coffee drink.
Ceramic cup instead of paper cup upon request is perfect.
Sufficient seating which is nice on a rainy day in Melbourne.
Industry Beans should be on your list.
Recommended!

Name: Industry Beans Lt. Collins
Author: Felicia Evelyn
Review: Tried their ice latte and i think it’s more into the sweeter side (coffee’s not too strong). Brownies were good. Limited seating.

Name: Industry Beans Lt. Collins
Author: Tan T
Review:
Not too bad for ice coffee but not sure if it’s just me but coffee bean didn’t felt the best. It was good to try but not maybe once in a while thing. The cafe set up is quite simpler and nice. Service was okish not very friendly.

Good for one time try.

Name: Industry Beans Lt. Collins
Author: Gigi T
Review: Very disappointing customer service. As a well-known coffee shop in the city, I expected above-average service. When we walked in, the barista, who was also handling the counter, rudely asked us to wait. Although we were not in a rush and waited patiently, she didn't make eye contact or greet us kindly when it was our turn to order. The store wasn’t busy, and while she seemed friendly with her co-worker, her attitude towards us, as customers, was unacceptable. This negatively impacted my coffee experience and the taste of my latte. Bad service and a bad impression of Industry Beans, which is supposed to be a must-try coffee spot. 😢

Name: Industry Beans Lt. Collins
Author: Andreymae Meora
Review: My partner and I love flat white coffee, and they nailed it. Their pastries taste freshly baked—the almond croissant was crisp and had the right amount of filling; the peanut butter brownie was gooey.

Name: Dixie Bakery Cafe
Author: Alexandros Karvounis
Review: I recently had breakfast at Dixie Bakery Cafe and had an incredibly positive experience. The food is absolutely delicious and the coffee is amazing. The staff are friendly and happy to recommend choices, which added to the welcoming atmosphere. They offer a great variety of options on their menu, all at reasonable prices. I particularly enjoyed the boreka breakfast. The venue itself is spacious with bright and pleasant decor, making it a delightful place to dine. I would definitely return!

Name: Dixie Bakery Cafe
Author: Lee Goldsmith
Review: One of my favourite places to go! One that is definitely a regular! The shakshuka is next level! So much flavour! Everything is prepared with such attention to detail! Super delicious!

Name: Dixie Bakery Cafe
Author: Tessa McMahon
Review: Coffee was great, loved my breakfast too, some great options and friendly staff. Recommend!!

Name: Dixie Bakery Cafe
Author: Hamish John Appleby
Review: My experience at Dixie cafe was very positive indeed. Famous for their sumptuous cakes, they also do lots of delicious savoury dishes - Roasted Sweet Potatoes And Beetroot Salad, Red Lentil Soup and Cheese and Spinach Bourekas, to name a few! The music is never too loud, the natural light in the cafe is relaxing and people are made to feel welcome, to take their time and savour what is on offer. Excellent service as well! 10/10 - Hamish John Appleby APPLEBY PHOTOGRAPHY

Name: Dixie Bakery Cafe
Author: Bar Attar
Review: Great food! My favorite place for breakfast. And definitely the best muffins

Name: MEEYA CAFE
Author: jorja lirsch
Review: We waited over 30 mins outside for a table, which was understandable given we went on a weekend. However waiting another 30 minutes once seated for two slices of cake and an iced tea was a bit excessive. People who arrived after us were served the exact same thing before us. The cake painting was fun and relaxing and I’d recommend it for the novelty of it but it didn’t taste amazing. I really wanted to like my time here but it was disappointing.

Name: MEEYA CAFE
Author: Liza Bun
Review:
Great place to come and chill with your buddies! I love the cute little corner store vibe of the store, very cozy but also very welcoming. I ordered the cake painting (17.9$) and the biscoff Latte (9$). Both were pretty acceptable in terms of taste but they do look very appealing! Very instagram worthy spot! Also the staff were extremely friendly. Limited in seating so recommended to come on a weekday!
Give it a shot if you looking for a small fun activity to do!

Name: MEEYA CAFE
Author: Yu-Mei Len
Review:
My best friend and I went to this cafe specifically for my birthday so we can paint on cakes. The place was small but lively and we had a wonderful time there. We spend around 2hrs on painting, the cake is a little too sweet for us, but it was the experience that was worth the visit.

The cafe has limited cakes per day, and it gets especially busy during the weekend, so it's best to come early or reserve a table in advance.

Name: MEEYA CAFE
Author: Ayu
Review: Cute hidden cafe in Melbourne. Creative cake painting experience. Drinks were interesting. Staffs were nice and gave us lots of equipments to paint the cake. Wished that they could have given us more “paint” to finish our work.

Name: MEEYA CAFE
Author: asmo
Review: A very wonderful experience. The cake decorating was fun, and the servers were also very sweet. I didn’t expect much from the sponge cake we decorated on in terms of taste, but the cake was actually very good and had a nice combination of sweet and salty. The biscoff latte was good, but it’s just a normal latte decorated with a biscoff cookie.

Name: Commonplace Coffee Brewers Open 365 Days
Author: Kendrick Naiborhu
Review:
One of good coffee shops in Melbourne. Coffee is good!! *strong flat white is my go to coffee
Also the pastries, especially my favorites is chocolate croissants 🤤
This place’s friendly with kids, they have baby chino plus marshmallows for my little daughter.
Almost every weekend spent time here with my family.
Really recommend this coffee shops. 👍🏼👍🏼👍🏼

Name: Commonplace Coffee Brewers Open 365 Days
Author: mrsm
Review:
This review is just for their cold drinks. The croissants and pastries look notmal but nothing special about them as you also get them from places like Merriment, Starbucks etc.

I ordered the Matcha cold drink. It wasn’t terribly cold, it was a bit bland and boring. I certainly can taste the matcha but that’s about it that I can taste. It wasnt sweet, no boba or jelly, no cream, just matcha and maybe milk. It was a bit of an underwhelming experience.

The place is small, there’s a bench outside to wait for your order, pastries inside, that’s about it.

Name: Commonplace Coffee Brewers Open 365 Days
Author: Russell King
Review: Daily filter coffee of different rotations, pastries, fast friendly service. Highly recommend. Give the 82hr slow filter brew a try!

Name: Commonplace Coffee Brewers Open 365 Days
Author: Queyn Lisl
Review: Lovely coffee, prompt service. And they're open early on weekends, which is an absolute plus. I was disappointed Commonplace only offers landfill cups, even for dine-in customers. Would love the team to please consider serving from non-landfill cups (something like Huskee would do nicely!), but I know many small businesses are dealing with rising costs these days. Keep on keeping on, Commonplace.

Name: Commonplace Coffee Brewers Open 365 Days
Author: Tristan Trotter
Review:
Breakfast 24/08/21
Coffee with ham & cheese croissant

Staff are so friendly, I think they have the most Covid safe setup I have seen in the area and lovely spot!
Coffee was 10/10 and so was the croissant too many people stuff up such simple things but these guys nailed it thank-you!

Name: Market Lane Coffee - Queen Victoria Market
Author: Alex Soliman
Review: Nice cafe located at Victoria Market. Service is friendly and speedy with the coffee tasting really good. We decided to sit outside as it was a nice and a fairly warmer day in Melbourne. There is some limited seating space available indoors as well. This place specialises in everything coffee with some accessories and merchandise available for purchase. Do drop by for your caffeine fix and enjoy.

Name: Market Lane Coffee - Queen Victoria Market
Author: Hugo Chan
Review:
We came here on a cold Saturday morning trying to locate a nice place to shelter from the rain and cold wind.

This is a small shop in size, but it was buzzing with busy customers buying a caffeine hit. We ordered a coffee (Colombian type of bean) and an iced latte.

The staff were super friendly and even offered us to try a special type of brew without us asking! Coffee is definitely subjective, but we enjoyed our drinks on top of the great customer service. Would recommend a visit for coffee lovers!

Name: Market Lane Coffee - Queen Victoria Market
Author: Sheila C
Review:
Household coffee brand
with a very convenient branch location in Queen Victoria Market. Expected coffee to stand out more considering that it managed to be such a popular name in Melbourne’s uber vibrant coffee scene but it was just OK.

Name: Market Lane Coffee - Queen Victoria Market
Author: Joweli
Review: One of my favourite cafes in town. The minimalist and chill vibes, and friendly staff make this cozy joint a perfect spot to refuel on caffeine and pastries. Indoor seating is limited to the high-top bar but outdoor seating is also available. Interesting merch and coffee beans are also in stock here.

Name: Market Lane Coffee - Queen Victoria Market
Author: Fire Hazard
Review: Nice place to get a cup of coffee and relax while enjoying the Melbourne markets. Plenty of space outside to sit and enjoy the weather and there are some activities available for kids. It also seemed to be very dog friendly.

Name: Puzzle Coffee
Address: Southern Cross, Spencer St &, Collins St, Docklands
Author: Janelle
Review: Tried the iced cappuccino here which was recommended by the staff, it's SO GOOD! Liked how it has a layer of cream on top, plus their own Riddle coffee blend, overall tasted so smooth and it's just awesome!!🤍 Also got to try their Strawberry Matcha, Strawberry Yogurt and Yuzu Espresso Sparkling (seasonal specials), love the sparkling yuzu espresso the most, it was very refreshing but also not overpowering the taste of coffee. Will definitely come back again for their coffee🤤

Name: Puzzle Coffee
Address: Southern Cross, Spencer St &, Collins St, Docklands
Author: Oracle Chow
Review:
good place for a quick caffeine fix! very conveniently located, with friendly staff 💓

ordered:
✦ iced strawberry matcha ($8.00)
✦ hot hojicha ($5.50) + edible grain cup ($3)
✦ ham & cheese croissant ($7.50)
✦ peanut butter & choc cookie ($5.99)

Name: Puzzle Coffee
Address: Southern Cross, Spencer St &, Collins St, Docklands
Author: Elle
Review:
Amazing coffee and service!! Great place for a pit stop and reasonably priced too!
Not to mention the yummy snacks

Name: Puzzle Coffee
Address: Southern Cross, Spencer St &, Collins St, Docklands
Author: 서동훈
Review: The cafe was really blusting, so I wasn’t expecting much in terms of service, but the manager and staff were super friendly and quick. In addition, the coffee tasted good enough.

Name: Puzzle Coffee
Address: Southern Cross, Spencer St &, Collins St, Docklands
Author: Julie Egitto
Review:
good coffee and have an option of edible cup.
was a good experience with the cup. just wish it the cup comes on different flavours, like cinnamon and honey or a chocolate one.

Name: Jumi's Cafe
Author: Rosie Ha
Review: Their acai was soo good and their housemade nutella was incredible! Generous portion size however the price is a bit steep. I saw that they had a wide selection of vegan sandwiches and energy balls and would definitely come back to try them! Their juice also was so tasty!

Name: Jumi's Cafe
Author: Foodie Files Melbourne
Review: The chairs feel like they are about to break and the staff are a bit frazzled (they brought us another customers order on 3 different occasions) but the açai itself was relatively tasty. Seems like a straight up version rather than banana blend as it wasn’t as sweet as others but if you enjoy a well balanced smoothie bowl definitely check it out

Name: Jumi's Cafe
Author: Andrea Suarez
Review: Staff was friendly and very helpful. My toastie was cooked perfectly and the coffee was great. I was happy to find this cafe so near to my hotel. Will go back regularly!

Name: Jumi's Cafe
Author: sarah
Review:
Wow For me the best smoothie bowls in Melbourne
I also had a great coffee and amazing juice here in South Yarra
I am in love with this place. They’re super friendly, serve great Açaí and their Rocher is a must
I love the flavour and texture of the blended deliciousness
I ordered the following:
Ferrero Rocher Açaí and you have to try it - don’t miss out on the delicious 🤤 vegan nutty hazelnut spread on top! I could eat a whole jar of this spread
Packed with flavours
They sell some protein bars and powder as well  you can either sit inside or outside

I also had
GREEN GARDEN
Pineapple, banana, spinach, avocado..
chia, spirulina, passiontruit & coco water
And a Flat white with oat

Açaí bowl
Ferrero rocher
granola, banana, strawberries,
hazelnuts & peanut choc
spread, kiwi
No banana in Açaí - pure Açaí and simply delicious

This is a fully healthy vegan spot which deserves some recognition💝🥰
Fuel your body with great Açaí 🥹🙌

Name: Jumi's Cafe
Author: ADITI SAHA
Review:
Start your week on the right note by detoxing with healthy sandwiches, juices, superfood smoothies and açai bowls from @jumis_cafe

A 100% plant based cafe specialising in freshly made morning breakfast options. We tried the rainbow sandwich which is a slaw sandwich full of so many goodness, feels very light and refreshing to eat. Must try.

The Reuben toastie was so satisfying and yummy, loved the combination of the flavours of sauerkraut, mustard, cheddar and Russian dressing besides tomato, tempeh and pickles. Highly recommended

Green machine and stay up beet juices are very filling, yet light and fresh. Both were equally amazing and must try.

The nutter açai bowl is perfect for someone who loves a strong nutty taste, and their house made almond butter is the key factor in this açai bowl, making it so irresistible, mouthwatering and very delicious. The açai is of good quality, and you could tell right from the very first try. Highly recommend.

The service is fantastic, prompt and attentive and the staff here are all super helpful, cheerful, friendly and very welcoming in nature. The prices are way better and reasonable than some overpriced açai places, also their quality is really supreme.

So why not make your Monday mornings wiser by grabbing a nutritious, vegan brekkie from @jumis_cafe

Name: Poolhouse Coffee
Author: 이영은
Review: Always lovely. Warm and peaceful atmosphere, best white coffee in Melbourne(in my opinion), authentic spring rolls, absolutely favorite. Looking forward to seeing yall soon. Luv, XX.

Name: Poolhouse Coffee
Author: Jye Jing Ng
Review:
A must visit cafe in Melbourne🫶They are the most underrated cafe that serves that most underrated matcha! 🍵 perfectly balanced with the milk, creating a smooth, rich flavor that’s not too bitter or too sweet. My partner also loves their Mocha and soy latte.

Even better, the atmosphere is always welcoming, thanks to Roland, the amazing barista! He makes the cutest coffee art that make our day 😊

Name: Poolhouse Coffee
Author: Owen McKenna
Review:
I had to go into RMIT, to deal with enrolment matters. The Poolhouse cafe was recommended, with starry eyes, I kid-you-not, by 2 separate RMIT employees.
Now I know why.
Sadly I work from home otherwise I would be there every day.
Great cafe, perfect barista service, warm friendly cheerful efficient staff, delicious toasties, bright comfortable environs.
Just go there!

Name: Poolhouse Coffee
Author: Paul Omen
Review: I got a cappuccino and it really wasn’t my favorite. I don’t know if it was the milk or the coffee but something tasted a bit too acidic. The owner was super kind but I still don’t think I would return since the quality of the coffee was not more favorite.

Name: Poolhouse Coffee
Author: Hajung Park (Rei)
Review:
Matcha is so good here and Roland drew me my bd cake on matcha. Impressive art.
Phong(the boss) is very nice and everyone here is so good i love this place :)

Name: Emerald Hill Cafe South Melbourne
Author: Beth Evans
Review:
Delicious food & fantastic service! Katerina and the team were great at handling a last minute birthday party for my son.
Thank you so much!

Name: Emerald Hill Cafe South Melbourne
Author: Kay Libson
Review: Katrina and the team have been great with our weekly orders for "Lunch and Learn" meetings. Always fresh and on time, any special requests are handled without fuss.

Name: Emerald Hill Cafe South Melbourne
Author: Patrick Trisna
Review:
Emerald Hill is a quaint family run cafe at South Melbourne. At only $3.80 a cup, their coffee is probably the best priced in the area.
I had their home made sausage rolls to accompany my coffee, and I enjoyed it very much. The roll was packed with filling and was full of flavour. The pastry was buttery and flaky. I thought it was much tastier than many of the specialty bakeries around the area. Left a happy customer :)

Name: Emerald Hill Cafe South Melbourne
Author: Aman B
Review: Love their poached eggs and toast, and the coffee is fantastic too! This set used to be $9.90 but is now priced separately, but remains as good as it was in quality. Take your time...inside it's as if time has stood still for a few decades.

Name: Emerald Hill Cafe South Melbourne
Author: Kathy Dennis
Review: The food was excellent especially the falafel salad. The dressing was so tasty. They are inventive with cakes too. Their chocolate and raspberry cake was delicious. So was their Black Forest cake. All vegan...so I thought of the cleverness it took to whip these gastronomic delights up. Big compliments to the chef! Their service was exemplary. The staff are lovely. I’d happily go back. Wish I didn’t live so far away.

Name: 茶屋
Author: Laurence Jackson
Review: Visited for the Princess Diana exhibit. What a great place!

Name: 茶屋
Author: Kylie Hull
Review:
Attended venue to see Princess Di Exhibition. Male staff very friendly upon arrival.  Showed me around. Plenty of seats and fans to keep us all cool. Bathrooms very clean
Close to town centre. Public transport right to Door. Worth 1000 stars. Highly recommended

Name: 茶屋
Author: Sue Hampson
Review: Went there this morning to see the Princess Diana exhibition, with a ticket I’d bought through Fever. When I got there the front door was locked but there was a woman on the phone at the front desk. She indicated she would open the door after the phone call. I waited several minutes, then realised when I checked the Fever site that my ticket had been cancelled. So I left, as there was certainly no point in waiting for the woman on the phone- she was in no hurry to help. I hope I get my money back….

Name: 茶屋
Author: Belinda Paterson
Review: Loved the building.

Name: 茶屋
Author: Lim Kian Ming
Review: Move Oolong. No tea to see here.

Name: Oriental Teahouse Melbourne CBD
Author: sventek
Review: We visited for a special occasion and had a pleasant experience. Food quality was above average. It is a bit more on the expensive side, but considering its location, reasonable. Staff were absolutely amazing, delightful and very family friendly, made you feel really welcome and provided that "let's have a good time here but still remain professional" vibes. Loved the ambiance. Overall would recommend. Price guide (Sep 2024) tea charged pp $6, cheapest dumpling plate $9 (Yumcha A), goes up a dollar every class, (Yumcha H $16), Yumcha special  $18,  weekend surcharge 10%, 15 plates (3A, 1C, 5E, 3F, 1G, 1H, 1special) + cocktail + drink + tea + surcharge costed us $259.

Name: Oriental Teahouse Melbourne CBD
Author: Novi Di
Review: Love there's a whole plant based section! Would love to see more vegan options, enjoyed the dumplings and what a wonderful mocktail 💕 dessert is nice as well! A bit sad the noodles used egg noodles wasn't offered an alternative for the noodles so went with the dumplings. Great vibe and attentive waiters:)

Name: Oriental Teahouse Melbourne CBD
Author: Stephen “STPN” Pillay
Review:
Everything about this place is great, the food, the staff, the ambiance 💯
I have found myself here on multiple occasions, from planned nights to walk-ins. They are always accommodating and I have never had a bad meal here.
This has become 'my fav place to eat when I don't know what to eat' in the city 😂

Highly recommend:
Steamed Fish with Ginger Garlic & Chilli
Wagyu Dumplings
Legit Fried Rice
🌶️ 🧄 🐟 🥟🍚

Name: Oriental Teahouse Melbourne CBD
Author: Jojo Kat
Review:
I recently visited this restaurant and overall, the service was good. It seems to be quite popular among nearby office workers, which is always a good sign!

For lunch, we ordered a variety of dishes, including the AUTHENTIC PRAWN DUMPLINGS, PORK & PRAWN SHU MAI, XO SCALLOP & PRAWN DUMPLINGS, XIAO LONG BAO, CHINESE CABBAGE PORK DUMPLINGS, and BLACK BEAN BARRAMUNDI FILLET. Most of the dim sums were quite enjoyable, although I found the Xiao Long Bao to be less juicy than expected.

However, I was a bit disappointed with the BLACK BEAN BARRAMUNDI FILLET – it was quite salty, and the fish was quite tough. The black bean sauce overwhelmed the other flavors, which was a letdown.

For dessert, we tried the CHINESE WONKA DUMPLINGS and COCONUT & OSMANTHUS JELLY. I absolutely loved the CHINESE WONKA DUMPLINGS! The sesame-covered shell was crispy, and the rich chocolate filling was not overly sweet – just perfect!

Overall, it's a decent place to satisfy your dim sum cravings. Would recommend for the experience!

Name: Oriental Teahouse Melbourne CBD
Author: Pavinee Gaffney
Review:
We had a company team meeting lunch here. As the group is bigger than 8+, we had to order a feed me menu which we were okay with at the start. The options on the menu are slightly limited and you can't choose whatever you want on the list. We opted to go with 8 dishes and the option we have to select 2 items from the stream section and the deep fry, 3 items from the main and one dessert. None of us eat Asian dessert so we asked to swap it with something else and you can only pick from the stream section to replace dessert. You can't just choose dumplings and Dim Sim for all 8 dishes.
As we had all the food details sorted before the day, we were expected a little quicker service than we received. They especially advertise the feed me as unlimited feed that can reorder as much as you can eat in 90 minutes (8 dishes for $56 per person). The first set of stream and fried dishes didn't get to the table until 22 minutes after we seated. Then when we finished all the stream and fry dishes and tried to order a couple more dishes, we were then told we can't reorder anything until all the dishes on the lists have arrived. All the mains and the rest of the dishes arrived at the table after 45 minutes. The staff took some reorder items then. Not all of group like eating the same things so we all reorder want we want to eat and the item I reorder which is a prawn and shallots wonton didn't arrive until 70 minutes into the seating time.
All the reorder dishes also go down in size, for example, the veggies spring roll has 6 pieces in the first serve, but only 4 in the second serve so when we thought one serve will be enough but then some people had to miss out.
To finish it off, they still manage to put through wrong charges of 10 dishes at $68 per person at the start after spending 5 minutes going through the 8 dishes to confirm our order.
Overall very disappointed with the group booking and foods.

Name: Oriental Teahouse South Yarra
Author: Ramsey Samad
Review: I recently dined at Oriental Tea House and while the food was enjoyable, particularly the salt and pepper calamari, the service left much to be desired. We also ordered a beef dish, some appetisers, and white chocolate dumplings for dessert. The appetisers came out quickly, but the main dishes took an excessive amount of time—around 35 to 40 minutes each, which really slowed down the meal. Unfortunately, this happened with every new dish we ordered, leading to a dinner that lasted over two and a half hours. While the food was solid, the poor service really overshadowed the experience, and I wouldn’t be in a rush to return.

Name: Oriental Teahouse South Yarra
Author: Min K
Review: Enjoyed a delightful birthday dinner here! The atmosphere was perfect, and the free tea tasting was a lovely touch. The prawn dumplings were outstanding, and the staff were incredibly friendly, always with a smile. Can't wait to visit again!

Name: Oriental Teahouse South Yarra
Author: Sandy Falcone
Review:
We had a very good experience overall with the “Feast with Us $66” Buffett and very attentive service and although we had some nice dishes there a couple that were ok only.
The dumplings overall with the great selection of sauces were great as well as the duck pancake and Chinese broccoli with oyster sauce but we didn’t find the special fried rice and white chocolate dumplings as enjoyable.  The beer selection was very good.  I recommend but do research on dishes.

Name: Oriental Teahouse South Yarra
Author: Carmy Law
Review: Had delightful time here as per usual. There’s nothing bad on the menu after dining here over 6 times. It stays true to Chinese but with a twist. The steamed buns and dumplings weren’t the best so don’t expect a full yumcha experience here, but dumplings are dumplings. The stir fry, fried rice and the noodles were great. Love the drink menu rotation especially the traditional festival inspired/limited drinks. The dessert wasn’t its strongest suit but the mains definitely make up for it. Will keep coming back for sure.

Name: Oriental Teahouse South Yarra
Author: Shayan Soroush
Review:
Lovely restaurant in South Yarra - newly renovated at the time we went. We ordered the legit special fried rice for $19 each.

The newly renovated decor provides a nice intimate vibe for date tonight, casual catch up or a family event.

The staff were very friendly, the friend that I came here with had been a few times and the owner recognised him and even took some time to chat. This adds a nice and personal touch and keeps him coming back.

Given the quality of the food, the ambience of the restaurant and the quality of the service we thought this was a great price. Thank you so much for the experience!

Name: The Third Tier
Author: Lua Astor
Review:
Today, I truly felt like royalty alongside my best friend at The Third Tier Tea Room. We experienced the High Tea Diamond Deluxe, and it was absolutely perfect!

It's giving "The Love Witch".

I highly recommend trying the Altina Le Blanc organic wine, it’s so refreshing and pairs beautifully with the high tea meal. For fellow rose lovers like me, the Pink Lady Tea is a must-try, with its delicate floral notes that make the whole experience even more magical.

Whether you're celebrating a wedding, baby shower, small gathering, or just indulging in some leisure time with friends, The Third Tier is the perfect spot. We booked in advance, and I recommend doing the same for horse racing days, they're open from 10 AM to 3 PM. For those who love a relaxing start to the day, breakfast begins at 9 o’clock, with morning coffee and sandwiches available for a laid-back experience.

The decor is pure elegance, with a blend of French and English styles, making the atmosphere feel luxurious. We indulged in traditional housemade scones with delicious cream and jam, which were truly the highlight of our visit!

For a sophisticated afternoon of leisure, The Third Tier also could offer table painting, paint and sip sessions, or even ladies' games for a bit of fun (for your celebratory needs). Whatever the occasion, whether it’s a bridal shower or just a classy sandwich with friends, this is the place to be!

Name: The Third Tier
Author: Mia Gardiner
Review: My friends and I were really impressed with The Third Tier. We partook in the most expensive option and we're really happy with our free (non-alcoholic) glass of bubbly & complimentary coffee before our three tier morning tea was bought out. The tea options were all delicious, and I recommend trying the Blue Tango tea, it's delicious. The food was delicious and there was only one item I wasn't a fan of. The atmosphere was quite lovely and the tea sets used, plates, etc., were all beautifully matched. Don't be fooled by the fact it's located on the busy Nicholson/Blyth intersection, it's an oasis of calm and elegance inside.

Name: The Third Tier
Author: Lalamama
Review:
Food looks great but the tast was far from expectations.
Bao buns were cold, scones were dry, deep-fried wrapped rice is crumbly..no pork or salami in sandwich is not a sandwich. Only consolation was the warm fresh tea.

Good place to gather in nice ambient but sun beaming directly into the diners caused us feeling sweaty wasn't great.
Wont come back again.

Name: The Third Tier
Author: Sophie and Clarissa
Review: Spent my birthday here and had such a wonderful time! Beautiful place, great food (the chicken raisin sandwich, fried chicken sliders and all the desserts including the scones were amazing), excellent service 🫖🍰 Can’t recommend it enough, great value for the amount of food as well!

Name: The Third Tier
Author: Mahwaqib Lifestyle
Review:
Gorgeous English tea room with exquisite food served at perfection. The interior of café is gorgeous, I am glad Melbourne finally has a beautiful English tea room with French vibes.

Placement of tea sets was perfect, china a was clean and silverware were polished.

The diamond platter presented perfectly and each item was fresh and delicious. I would rate your quality to be surely of a luxury high tea level.

Name: Kallista Tea Rooms
Author: Amy Hicks
Review: Beautiful view, loved watching the birds in the tree. The customer service was fantastic!! I loved the scones with jam and cream. Some of the savoury items could have been better and the sweet tarts tasted like the tart crust was bought but the fillings were delicious. It got a bit noisy a bit later on but it was the school holidays.

Name: Kallista Tea Rooms
Author: Valyn White
Review: Visited Sep 2024 for late afternoon tea. Staff extremely welcoming and service was great. Food slightly slow to come out but it was busy so, to be expected. Kids loved sitting by the windows with the cockatoos sitting outside less than a meter away. We had the calamari salad, some pumpkin soup, a ham and cheese foccacia, chips, scones and some cake. Everything was exceptionally well prepared.

Name: Kallista Tea Rooms
Author: Nicole Block
Review: Went for Friday afternoon scones, jam and cream. Wasn’t busy as it is winter. Needed to get up and get our own menus. Service was just ok. Scones were big and tasty, although a little dry. Apple crumble was nice, but was barely warm. Lots of easy parking.

Name: Kallista Tea Rooms
Author: Vivian Phan
Review:
Kallista tea rooms kindly accommodated our decently sized (6 people) group as walk-ins on a rainy cold day. But it is recommended to book we just got lucky!

Service was a bit slow but we weren't in a rush and they did get slammed when we were there.

Everything was a good sized portion. The scones were huge and had good flavour but did have that chalkiness at the end. Good amount of jam and cream provided. Creamy delicious hot chocolate.

There is parking down the hill but it can be hard to traverse if it's very muddy.

Name: Kallista Tea Rooms
Author: Bella B
Review: Visited 05.08.2024. Beautiful place with a vintage setting. Love that feeling of country style. The staff are friendly and they service is great. Plenty of parking. There's even a playful area for the kids. Food is delicious.

Name: Miss Marple's Tearoom
Author: Monique G
Review: This was packed at 2:30pm on a Tuesday. | everything about this place took me back to my childhood. It is definitely a vibe. | Everything we had was good and the staff were lovely. | They had an old school highchair to fit the aesthetic. I’d only mention that my little one kept climbing out of it as there’s no belts. | Parking is limited on the main road but you will surely find some close by

Name: Miss Marple's Tearoom
Author: Candice Lee
Review:
Enjoyed our meal in this quaint little tearoom! Kids really loved the ham and cheese finger sandwich, soup (potato and leek) and the raspberry cream that was served with the scones. Scones were served warm and soooo moist. We had a plain one and a fruit one.

They also recommended the beef cottage pie which we cleaned up entirely. It was really delicious considering cottage pies are usually not our choice of food.

Would be worth a return visit if passing by the ranges again.

Name: Miss Marple's Tearoom
Author: sina najarha
Review: Ms. Marple restaurant offers a charming and nostalgic dining experience. The atmosphere transports you to another era, with vintage decor that beautifully captures the essence of the old Ms. Marple series and movies. The walls adorned with pictures from the show brought back memories from my childhood when I used to watch it. It felt like stepping into a cozy, mystery-filled world. The ambiance, combined with great food and attentive service, made it a unique and delightful visit. Highly recommended!

Name: Miss Marple's Tearoom
Author: S & S
Review: The scones definitely stole the spotlight, they were so soft and fluffy! We loved the vibe of the tea room and will definitely be back for more! However, be prepared to queue for a bit!

Name: Miss Marple's Tearoom
Author: Romney Muliaina
Review: Great experience, very nostalgic little place in the hills. Definitely worth the wait. Best scones I've had topped off with endless jam and cream.

Name: Serenity Teahouse
Address: Shop SP023A, Box Hill Central (North, 17-21 Market St, Box Hill
Author: Paige
Review: Bought a beautiful tea set from here. The lady was soo nice, friendly and helpful. She answered my questions about the tea set and showed me how to use it. Definitely recommend coming here. And I’m sure I’ll be back to buy more bits and bobs for my tea!

Name: Serenity Teahouse
Address: Shop SP023A, Box Hill Central (North, 17-21 Market St, Box Hill
Author: Aloka Perera
Review: Love the place and lady is very nice. I buy Oolong tea from there and has such a large collection of quality teas available for a reasonable price.

Name: Serenity Teahouse
Address: Shop SP023A, Box Hill Central (North, 17-21 Market St, Box Hill
Author: Maria Ngo
Review: A really good selection of quality Taiwanese teas that the connoisseur is due to appreciate. You can sample the tea before you purchase it. Real rose petals from France as well as ginger slices for use in tea. Helpful staff.

Name: Serenity Teahouse
Address: Shop SP023A, Box Hill Central (North, 17-21 Market St, Box Hill
Author: Lena Boros
Review: What a gorgeous little tea shop / with all your favourite tea cups & pots / Great Gift ideas for Friends & Family!! In Box Hill pay a visit!! Serenity Teahouse/ nice Lady in Shop!!

Name: Serenity Teahouse
Address: Shop SP023A, Box Hill Central (North, 17-21 Market St, Box Hill
Author: My Retro Game Hub
Review: There’s a huge range of all sorts of teas, from green, oolong, black, white and herbal teas. Specializes in Taiwanese teas and authentic purple clay tea pots and bamboo tea sets. A must go for all tea connoisseurs.

Name: Tea Room at National Gallery of Victoria
Author: Brooklyn B
Review: Simply incredible! We enjoyed the high tea with champagne. Everything was incredible. All the food was beyond amazing. We were treated with care and respect. The customer service was next level! I hope the waiters and chefs are paid very well, they all deserve it. Thank you so so much.

Name: Tea Room at National Gallery of Victoria
Author: Lucien Lim
Review:
A great place to sit and chat with friends and loved ones after visiting the NGV. While the food and tea weren’t anything special, the service was so good that it made the food and drinks taste better. In particular, our main server was extremely courteous and friendly, really brightening up our day.

A lovely experience overall, highly recommended!

Name: Tea Room at National Gallery of Victoria
Author: Gina
Review: Yum yum, 🥂 club sandwiches, savory pastries, the most delicious little sweet treats topped off with Tea, scones, Jam & cream. With a nice walk around NGV. Who would have thought this little place existed 😍

Name: Tea Room at National Gallery of Victoria
Author: Bronwyn Dajczer
Review:
Fabulous experience of High Tea @NGV. It was busy as it's the Christmas/New Year break but we were warmly greeted, served with grace and the food was delicious.
The Champagne was creamy and refreshing, the only fault was the thick glassed flute. The food was amazing and just enough without being too much to enjoy.
Definitely recommend this treat 💕

Name: Tea Room at National Gallery of Victoria
Author: Kim R
Review: Very enjoyable with a menu that catered for all tastes.  Fresh local produce from local suppliers including the Yarra Valley which was presented well and in a timely manner. The staff were attentive, friendly and treated us with care and respect.  The atmosphere was one of fine dining and relaxation. All within a reasonable price range.  If you are spending an enjoyable day at the art gallery, I can highly recommend the Tea Room for those breaks in between your viewing pleasure.  Communication from booking a table to requesting feedback on your visit is next level compared to other dining establishments. You will not be disappointed.

Name: JSY Tea Westfield Doncaster
Address: Westfield Shopping Centre, G151/619 Doncaster Rd, Doncaster
Author: hoi yiu wong
Review:
Great shop with great teas! Enjoyed the tea tastings and the experience of watching them brewing the teas in traditional ways.
They do serve very good Matcha and Oolong teas which are my favourites! I ordered their Organic Tea Ceremonial Grade Matcha Latte and it’s the best matcha I’ve ever had…
I tried their new arrival Peach oolong as well, and its natural flavor is just as same as a real peach!

Name: JSY Tea Westfield Doncaster
Address: Westfield Shopping Centre, G151/619 Doncaster Rd, Doncaster
Author: Yao Bob
Review:
I went to meet a friend in Doncaster today and found this JSY TEA very interesting. The environment is great and it's real Chinese tea, not Bubble tea. ordered a hand made brewa tea and a cold brew tea, the taste is great, it's really made from very good tea leaves, not the industrialized product made from tea leaf powder that Bubble tea uses.
The service staff was very nice and introduced a lot of tea knowledge. It is a very interesting store.

Name: JSY Tea Westfield Doncaster
Address: Westfield Shopping Centre, G151/619 Doncaster Rd, Doncaster
Author: nib
Review: Sweet and knowledgeable staff who offer samples and recommendations when you need it :) Pleasant and warm environment with large range of teas and tea-wares to browse and choose from!

Name: JSY Tea Westfield Doncaster
Address: Westfield Shopping Centre, G151/619 Doncaster Rd, Doncaster
Author: Ester Glucose
Review: A very nice Taiwanese tea shop! They have a wide selection of tea, and the staff members have great expertise. The cold brew osmanthus oolong tea for takeaway was incredible, crazy fragrance and tastes great - Would recommend

Name: JSY Tea Westfield Doncaster
Address: Westfield Shopping Centre, G151/619 Doncaster Rd, Doncaster
Author: Y.H. Lin
Review:
Today I randomly wandered on the ground floor and was attracted by the aromatic and adorable decorations of this Taiwanese tea house, JSY Tea. I knew this brand since the Taiwanese director hosts a podcast sharing tea stories.
They currently invite customers to have a free lucky draw upon entry. I got a “Si ji chun Oolong” with a warm blessing “Wishes granted”. The staff invited me to experience the process of tea brewing, explained the tones and benefits of oolong, and let me enjoy the tea with elegant tea set.
As a Taiwanese, I feel they are enthusiastic about bringing authentic and premium quality tea to customers overseas. I enjoyed the moment of tasting my favourite teas and connecting the tastes and flavours to my life experiences…haha that’s right! Plenty choices in JSY Tea provides so many wonderful teas that can challenge taste buds. I would say this is a treasure place for tea lovers:)

Name: Naröcha Premium Tea Room
Author: Rouven Grossmann
Review:
Good place to grab a matcha latte and some sweet treats.
Enjoyed the coconut pandan cake a lot💥💥💥💥💥.
Can’t wait to try the other sweets there.
They also offer a range of cakes and bubble teas.

Name: Naröcha Premium Tea Room
Author: venice min
Review: I can’t believe that can served out to customers. Tasted strong chemicals favored As you can see the photos ！Worst drink! Never ever had in my life. If you can’t make this flavor don’t make it . Disgusting taste

Name: Naröcha Premium Tea Room
Author: Shandy Mae
Review: I was randomly craving bubble tea and ordered at Narocha because of their cakes and variety of bubble tea flavors!!!! They did not disappoint 🫶🏻🫶🏻 My first time ordering here but it certainly won’t be the last. Thank you to the lovely staffs for the sample of your matcha strawberry cake 🍰❤️ It made my day so much better!!

Name: Naröcha Premium Tea Room
Author: Suvarna Lee
Review: This is hands down the best tea place in Melbourne!!! And that’s massive considering I have tried such a large variety of bubble teas! The staff are amazing and their service is just outstanding. The place is large enough to sit down, enjoy your milk or fruit teas and settle into a delicious small cake! Their cakes are fantastic too! The flavours of tea and cakes are endless but I recommend the grape and vanilla, mango and pandan or mango and Thai milk tea cake! Just yummm!

Name: Naröcha Premium Tea Room
Author: Mel K
Review: Such a little gem for anyone who love bubble tea. This unique shop had lots of Japanese variety and Malaysian flavor as well as usual bubble tea varieties. Finally hojicha is on the bubble tea menu. They use A2 milk for their tea, which can suit for everyone. They also made the Japanese branded powder for their Japanese tea. This tea shop menu show luxury feeling with usual price. Love this tea shop so much. Plenty of indoor seating and very spacious. Social distance definitely not an issue at all. Very close to train station and located on the main street. Plenty of parking around. Stuff are very helpful to explain their menu. I definitely will come back for other menu :)

Name: CHOULEE 丘丘茶
Author: Chau Tran
Review: The drinks were great. I’d love to try other drinks at Choulee later. But, the staff members were not friendly and welcoming with a cursoy attitude and no facial expression at all, although it was not busy at that time. I hope the store will take this into consideration and bring the customers better customer experience. ❤️

Name: CHOULEE 丘丘茶
Author: Leonard
Review:
Really fun, clean, and vibrant atmosphere for bubble teas, yogurt drinks and more.
I ordered the people rice yogurt drink which was really good.

Aside from it being a tad bit more sweeter than I prefer and the rice being too small and not as cherry... all was good. I recommend the rice yogurt over Milk Flower place.

Name: CHOULEE 丘丘茶
Author: Prashna Gautam
Review:
I love this place, I've been here thrice. I ordered the passion fruit tea for the first try, which was sincerely my first time drinking a fruit based tea but the only thing I can say about it is that it was very refreshing in the 30 degree weather of Melbourne. The next two times I have ordered the Earl Grey tea, since it's my favourite tea base. I have enjoyed adjusting the sweetness level and the milk as I like less sugar or no sugar in my drink.
I brought a friend of mine along with me, who didn't find boba tea as appealing when we had it in other places but she loved this one. We went there today and I found that the quality of the drinks have been consistent which I have not found in other places, so I like this place + I can sit and talk with my friend while drinking tea. The staff at the front desk was also really friendly and the service overall was great... I'm looking forward to exploring more flavours in the future, I love match and strawberries so maybe that'd be a good one to try...

Name: CHOULEE 丘丘茶
Author: v
Review: visited here multiple times & so far the drinks have always been good quality! store interior is also aesthetic so it’s nice to chill inside. drinks have always been served quite fast too! recommended to try :)

Name: CHOULEE 丘丘茶
Author: Brissy Reviews
Review: Such a cute bubble tea shop. Mu sister and I loved it. It was a bit hard to find using apple maps but that might have been my phone.

Name: Hopetoun Tea Rooms est. 1892 (TM)
Author: Raelyn M
Review:
Enjoy their desserts, have tried most of their cakes. Really good not too sweet and pairs well with tea.

It can be a bit pricey and the space is quite cramped. Hence I think the staff may rush you once you’ve finished your food.

Name: Hopetoun Tea Rooms est. 1892 (TM)
Author: Pri Brabec
Review: The atmosphere, cakes, teas…everything is so delightful. My favourite is the Black Forest cake and Snow White Tea.

Name: Hopetoun Tea Rooms est. 1892 (TM)
Author: KE BILL
Review: A very suitable place for afternoon tea with friends. Although the internet here is not stable, the tea is very drinkable and the cakes are not too sweet.

Name: Hopetoun Tea Rooms est. 1892 (TM)
Author: Ashley Schmidt
Review:
Visited Hopetoun Tea Rooms in Aug 2023 on a friends recommendation.
Awesome visit with the Mrs, great combo of food to have with the must have teas. Such a diverse selection of teas to choose from.
Staff were friendly and very helpful.
Would def recommend this place for a couple to have some chilled time with each other.

Name: Hopetoun Tea Rooms est. 1892 (TM)
Author: Aruneema Das
Review: Its an iconic Melbourne institution located at the Block arcade in cbd. The gorgeous cake display window & the classic decor always lures the crowd for photos and a taste. We had lemon meringue pie & red velvet roulade with coffee, enjoyed every bit of it.

Name: Amazing Graze Tea Rooms
Author: tashstar75
Review: We had a beautiful High Tea this morning! The food was delicious and fresh, so many tea choices (French earl grey is lovely!), and the service was quick and attentive. The venue is very pretty. We really enjoyed our experience, thank you 💖

Name: Amazing Graze Tea Rooms
Author: Justin Hawke
Review:
Possibly the most disappointing high tea I've ever experienced (having had in excess of forty over the past few years). The service was adequate, with food and tea brought promptly (granted, we were the only customers there). The seating and decor are quite lovely, with the scent of flowers wafting from the adjoining flower shop.

However, the main issue - and it's a significant one - is the quality and quantity of the food. For $70 per person, we only received two rather tasteless sandwiches each, a dry bao and a miniscule arancini ball as savouries. Sweets only consisted of four (two each) small macarons. There was also one scone each, which was fine. Needless to say, for the price paid this was an incredibly meagre serving. Would not recommend unless considerable changes were made in this area.

Name: Amazing Graze Tea Rooms
Author: Angela Kliendienst
Review: Gorgeous mini high tea. The staff are super friendly and the food is very tasty. Russell even gave my mum a bouquet after knowing she’s leaving Australia soon. Wonderful experience and will definitely come back.

Name: Amazing Graze Tea Rooms
Author: Jessica W
Review:
Had a reservation booked for 9 people. Was told the night before that the address had changed from Essendon to Ascot Vale. Venue isn't finished, half empty, tradies walking in and out as it is still under construction.

We sat on stained chairs, and at plastic trestle tables with a sheet of foam laid on top, covered with a tablecloth. Bathroom was filthy and had to walk through the construction zone to get there.

Arrived at 10, didn't receive our drinks until after 11.15, didn't receive food until close to 12. We let them know in advance one of our party has allergies, they didn't have food prepared separately for him as they said they would. Even though we paid extra money to ensure he had separate food provided.

Minimal food for our party of 9 and not great quality. The platters came out half empty as they didn't have the scones ready yet. When the scones did come out, they were tiny and we only got 1 each. We all left hungry.

Staff were not apologetic at all, quite rude and defensive.  Extremely overpriced and overhyped experience, definitely won't be back.

Name: Amazing Graze Tea Rooms
Author: Justine Sales
Review: A fabulous space with the most beautiful staff! They made us feel special from the moment we stepped in and made our daughter’s 6th birthday absolutely unforgettable. Thank you Amazing Graze, we look forward to returning ASAP! 🩷

Name: Green Tea House
Author: Andre Medina
Review: bloody amazing. defs not a trap house. if you are looking for traps do not go here. 🐀

Name: Green Tea House
Author: Ravi Newman-Pache
Review: Great tea, amazing location. Truly a secret gem

Name: Green Tea House
Author: Angus “ang” Grant
Review: I don't know this place but it seems ok. 4 stars

Name: Green Tea House
Author: Barnaby Hayward
Review: Just a really great place!

Name: Green Tea House
Author: Katrina KS
Review: Good stock lines

Name: Preston Tea House
Author: Alyson Lima
Review: Amazing

Name: The Tea Rooms 1892
Address: Shops 1 & 2 The Block Arcade, Collins St, Melbourne
Author: Melissa Flowers
Review: Wow such a lovely welcoming and warm Tea House. Friendly professional staff. You are made to feel special from the moment you walk in. So many delicious treats and food to choose from. The tea is served with beautiful tea cups. The history makes it extra special and sends you back in time. We will be back next visit to Melbourne, thank you.

Name: The Tea Rooms 1892
Address: Shops 1 & 2 The Block Arcade, Collins St, Melbourne
Author: Mick, Duck and Bear
Review:
We loved our visit here. We arranged a High Tea for 3.The booking process was very easy with great communication.

Everything was ready for us when we arrived at our scheduled time, with linen table cloths and napkins with a lovely set table including fresh a fresh rose.

The selections of teas is fabulous but they also offer juice, coffee, cold drinks and lemon water.

The high tea stand is packed to the brim with fresh and yummy food, savory, sweet and fruit.  PLUS The fresh scones are amazing. Don't worry though what you don't eat the team package up so you can take home!

The team did an amazing job in checking in and offering their assistance. Super friendly and always willing to go the extra mile.

The rooms are delightful and full of charm. Although in the busy part of Melbourne the rooms are very peaceful and take you back in time.

We loved our time here are highly recommend you book a high tea experience.

Name: The Tea Rooms 1892
Address: Shops 1 & 2 The Block Arcade, Collins St, Melbourne
Author: sakina amani
Review:
This tea room isn’t worth a star. A banana bread and coffee from McDonald is a five star experience. I had a Mango Mousse slice here, yesterday. It was as if I’m eating a mud. The cake wasn’t fresh and looked like it has been stored from days before. I and other customers also saw a fly flying on top of the cakes in the front window, I immediately informed their staff about the matter.
When I provided my feedback about the cake and whatever I heard the day before about their process, today their management called me and in a very rude manner told otherwise - puzzled me who’s right/who’s wrong?
Looks like this tea room love to have insects on top of their cakes and we, the customers are wrong about their cakes and services completely.
Overall, this tea room is totally fake and lacks everything to deserve a good name. High-end name needs advanced effort and professionalism!

Name: The Tea Rooms 1892
Address: Shops 1 & 2 The Block Arcade, Collins St, Melbourne
Author: Rachel Maunder
Review: Arrived Friday mid morning with no reservation and blessed to be accommodated with indoor dining at historical tea rooms in Melbourne. We tried  sample of sandwiches and tea 🫖 to then explore the famous Block Arcade . Stunning decor and love the menu with history of the tea rooms . Especially loved reading a waitress who worked there back in the day was admired and investment of money established her to be first women to create successful cosmetics business, love reading fascinating tidbits of history. 5 stars 🌟 tea rooms

Name: The Tea Rooms 1892
Address: Shops 1 & 2 The Block Arcade, Collins St, Melbourne
Author: Travel Gen
Review:
The Tea Room – Block Arcade, Melbourne

Stepping into The Tea Room in the Block Arcade is like stepping back in time into an era of elegance and charm. This extraordinary shop, dating back to 1892, is a true Melbourne gem that captures the essence of the city’s rich history.

We recently had the pleasure of sharing coffee in this remarkable setting, and it was an experience to remember. The ambience is nothing short of magical, with its ornate décor, high ceilings, and a timeless atmosphere that transports you to another world. It’s clear that The Tea Room has preserved its historical roots while offering a warm and welcoming space for visitors.
