Review: Black Sugar Pearl Fresh Milk and Golden Passionfruit Green Tea so fresh and full of flavour!

Name: Gotcha Fresh Tea - Watergardens
Author: Anisa Anwar
Review: Great drinks, lovely staff. I had the lychee green tea and thoroughly enjoyed it, will definitely be trying other flavors next time!

Name: Popping & Tea Deer Park
Author: Mary Jade
Review: Favourite Bubble Tea place by far, my kids love it too. Great flavour, not too much ice. Fave is the coconut milk tea. Is definitely a must!! Always greeted by friendly and lovely staff. Fast and efficient service.

Name: Popping & Tea Deer Park
Author: Lanni
Review:
Been here about 3 to 4 times.

Last time the oolong milk tea made was rich however tonight the oolong milk tea I ordered tasted soooo watery. I can’t taste the tea properly.

I’d recommend to train your staff to make the milk tea ratio correctly.

Name: Popping & Tea Deer Park
Author: Taliah Yasmine
Review: My favourite boba shop and franchise in all of Melbourne. While it is a little more expensive than your other popular bubble tea shops around, their handmade boba pearls and premium taste in their drinks totally makes it worth the price. The staff are always friendly, greeting you every time you walk in and when you leave. I highly recommend to treat yourself to Popping and Tea boba if you ever walk past one!

Name: Popping & Tea Deer Park
Author: jian
Review: this review is written out of anger but I ordered drinks for my group of friends and I and they didn’t put any straws in the bag? We all had boba so we can’t drink with regular straw! Honestly so annoying!

Name: Popping & Tea Deer Park
Author: Andrea Tran
Review: I alway been a fan of jasmine milktea and strawberry green tea from popping & tea 😭😭💕💕💕 their handmade pearl alway got me melted with its chewy and sweet taste. If you have a chance please come to the shop. The staff was super friendly and nice. They offer free upside when u order directly from the store from mon to fri toooo :33 come here once and im sure you gonna crave for moreeee ❤️❤️❤️

Name: 御黑堂The Yunique Tea Docklands
Author: GossipGirl XOXO
Review:
updated:
I had a bubble tea today. The tapioca pearls were still hard. Could not even be chewed!

This is the last time to try!

——

The tapioca pearls are way too hard.
Very average.
3 stars are for the staff, they are very nice (sorry I have to downgrade the review from 3 to 1 star!)

Name: 御黑堂The Yunique Tea Docklands
Author: Christina Dang
Review:
I decided to try Yunique for the first time and ordered five drinks. To my surprise, the employee told me it was too much. When I asked what she meant, she reiterated that it was too much. I was left confused—I’ve never been to a place where ordering their products was considered excessive.

The employee got visibly stressed and annoyed by my order. It’s crazy to me because I’m supporting the business and giving them money. While she did take my payment, her attitude and the level of customer service I received were unlike anything I’ve ever experienced. The drinks were okay, but not the best. I will not be coming back. It seems like they’re just open for decoration, not to actually take orders or sell stuff.

Name: 御黑堂The Yunique Tea Docklands
Author: 樱木花
Review:
Bubble tea - black sugar Pearl with matcha.
Always wanting to try out new bubble tea.
This is yet another one. Hidden in the mall but would see it if you go to Woolworths.
Little pricey but you get to decide sugar level which is sometimes rare with other bubble tea store. But less ice ended up with no ice and not cold enough.  Pearls are Q… and tasty though!!!!

Name: 御黑堂The Yunique Tea Docklands
Author: Sreerag Nampoothiri K
Review: I think it’s safe to say, this is the only bubble tea place in docklands. Located in the close proximity of the mall, makes it an easy choice to go for and to have something to sip while you take a walk around. The lady at the store was extremely polite and she was very friendly. I have tried the Brown Sugar Milk tea and the pearls were very nice.

Name: 御黑堂The Yunique Tea Docklands
Author: Ashley Jenny W
Review:
Tried this cute bubble tea shop for the first time since it's nearby, and it didn't disappoint! The pearls were fresh and just the right amount of chewy. The milk tea was yummy too! Didn't love the grapefruit tea as much as the syrup tasted too artificial for my liking. But will definitely be back to try their other fruit flavours!

The staff were friendly, quick and professional too! Here's hoping them all the best - this side of Docklands needs its own staple bubble tea shop!

Name: Boba Tea
Author: F Oeij
Review: Very good Vietnamese coffee.. perfect place sitting outdoor with Vietnamese ice coffee and ciggies

Name: Boba Tea
Author: Sa P
Review: some pretty standard boba, defs not gongcha or share tea standard but decent. Its $5

Name: Boba Tea
Author: Judith
Review: My ultimate favourite Milk tea spot! They nail it every time with my milk tea flavours, and all their other items they sell here! Love the service! If I could I’d come here everyday!

Name: Boba Tea
Author: Ange
Review: Ordered a $8 half ice sugar cane juice which turned out to be full of ice and tasted like kumquat juice. Totally unacceptable!!!

Name: Boba Tea
Author: Guantong Wo
Review: love this place. it's quite and peaceful after 6pm, you can grab a drink and enjoy your company.

Name: Tas Tea Bubbles
Author: belle
Review: Drinks are always made fresh to order, love coming here! The owner is always so friendly. Definitely recommend the Taro and Avocado coconut milk :)

Name: Tas Tea Bubbles
Author: Human
Review:
Excellent brown sugar milk tea with creme Broulee. The pearls were also extremely soft and nice, compared to most bubble tea places.

Super friendly owner as well!

Name: Tas Tea Bubbles
Author: Z H
Review: Honestly a great place, the owner was super lovely and the drink was really good. he was very professional and when i mentioned that i wasn’t a fan of sweet drinks, even though i forgot to ask for less sugar he automatically did it and got me to see if i liked it. which i really appreciated as it was perfect! highly recommend :))

Name: Tas Tea Bubbles
Author: Angela Roybal
Review:
Drinks are made fresh and are absolutely delicious! The couple who run the shop are so lovely, if you’re in the area or a local, this should definitely be your go to boba shop!!

You may need to wait a little but what’s a little wait time compared to a delicious drink? Come visit ASAP

I always order their fresh Taro Milk Tea but all their drinks are great

Name: Tas Tea Bubbles
Author: cheung yat ming
Review: We tried their taro pearl milk tea and cannot forget how tasty it is, we bought a pearl milk tea from other shop at the same time to compare, and we definitely can say this shop has the best taro milk tea which is made from real taro, you can definitely taste the freshness and rich in flavor.

Name: Mr.Panda Bubble Tea
Address: Cranbourne Park Shopping Center Food court (SP005, Cranbourne
Author: Adeline
Review:
Been coming here since it first opened!

The atmosphere of the shopped has improved since! Seating is available if you wish to sit inside, self serve board is readily accessible and of course when you can’t decide what bubble tea, smoothie/frappes and egg waffles you want… their are plenty of posters and menus to help you.

The staff are always super friendly and do their absolute best to make the best drinks to your liking.

100% recommend over chatime.

Name: Mr.Panda Bubble Tea
Address: Cranbourne Park Shopping Center Food court (SP005, Cranbourne
Author: Nixxy Chan
Review: I love the way they have done up the place and the menu was so cute! The drink was really good, I loved the packaging too. I would recommend!

Name: Mr.Panda Bubble Tea
Address: Cranbourne Park Shopping Center Food court (SP005, Cranbourne
Author: Tionne Lee Driscoll Bartel
Review: Omg I love panda tea especially the mouse cake range so delicious! Definitely try it out - friendly staff also at Cranbourne Park :)

Name: Mr.Panda Bubble Tea
Address: Cranbourne Park Shopping Center Food court (SP005, Cranbourne
Author: Lanny kurnia
Review:
I really love it . So yummy and Tasty ;)
Good price ;) good service ;) highly recommanded ❤️

Name: Mr.Panda Bubble Tea
Address: Cranbourne Park Shopping Center Food court (SP005, Cranbourne
Author: yuan wei
Review: Best bubble tea in Cranbourne Park. The lady serviced us is friendly. Highly recommend!

Name: Comebuytea Melbourne Central
Address: Melbourne Central Lower Ground, 32 Swanston St, Melbourne
Author: Huoy
Review: First I try brown sugar here. It tastes very different from other milk tea shop. The milk tea isn’t sweet at all and pearl is soft which is quite nice. I never taste kind of this texture before. Recommend to try this one. Atmosphere is good and enjoyable. Cashier is friendly. Give a try of number 5 brown sugar milk tea and u will know

Name: Comebuytea Melbourne Central
Address: Melbourne Central Lower Ground, 32 Swanston St, Melbourne
Author: shaira noshin
Review: I am not a tea person but what sets this place apart for me is the freshly brewed tea which is shaken by hand. Kind of fancy but worth the price through their app. They are between pop mart and laser clinic, opposite to St station. Give them a try and hope you like it

Name: Comebuytea Melbourne Central
Address: Melbourne Central Lower Ground, 32 Swanston St, Melbourne
Author: Sandeep Singh Bindra
Review:
COMEBUYTEA at Melbourne Central is a good place for Tea lovers.
Though a small seating place but is really nice and good .
Price $17.30 for both .
We tried Mango Oolong Tea and Brown Sugar Creamy  Milk Tea
Both Tea tasted good.
Must try .

Name: Comebuytea Melbourne Central
Address: Melbourne Central Lower Ground, 32 Swanston St, Melbourne
Author: Ralphy
Review: The warm matcha latte with kanten crystals always hit the spot on a cold winters day. The queue goes down past 1:30-2pm, but make sure to make it there early because it shuts early. 🍵

Name: Comebuytea Melbourne Central
Address: Melbourne Central Lower Ground, 32 Swanston St, Melbourne
Author: Alina Bugge
Review: I got the Brown Sugar Creamy Milk Black Tea, probably the best bubble tea I ever had. There is a bit of a waiting time but it’s absolutely worth it. Staff was friendly as well!

Name: T2 Tea - 269 Little Collins
Author: Ruth Natalia
Review:
Lovely place, nice tea. Lovely gifts.

but the fat lady serving me on 31 DEC at 2:25 pm while trying to get melbourne breakfast tea was pretty effing rude and not helpful at all. probably because she’s fat, unhappy, lonely and had to work at end of year. really ruined my shopping experience.

Name: T2 Tea - 269 Little Collins
Author: cherry y
Review:
I visited this place to buy a present because of my friend's recommendation.
The interior is full of various teas and teacups, and it is very beautiful. The staff were friendly. I'm going to revisit soon and buy a cup for myself.

Name: T2 Tea - 269 Little Collins
Author: Mc Melon
Review:
Wow what an amazing little shop. Best of all the t2 shops. Very Unique.
Massive Thanks to Zoe for her excellent service and knowledge. What a lovely person. Thankyou

Name: T2 Tea - 269 Little Collins
Author: Neil
Review:
Exceptional Service and Kindness from Zoe at T2 Tea

I had the pleasure of receiving exceptional service from Zoe at T2 Tea. When two of my tea pot lids broke, Zoe went above and beyond to assist me. She not only helped me find the two replacement lids I needed but also provided them to me free of charge. Additionally, she took the extra step to carefully wrap the lids in packaging paper. I am extremely satisfied with her service and give her a five-star rating. I will definitely be returning to T2 for future purchases.

Name: T2 Tea - 269 Little Collins
Author: Aleena Smith
Review: Amazing store!!! I went in thinking I'd never find my favorite tea again and after a short description Zoe had it in my hand and a big smile on my face. Amazing service, Zoe was warm, inviting and incredibly friendly, I would go back just for the atmosphere she created. Would definitely recommend.

Name: Gong cha Elizabeth St
Author: Roongrawee Eabsakul
Review:
Order 3 items, then got only 2???? The funny thing is both 2 items that I've got are ALL WRONG. Moreover, I cannot contact the restaurant through the phone. Bravo!!! This branch is the NEXT LEVEL!!!! Try order something if you want your order to be randomised and cost more than what you'll get :)

Gong Cha, you better to your work not just sleep on it 🤯!

Name: Gong cha Elizabeth St
Author: Nawar Arnaout
Review: We got multiple boba flavors as I was with family. The drinks were nice and tasty as classics would usually be. Service was pretty quick and friendly. My only criticism would be having the menu only in front of the cashier hidden from the rest. We spent a while picking a drink because we had to wait out turn to see the menu. I suggest displaying the entire menus on the monitor screens on top so you can decide before it’s you’re turn to order.

Name: Gong cha Elizabeth St
Author: Kieren Watkins
Review: The taro flavour was gritty, like they had made it from a powder that hasn't dissolved properly. The toppings were good though. The self-ordering system with the huge display was great though, but it should estimate the wait time for your order; we were waiting there unexpectedly for 15 minutes with no place to sit.

Name: Gong cha Elizabeth St
Author: Thanya Thanyarin
Review: Gong cha is good, sometimes queue can get really long so do keep track of ur time!

Name: Gong cha Elizabeth St
Author: Ngan Le
Review:
I ordered 2 drinks for me and my boyfriend on october 6th around 11pm to 11.30pm.
The female staff made my drink wrong and instead of making another new one she poured out the wrong milk tea, washed the plastic cup with dirty hands and poured new milk tea in. What the hell is she doing ???
The owner or manager please should review the camera and instruct the staff on food safety and hygiene. 😶🙏🏻

Name: 茶屋
Author: Laurence Jackson
Review: Visited for the Princess Diana exhibit. What a great place!

Name: 茶屋
Author: Kylie Hull
Review:
Attended venue to see Princess Di Exhibition. Male staff very friendly upon arrival.  Showed me around. Plenty of seats and fans to keep us all cool. Bathrooms very clean
Close to town centre. Public transport right to Door. Worth 1000 stars. Highly recommended

Name: 茶屋
Author: Sue Hampson
Review: Went there this morning to see the Princess Diana exhibition, with a ticket I’d bought through Fever. When I got there the front door was locked but there was a woman on the phone at the front desk. She indicated she would open the door after the phone call. I waited several minutes, then realised when I checked the Fever site that my ticket had been cancelled. So I left, as there was certainly no point in waiting for the woman on the phone- she was in no hurry to help. I hope I get my money back….

Name: 茶屋
Author: Belinda Paterson
Review: Loved the building.

Name: 茶屋
Author: Lim Kian Ming
Review: Move Oolong. No tea to see here.

Name: Black And White Milk Tea
Author: ThuyVan Mai
Review: I don’t usually drink milk tea due to sugar restrictions but I was gift to try it out and I must admit it was perfectly form and sugar lever was perfect, all I can say is addictive, the toppings were amazingly tasty. Highly recommend!!! You must try but be beware you be back for more. Which happens to me. Decent price too for a large cup, very friend service :) thank anh

Name: Black And White Milk Tea
Author: Miss Minchin
Review:
A tiny shop in Big Sam’s Market.

Ordered Taro Milktea ($5) and Brown Sugar Milktea ($6).

Taro was really nice and had so many toppings which were delicious. Brown Sugar was lacking taste so we didn’t enjoy it.

Overall good value as it is a big cup for cheap. Order was served almost instantly!

Name: Black And White Milk Tea
Author: John Moore
Review: Been here multiple times and had no issues with excellent milk tea that the usual ladies made. Came today, and was served by an older man and asked for no ice for me and another, made myself clear multiple times that I do not want ice, and he put ice in each cup. Another employee helped him to understand that I did not want ice. So the older man went to the back and grabbed it out with his unwashed hands and brought the cup back to continue with the drink. It was absolutely unhygienic.

Name: Black And White Milk Tea
Author: Et Cetera Melbourne
Review: I literally drive 30 mins out of my way just to get the salted cream coffee, hands down the best in melbourne.

Name: Black And White Milk Tea
Author: Vanessa DM
Review:
How did just discover this place now!?
The toppings are AMAZING! My favorite was the chestnut and the cream cheese jelly.  The herbal and coffee jelly were very flavorful, too!
The amount of toppings served is the winner! Large milk tea with all the beautiful toppings for $5 only, so cheap! This is the best tasting, best value milk tea in St Albans. Not getting $7-8 milk tea from Gong-cha anymore.
The original milk tea is a must try - it's like the Filipino version of halo-halo but in the form of a milk tea.
Huge plus is that the store is environmentally conscious with their packaging.
Highly recommended, staff is amazing!

Name: MOMO TEA (Burwood)
Author: Rachel Tang
Review: pearls are hard, egg puff is 7.5$ but not sweet at all, very plain while other egg puffs at other places are sweet.

Name: MOMO TEA (Burwood)
Author: Mia
Review: Lovely service, sadly no aloe vera :( but alas, perfectly good bubble tea, just what you would expect from a bubble tea shop, but the hours are amazing!!! Being open so late is great for students

Name: MOMO TEA (Burwood)
Author: Andrew W
Review:
01/2023

Ordered a $8.20 L size Black Sugar Milk Tea with The Lot (pearls, cheese foam, Oreo pieces) and it was perfectly balanced :) Discovered they are on the MemberBuy app and got 25% earning credits too ^_^

I really hope they get more customers because this shop is really good.

3/2/2024 - Seems like under new management the past few months, and has improved the decor a bit. Using 10% cashback on Shopback app my tracked spend has been $250 here per 6 months 🫣

Name: MOMO TEA (Burwood)
Author: Yohan Pasqual
Review: Great bubble tea, friendly and helpful staff.

Name: MOMO TEA (Burwood)
Author: darker0726
Review:
Shows open on Sunday but actually not.
Waste times. Just saw another two girls drop by too.

Ware time

Name: Tea More
Author: 樱木花
Review:
Sorry but both tea - peach flavoured oolong and jasmine green tea has no tea taste at all.
Staff was poker face. Doesn’t ask if he didn’t understand us.
We actually wanted the tea with no milk or cheese. Just tea with pearls.
Not really a comfortable internet bar as well.

Name: Tea More
Author: Cauliflower tsai
Review:
I used the delivery platform to purchase, and with discounts, I purchased three drinks for only $5.7, which is great value for money! The drink is not bad.
It is recommended not to order less than 50% of the sugar content, otherwise it will not taste good😜

Name: Tea More
Author: James K
Review: Very good environment, great service, and friendly staffs. They don’t speak English, but man I love how kind they are to those who don’t speak Chinese. They also have fantastic drinks, and snacks! Awesome place 🤙🏻👌🏻

Name: Tea More
Author: Tony
Review:
So this little bubble tea place is a part of an internet cafe, which is fine, they open long hours as a result, and that helps.

The offers are very limited, but what they do offer is actually quite decent.

The prices are really great, so thumbs up 👍 there.

I wish they'd spend a bit more effort on this side business of theirs and make a few more varieties, but obviously it's none of my business to interfere with that. But one can wish.

Name: Tea More
Author: MDOG
Review: Does not get better than this place when it comes to bubble tea I remember first trying a drink from here in August 2021 and always come back to relive that time  ❤️

Name: Charlie’s Tea
Author: Shellie Vu
Review: I drink milk tea here very often. There is always a long line, but the staff prepares it quite quickly. So it doesn't take much time to queue. The best is still the peach oolong with osmanthus jelly. The tea is strong and fragrant, but doesn't cause insomnia. The milk is fatty and sweet. The cream on top is also very delicious.

Name: Charlie’s Tea
Author: Noey Natnaree T.
Review:
Everything was written in mandarin, less in English which I think it was quite a bit overwhelming. The tea itself was strong in the taste, less sweet and a bit diluted on the creamy taste if you know what I mean ( in my opinion they are more like Chinese style milk tea, not Taiwanese style which they more creamier and sweeter)
The price is quite high, staff are ok. Will be more nicer if they are more friendly for English speakers.

Name: Charlie’s Tea
Author: Ayu
Review:
Great fresh milk tea. Recently they have changed into a flat straw, which is designed to help you taste the tea flavor better. In my opinion it also makes the boba lasts longer 🤭. Milk tea has been really good.

It is a very small store with no seating space at all, but the pick up table area has not been cleaned properly, always got trash and spots of cream on it.

Customer service: there is a specific staff that has been quite rude when taking orders. It’s the one that the other comments have been mentioning. Please train the staff properly. There’s no greetings and they just assumed that I speak Chinese so they just started talking to me in Chinese without me saying anything. This happened multiple times with multiple staffs.

Overall, the milk teas have been great, reasonable price, but the customer service needs to be improved.

Name: Charlie’s Tea
Author: Martin Hhyieu
Review: I ordered their best seller, the Ceylon black tea with pecan cream top. It was good, the hot drink was especially enjoyable during the cold winter season. Despite the long lines, orders moved quickly, which was convenient.

Name: Charlie’s Tea
Author: Mw Mi
Review:
Tried the number 1 Pecan Black Tea with peach whipped foam as their bestseller and recommendation...
And its so good.
They give u spoon to eat whipped cream with the pecan, and itsss good! The tea is clear taste, refreshing and certainly comes from a very good leaf.

Their service topnotch, efficient and super fast

Name: Gong cha State Library
Author: Jessica NGUYEN
Review: Very bad customer service! The male cashier I encountered today was very rude. I just wanted a cup of milk tea without ice because I was going to drink it after a few hours, he shouted at me "NO CHANGE NO CHANGE". I'm not asking for a free drink, I might even be willing to pay extra (although that's a free choice for custom your cup). I changed to another type then the female employee mistakenly made it with ice and she tried to remove all the ice and add more tea. I received a cup that was as bland as water. No second time

Name: Gong cha State Library
Author: Olga O
Review: Bubble tea is another obsession I picked up throughout my time in Melbourne. Gong Cha has been our go to place. There are many other bubble tea place in town. But Gong Cha is the most consistent one which delivers delicious beverages day in and day out. My favourite is still the classic pearl milk tea. I also like to add aloe vera or coconut jelly. We go there almost every week. I used to love boost, but now Gong Cha has taken its place.

Name: Gong cha State Library
Author: Piggy Eatalot
Review: 2.5 Star ⭐️ I used to like Gong Cha but I wondered if they have changed the formula to the taro milk tea in the last 6 months?  They used to use more fresh taro and I just find that this time it tasted a bit fake and the colour is more purple compared to last time I drank it... then again I did drink it in another store...  Thank goodness when I asked for a little sweet they really did give me just a little sweet, so it’s just the right sweetness.  I must try another Gong cha outlet to compare cos they used to make such good taro milk tea!

Name: Gong cha State Library
Author: Kim Vu
Review:
Very bad customer service and bad drink!
The male cashier's attitude was very unpleasant. Unprofessional and dirty female bartender. The drink is not like gongcha, the glass doesn't close.
I'm not here for a free drink!!!!!!

Name: Gong cha State Library
Author: Terry Reidy
Review: Good reliable gong cha bubble tea. Just like all their stores you will get exactly what you ordered. Consistent taste good quality and efficient service. This particular culture seems a little bit quieter than the others in the CBD so it's a good option if you're in a rush.

Name: Top Tea
Author: Thanh Hảo Lê
Review:
While it wasn't bad, it wasn't quite the bubble tea revelation I was hoping for. They have a huge selection of flavours and toppings, which is definitely a plus. I went for a classic brown sugar milk tea with tapioca pearls, and the tea itself was good - strong and flavourful. The pearls were a bit on the chewier side than I like, but that's a personal preference.

One thing that surprised me was the atmosphere. The place was clean and bright, but it felt more like a grab-and-go coffee shop than a place to relax and enjoy a drink. The music was loud, and the seating was a bit cramped.

Overall, Top Tea is a decent option for a quick bubble tea fix. If you're looking for a place to catch up with friends and chat, there might be better options out there.

Name: Top Tea
Author: Czarina Buenaventura
Review: Great Brulee milk tea, as well as the hand-peeled pomelo but the price for the latter is quite exorbitant. I love the chewiness of the pearl and taste of their drinks. Store is always crowded but the staff moves quickly to get the orders out. The paper bag for two drinks or more is appreciated so I can lug the drinks back safely to my hotel.

Name: Top Tea
Author: Ζ L
Review: I got the creme brulee trea with pearls. It was an okay bubble tea. But, for a 30 minute wait, it does not seem worth it.

Name: Top Tea
Author: Trang Nguyen
Review:
Popular tea shop. Got queues most of the time so you need to wait for like 15-20 mins to order and to get your drinks.
Tried brulee milk tea with extra cheese foam. It was just so so, nothing special. The milk tea is no different to homemade milktea. Easily find better milktea somewhere else. Havent tried their fruit teas yet, hope they are better.

Name: Top Tea
Author: Brian Duy Nguyen
Review: I recently visited Top Tea Melbourne and had a fantastic experience. The tea selection was extensive and the staff were knowledgeable and passionate about tea. The atmosphere was cozy and inviting, and the decor was beautiful. I ordered a pot of their signature blend and it was absolutely delicious. The tea was brewed to perfection and served in beautiful teaware. I also tried some of their desserts and they were heavenly - the perfect complement to the tea. Overall, I highly recommend Top Tea Melbourne to anyone looking for a high-quality tea experience. I can't wait to visit again and try more of their blends!

Name: Top Tea - Clayton
Author: Nic
Review:
I'm pretty sure I got dishwater and not tea.
I am not a robot this is a real review. Worst tea I have had in my life. I don't know why there were so many people inside waiting.

Name: Top Tea - Clayton
Author: Noland Hoang
Review: Best oreo cheese brulee milk tea ever, no one can beat them for real. However the brulee matcha is not consistent, its usually not as creamy and thick as in the city. Love their pearls and milk mochi. I wish top tea had a loyalty card. Also wish they had the mochi bread they sell in the city as well.

Name: Top Tea - Clayton
Author: Rebecca LI
Review: Super friendly staff Vanessa - always passion and smiling whenever we visit, makes me happy whenever I see her in store. I’m very appreciating her enthusiasm, this is the best customer service I can ever experience in any shops like this. There are many times when we waiting for the drinks to be ready, I could see Vanessa is always being so kind and respectful to all customer. Thanks for always making my day extra happy despite the great drinks too.

Name: Top Tea - Clayton
Author: Oscar Riches
Review: Is okay, lots of fruit teas and specials. Nice aesthetic and style and open "kitchen"

Name: Top Tea - Clayton
Author: Soohyun Lanna Ro
Review: The latest menu. Top tea is all time favourite

Name: Kallista Tea Rooms
Author: Amy Hicks
Review: Beautiful view, loved watching the birds in the tree. The customer service was fantastic!! I loved the scones with jam and cream. Some of the savoury items could have been better and the sweet tarts tasted like the tart crust was bought but the fillings were delicious. It got a bit noisy a bit later on but it was the school holidays.

Name: Kallista Tea Rooms
Author: Valyn White
Review: Visited Sep 2024 for late afternoon tea. Staff extremely welcoming and service was great. Food slightly slow to come out but it was busy so, to be expected. Kids loved sitting by the windows with the cockatoos sitting outside less than a meter away. We had the calamari salad, some pumpkin soup, a ham and cheese foccacia, chips, scones and some cake. Everything was exceptionally well prepared.

Name: Kallista Tea Rooms
Author: Nicole Block
Review: Went for Friday afternoon scones, jam and cream. Wasn’t busy as it is winter. Needed to get up and get our own menus. Service was just ok. Scones were big and tasty, although a little dry. Apple crumble was nice, but was barely warm. Lots of easy parking.

Name: Kallista Tea Rooms
Author: Vivian Phan
Review:
Kallista tea rooms kindly accommodated our decently sized (6 people) group as walk-ins on a rainy cold day. But it is recommended to book we just got lucky!

Service was a bit slow but we weren't in a rush and they did get slammed when we were there.

Everything was a good sized portion. The scones were huge and had good flavour but did have that chalkiness at the end. Good amount of jam and cream provided. Creamy delicious hot chocolate.

There is parking down the hill but it can be hard to traverse if it's very muddy.

Name: Kallista Tea Rooms
Author: Bella B
Review: Visited 05.08.2024. Beautiful place with a vintage setting. Love that feeling of country style. The staff are friendly and they service is great. Plenty of parking. There's even a playful area for the kids. Food is delicious.

Name: Top Tea
Author: Ardelia Riza Artanti
Review: Love love love the brulee milk tea with pearl. The milk tea itself was $5.9 and the pearl was $1, so total was $6.9, which I think is well priced. Size is perfect, not too big and not too small. Line was short too, didnt wait long for my boba. Def one of my fav boba in Melbourne. Got this cute bag too! :3

Name: Top Tea
Author: Michelle Ou
Review:
This is one of my favourite boba shops! I always visit when I fly to Melbourne 💖

The grape cheese tea was a blend of sweet and creamy notes 💚 The longan and coconut fruit tea was a refreshing tropical delight 🫶 I love how they use real fresh fruit!

Visited during the Easter holiday and ordered the Easter cake. It was not only cute but also tasty, with the subtle tea flavour 🐰🍵

Name: Top Tea
Author: Oscar
Review: This bubble tea shop certainly sets itself apart from others in terms of its distinctiveness. The packaging is impressively well-crafted, giving off a premium vibe that comes with a higher price tag for both the cakes and drinks. While the cakes may appear visually appealing, their taste doesn't quite match my expectation. However, if you have a sweet tooth and enjoy sugary treats, this is a place worth exploring.

Name: Top Tea
Author: Nawar Arnaout
Review: The drinks are a bit on the more expensive side however they are nice. They have a variety of different flavors that aren’t typically found in other boba shops. The service was quick but hygiene standards are a bit disappointing.

Name: Top Tea
Author: Zeek
Review: Very good variation of drinks, even for people who don't like bubble tea would be able to find something they like here. Recommend the osmanthus milk tea. Keep in mind that there's usually a long wait and they have 15% weekend surcharge for fairly pricey drinks

Name: Hopetoun Tea Rooms est. 1892 (TM)
Author: Raelyn M
Review:
Enjoy their desserts, have tried most of their cakes. Really good not too sweet and pairs well with tea.

It can be a bit pricey and the space is quite cramped. Hence I think the staff may rush you once you’ve finished your food.

Name: Hopetoun Tea Rooms est. 1892 (TM)
Author: Pri Brabec
Review: The atmosphere, cakes, teas…everything is so delightful. My favourite is the Black Forest cake and Snow White Tea.

Name: Hopetoun Tea Rooms est. 1892 (TM)
Author: KE BILL
Review: A very suitable place for afternoon tea with friends. Although the internet here is not stable, the tea is very drinkable and the cakes are not too sweet.

Name: Hopetoun Tea Rooms est. 1892 (TM)
Author: Ashley Schmidt
Review:
Visited Hopetoun Tea Rooms in Aug 2023 on a friends recommendation.
Awesome visit with the Mrs, great combo of food to have with the must have teas. Such a diverse selection of teas to choose from.
Staff were friendly and very helpful.
Would def recommend this place for a couple to have some chilled time with each other.

Name: Hopetoun Tea Rooms est. 1892 (TM)
Author: Aruneema Das
Review: Its an iconic Melbourne institution located at the Block arcade in cbd. The gorgeous cake display window & the classic decor always lures the crowd for photos and a taste. We had lemon meringue pie & red velvet roulade with coffee, enjoyed every bit of it.

Name: Gotcha Fresh Tea - St Albans
Author: Miss Minchin
Review:
Clean and well maintained shop. Not a very long wait to get your order. They also have their Top 10 list plastered.

When I first tried Gotcha, I didn’t like their Pearl Milk Tea (but this was ordered from Melbourne CBD before) so I was hesitant to try Gotcha in St Albans. But their shop is too cute so I decided to go in and try their Top 1, which is the Black Sugar Pearl Milk Tea. I loved it—pearls were cooked perfectly but they could add more sugar.

Will go back to try other drinks!

Name: Gotcha Fresh Tea - St Albans
Author: Mel B
Review:
Overall: 3.5/5

Quick and efficient service. Milk tea for $9 is outrageous but can be reasoned by their crazy and innovative concoctions - from milk tea, fruit teas with cheese foams, to yoghurts and very odd yet yummy drinks, your options are endless!

We each loved our drink:
Black sugar milk tea (3.5/5): classic milk tea. Not the best tasting, but the pearls were super soft and bouncy.

Chrysanthemum milk tea with lychee jelly (4/5): not too sweet since we asked for less sugar, and very refreshing!

Mango yoghurt popping pearls (4/5): breakfast but in a better form. Popping pearl gave it that fun element and a pop of sour and sweet, and yoghurt balanced that sour kick to it, whilst still tasting the mango. However, wish the authentic mango flavour was more pronounced.

Strawberry macchiato (4.5/5): refreshing and creamy! Tastes like strawberries and cream.

Taste: 4
Value: 3
Ambience: 3
Service: 4

Name: Gotcha Fresh Tea - St Albans
Author: Phung Truong
Review: The drinks come in two size, medium and large. The drinks taste great, they have a big variety of drinks in-store. However, service is a little slow.

Name: Gotcha Fresh Tea - St Albans
Author: Helen Chan
Review:
Thai Milk Tea, large, no ice. Quality and taste were on point. Although disappointing to have about 1/3 of the cup being air.
*Just noticed second photo looks like it’s been pierced, but photos are of it being unopened.

Name: Gotcha Fresh Tea - St Albans
Author: Jessica Huynh
Review: I brought 3x Brown sugar fresh milk tea. I can't see the tea or brown sugar. All I see is milk.

Name: Pearl Tea Sanctuary Lakes
Address: Sanctuary Lakes Shopping Center, Kiosk 7/300 Point Cook Rd, Point Cook
Author: Alex T
Review: One of the Best bubble tea spots in the West. Drinks always deliver and is great for value. My personal favorite is the Winter Melon black tea you can't go wrong. Great customer service too, I would definitely recommend.

Name: Pearl Tea Sanctuary Lakes
Address: Sanctuary Lakes Shopping Center, Kiosk 7/300 Point Cook Rd, Point Cook
Author: Food and Travel
Review: Pearl Tea is a newly opened bubble tea store in Sanctuary Lakes, the menu has some unique items but is also very limited, they have a good range of toppings such as Mini Taro Balls and white pearls, however their drinks range is limited, the honeydew tasted weird and nothing like honeydew, however the tea taste was strong. A good pro was the fact they provide a mini plastic knife to open the seal in order to drink the cheese foam. Overall a good experience with nothing special.

Name: Pearl Tea Sanctuary Lakes
Address: Sanctuary Lakes Shopping Center, Kiosk 7/300 Point Cook Rd, Point Cook
Author: Hymns Wong
Review: Pearl Tea is my go to for fresh teas in Sanctuary Lakes! Teas are always chilled, delicious and fresh and shop is clean and welcoming. My favorite is the Citrus Punch. Best drink for a summer day! Highly Recommended!!

Name: Pearl Tea Sanctuary Lakes
Address: Sanctuary Lakes Shopping Center, Kiosk 7/300 Point Cook Rd, Point Cook
Author: Afaque Chivelkar
Review:
Nice some options. Not to overly complicated with menu but what they sell is aboalutely perfect and consistent. I wasn't a fan of bubble tea but now sucked into the Cheesy range. Also the son asks them to mix a few options and they happy to do and recommend changes.

In photo -
2 x Mango Spoilers
1 x Cheesy Lychee
1 x Cheesy Watermelon

Name: Pearl Tea Sanctuary Lakes
Address: Sanctuary Lakes Shopping Center, Kiosk 7/300 Point Cook Rd, Point Cook
Author: Rupinder Sachdeva
Review: I enjoyed my drink it was really yummy . I have been here many time and had a very good drink.  the drink was really good and i enjoyed.

Name: Fu CHA TEA SHOP 福茶社
Author: Neeta
Review: Jasmine milk tea is one of the best I've tried! Perfect at 50% sugar. Menu is half handwritten and shop gives home-made vibes. Keen to try more of their milk tea flavours. Would be nice to have more seating available as it's more of a takeaway joint. Packaging and takeaway bag has a more premium feel than expected.

Name: Fu CHA TEA SHOP 福茶社
Author: Positive Man
Review: I go there regularly since it is close to my work especially in summer. My favorite is super grape without milk foam and my husband favourite super grape with milk foam. They use real grapes unlike other place using serum only.You can pick different toppings and sugar level to suit your taste. Owner is nice and friendly. He made amazing tea 👍🏻

Name: Fu CHA TEA SHOP 福茶社
Author: Chayan Ray
Review:
Defs some of the best bubble tea in the area. There are lots of spots in Glenny but this one is the best.

Taro milk tea with crème brûlée is our favourite. Matcha flavours are all good, watermelon is great. Toppings are well made and not too sweet.

Service is always quick, even when the line is long they’re pretty fast.

Open late and always worth a visit.

Name: Fu CHA TEA SHOP 福茶社
Author: Zoe Lewis
Review: Best bubble tea I've ever had. Staff are nice and get orders through as quickly as possible but it's a small shop so it can take a minute when it's busy. Amazing range of drinks, some of the fruit teas are especially incredible. The pinky lychee is unmatched.

Name: Fu CHA TEA SHOP 福茶社
Author: Hannah wang
Review:
I love Fu Cha
when I come to glen
I always get a drink from here
Very strong tea and fresh fruit
I love Yakult series they make by Yakult .not the syrup
I love their cheese from they made by cream cheese not the powder or any essence

Last month I try the king durian
I talk with the staff and see they make by fresh smash durian not the durian ice cream !!
I enjoy everything from Fu Cha
Genuine goods at a fair price

Name: Tea Tonic Factory
Author: T P
Review: I ordered some of the new tea range and they are wonderful. I love how subtle and refreshing they are. I haven't stopped drinking them since I got them. I take a little of you guys with me now, everywhere I travel, as the travel tins are a great travel size - just a little luxury everywhere.

Name: Tea Tonic Factory
Author: Asima
Review:
Purchased a few tins of tea at the show, big shout out to the lovely staff member who was patient with mum and I whilst we tried some of the teas and made our selection (wish I got her name!). Have had a few cuppas since coming back and everyone in my family has enjoyed the teas, you can really feel the top notch quality. Would definitely make a trip to the factory in the future. Keep doing what you do guys!!

We purchased the Body Reset, Complexion, Breathe Easy and Throat Soother teas - all fab!

Name: Tea Tonic Factory
Author: Jenny
Review: We were at the Royal Melbourne Show and had the loveliest person serving us at the Tea Tonic stand, her name is Layla and her knowledge of tea was very impressive. I just wanted to give her a shoutout and acknowledge how great she’s doing!

Name: Tea Tonic Factory
Author: Eleanor Blythman
Review: I adore Tea Tonic. The French Earl Grey, is to die for. I have shared with so many of my friends who also love it. I also love the Tea Party Tea, which I enjoy with my daughters and nieces as a caffeine free treat. Another favourite is the Throat Soother Tea which I always use before a singing gig. Finally, I love the Mint Choc Chai for a guilt free evening treat. Tea Tonic is divine!

Name: Tea Tonic Factory
Author: Jackie Sixth
Review: Love your tea especially the Earl Grey and French earl grey and the black tea and alot more. Love the fact that some teas do actually calm you down or stop cramps. High quality tea. Some can be made into ice tea too. Orange choc a real treat too. Heaps of chai choices too.

Name: Mciver's Coffee & Tea
Address: Queen Victoria Market, Stall 101/102 Dairy Hall, Melbourne
Author: Lee Oswald
Review:
Quaint little stall right at the entrance of the dairy hall, friendly staff with reasonably priced coffee beans.

Coffee beans are of high quality, the richness and liveliness of coffee made from a pour over is indicative of the freshness of their beans, my first time seeing a bed that looks beautiful after I'm done making the coffee, almost looks like a piece of delicious chocolate!

Name: Mciver's Coffee & Tea
Address: Queen Victoria Market, Stall 101/102 Dairy Hall, Melbourne
Author: Hsiao-Chi Liao
Review: These are exactly the beans that I was looking for (& they're fresh)! Honduras Fairtrade Organic with buttery, sweet, and silky notes - loving the taste of warmth coming out of it. Suitable for winter and mornings! The elderly lady at store was super nice, helpful, and friendly.

Name: Mciver's Coffee & Tea
Address: Queen Victoria Market, Stall 101/102 Dairy Hall, Melbourne
Author: MinNee Loh
Review:
They provide many types of coffee and teas, you can ask stuff for try the aroma of your pick.
The stuff was so nice and patient, and even asked if you wish to grind with your home coffee machine / moka pot / etc.

Name: Mciver's Coffee & Tea
Address: Queen Victoria Market, Stall 101/102 Dairy Hall, Melbourne
Author: Julie J (A/X Publisher Digital)
Review:
Fantastic coffee beans loved by both locals and travelers.

The coffee beans are freshly roasted and said to be typically less than a month old if I recall. This is far better than buying commercial roasted beans from supermarkets that have been on the shelves for months.

I personally cannot tolerate coffee beans that aren’t freshly roasted because it gives me terrible anxiety and caffeine crashes mid afternoon. This is why I recommend places such as these so you pay for quality.

Initially unsure if they were open because there was no greeting till I asked if they were serving but afterwards the lovely lady was helpful in my coffee selection.

Side note: Sometimes reviewers can’t tell you that we’re reviewing because we must write about our experience authentically. And we can be anyone from anywhere around the globe.

I’m yet to try their selection of teas but might send my friends who are tea lovers instead.

Anyway I’m really excited for my morning coffee in the upcoming weeks after purchasing two bags happily! Thank you.

Name: Mciver's Coffee & Tea
Address: Queen Victoria Market, Stall 101/102 Dairy Hall, Melbourne
Author: Silluart Studios
Review: A friend from MEL brought us your coffee beans to try and we fell in love. So ordering the same beans online. The beautiful aroma came rushing out of the packaging as soon as the parcel was opened. We are based in BNE, must come to visit your lovely shop when we visit MEL next time!!

Name: Patricia Coffee Brewers
Address: Rear of, 493-495 Little Bourke St, Melbourne
Author: 뜌니Dduny
Review: I went there because it was one of the top three coffee brands in Melbourne. I waited in a long line to meet my reputation and drank coffee. Actually, I don't like acidic coffee, but I came here just tried it. and the milk foam is so soft and savory that I want to drink it again.

Name: Patricia Coffee Brewers
Address: Rear of, 493-495 Little Bourke St, Melbourne
Author: Noey Natnaree T.
Review: Small little cafe hidden in the alley way. They are always busy but the service was fast. The coffee was exceptional as well as the pastries. For me they canelè was one of the best canelè in Melbourne cbd ever. It was so crispy on the outside and custardy on the inside. Staff are friendly and nice 😊

Name: Patricia Coffee Brewers
Address: Rear of, 493-495 Little Bourke St, Melbourne
Author: K K
Review:
Amazing cafe..
Staff are all friendly..
coffee is so delicious..
Although it is pack all the time, atmosphere is warm and welcome.
They used the bean from #market lane cofee, which is great taste.
No.1 Cafe where we tried in Australia. Strongly recommend.

Name: Patricia Coffee Brewers
Address: Rear of, 493-495 Little Bourke St, Melbourne
Author: Minami
Review:
My friend recommended this place, so I went there with my parents☕️
Since it was our first time, we weren’t sure how to order, but one of the café staff kindly helped us☺️
Just as we were about to finish, one of the baristas gave each of us a cup of coffee that we hadn’t ordered! We loved that they offered us a chance to try another type of coffee🤭☕️
The food was amazing too! I especially loved the croissant that the first staff member recommended😋🥐

We absolutely loved everything about this café and would love to visit again if we travel to Melbourne🥰

Name: Patricia Coffee Brewers
Address: Rear of, 493-495 Little Bourke St, Melbourne
Author: Kylin Mao
Review: On my first day in Melbourne, I rushed here with my suitcase right after getting off the airport shuttle bus. The shop sign was not very conspicuous, so I walked back and forth twice. A customer nearby hurriedly caught up with me to tell me that the coffee shop was here, but it was already closed. So he persuaded the staff to sell me a cup of coffee. The owner（or staff） told me that the coffee machine was already off, but he could give me a cup of cold coffee. When I was ready to pay with my credit card, the owner handed me a cup of coffee and bread and told me there was no need to pay. I was very shocked and also very lucky. It was a really good day for me.

Name: Rise & Grind Drive Thru Coffee Preston
Author: George Choueiri
Review: The go to coffee! Perfect service, speed and efficiency. Great food options and pastries. The also have combos. You get a "pick me up", before you get your pick me up! Well done team.

Name: Rise & Grind Drive Thru Coffee Preston
Author: Sonali Casual
Review:
The hot chocolate is really good.

The atmosphere and vibe is awesome as well.

I have tried the food but its between meh to average.

The banana bread however is soooo good. Ask for it to be toasted.

Name: Rise & Grind Drive Thru Coffee Preston
Author: Harcourts Coburg
Review: This coffee drive-thru is hands down the best way to start my morning! I come here every day before work, and they never disappoint. The staff is always friendly and remembers my order, which makes me feel like a valued customer. The coffee is consistently delicious, always hot, and exactly what I need to kickstart my day. Service is fast and efficient, even during busy hours, so I’m never late to work. This place is a true gem – highly recommend to anyone looking for great coffee and exceptional service!

Name: Rise & Grind Drive Thru Coffee Preston
Author: Jasvinder Kaur
Review: This is my regular go too.   Best coffee, best smoothies and the choice of pastries and food is endless.  The staff are amazing and service is always cheerful, with a smile.  They always remember my order and overall these guys deserve more than 10 out of 10 for their consistency and exceptional service.  The coffee is amazing and beats any other coffee I've ever had.  Thankyou rise n grind 😃

Name: Rise & Grind Drive Thru Coffee Preston
Author: Michael Scott
Review:
Great coffee! Even better service! Always greeted by the lovely Persian girl, her customer service is unmatched in comparison to other drive through coffee shops.
Customer service like this keeps me wanting to come back 👍🏼

Name: Rise & Grind Drive Thru Coffee Mitcham
Author: Dav Ster
Review: Amazing coffee. Friendly staff greeting you the moment you arrive. While I didn't drive thru, I was glad I walked in for a delicious flat white.

Name: Rise & Grind Drive Thru Coffee Mitcham
Author: Marreck Cleve Loren Hood
Review:
The Crew at Rise & Grind are “Simply the Best” (cue Tina Turner)!

No matter the day of the week, rain, hail or shine, or the time of day. The quality of service and product is exemplary. No matter how busy, your needs a catered for very efficiently and always with a smile.

The consistency of the coffee is highlighted by the constant rotation of team members through different areas of the business. Clearly training is at the forefront of daily operations.

A top-class hospitality operation, one that not only the owners can be proud of but one that all the team can take immense pride in.

Name: Rise & Grind Drive Thru Coffee Mitcham
Author: MATHIUS V ELITE GAMING
Review: Wanted to leave a review for one of the staff members. I don’t know her name but she is the persian lady. Her kindness, smile, and positivity is contagious! It’s the main reason why i keep returning regularly! Besides the great coffee it’s nice to be greeted every single time! Good staff are hard to find! She even smiles in the rain! Thank you for the consistent exceptional service! Happy customer 😃

Name: Rise & Grind Drive Thru Coffee Mitcham
Author: Fae Ya
Review: Delicious coffee. Super nice staff and speedy service. I visited on Tuesday afternoon and was provided with great recommendations by staff and the most amazing and kind customer service. Thank you!

Name: Rise & Grind Drive Thru Coffee Mitcham
Author: Jason Kirk
Review:
I stop here daily for iced coffee. They all know me and service as top shelf, friendly and they make my mornings easier to deal with!
Get all ice cream, extra shot and choc dusting… can’t go wrong.
Someone mentioned bad iced choc… they need to have one with all ice cream. Will make their day!

Name: Sbriga Espresso Bar
Author: Joweli
Review: A very nice sheltered outdoor cafe with heaters to keep warm in winter. The egg and bacon roll with smashed avocado and flat white was a great combination to start a long day. This joint is also popular with the working crowd in the area.

Name: Sbriga Espresso Bar
Author: HY T
Review: Popped by sbrigo after seeing great reviews and the coffee lived up to expectations. Staff was super friendly as well and the sourdough was amazing with the coffee.

Name: Sbriga Espresso Bar
Author: Suzie Stosic
Review:
This is the BEST espresso coffee I've had ever!
And the service here was fantastic too!

I will definitely be visiting this cafe every time I'm in town
10/10

Name: Sbriga Espresso Bar
Author: P
Review: Now I know why this small cafe has a rating of 4.7: The cappuccino (AUD5) was the best I've drank so far during my past 2 weeks' stay in Melbourne and Cairns. I had an almond croissant (heated in the oven) for AUD7. It had one seat indoor. By the time I left at about 8.40 am, there was a queue of 5 people waiting for their coffee!

Name: Sbriga Espresso Bar
Author: Kathryn Lambkin
Review: Affordable quick breakfast. Light-sized meal. Which is a nice change from over-sized high calorie meals. Quick, friendly service. Outdoor seating only, with large umbrellas and effective low gas heaters.

Name: Fabian
Author: Janina Wanderlust
Review:
They just released their Strawberry Cloud Iced Matcha. It is the perfect balance between earthy matcha and sweet, refreshing strawberry. Honestly, one of the best matcha drinks I’ve had in Melbourne, hands down. They’ve also added a new drink, Nutella Latte-perfect for Nutella lovers.

You can also pair your drinks with their savory croissants or bagels.

The staff were so friendly and shows great service too which is another reason for me to come back.
-
Must try drink/dishes:
Strawberry Cloud Iced Matcha - this does not overpower each other’s flavour. Really one of the best I’ve had. 🙌🏻
Nutella Latte. - for Nutella Lovers, like me!  Creamy, chocolate-y and just enough sweetnesst! It’s everything you’ve been dreaming of.
Iced Coffee with Ice Cream – smooth coffee and creamy ice cream make this a must on warmer days. Smoked Salmon Bagel - smoked salmon and cream cheese combo is a winner for me!
Reuben’s Bagel - Tangy, filling, and absolutely melt in your mouth thinly sliced roast beef!

Name: Fabian
Author: taunthom
Review: Nice little gem located inside the busy CBD of Melbourne, the staff were friendly and the service was quick. The Cappuccino and Ham and cheese croissant were delicious and the interior had a homely feel to it.

Name: Fabian
Author: Melbourne Foodie in a Hoodie
Review:
This small space is so comfy and welcoming, making it a perfect spot for a coffee break! Excellent customer service by the staff.

- Tried the VIRAL Strawberry cloud iced match. It was fluffy, not overly sweet, and OH SO GOOD! 🍓
- Nutella hot chocolate was beautiful. 🍫
- The salmon bagel made a perfect lunch.🥯
- Almond Croissant was flaky and fresh. 🥐

Name: Fabian
Author: gy k (모리언니)
Review: A cafe in Melbourne that is famous for coffee. Soy latte was more fragrant than other places. The foam also soft 🤎

Name: Fabian
Author: Violet Mascarenhas
Review:
Love the Strawberry Matcha here! Soo good!
The salmon bagel is the best 👌
Very convenient location and the staff is extremely friendly.

Name: SAAZAA Coffee
Author: Ricky
Review: A nice and quiet place for a coffee on a Sunday afternoon. The location was easy to get to with ample street parking. The coffee was nice with a variety of beans selection! I hope they could serve more pastry options in the future that would be a great improvement!

Name: SAAZAA Coffee
Author: Martin Rayner
Review: Had a coffee and cake here today as part of an AmazingCo experience. The staff were all very friendly and helpful in choosing from their delicious range of cakes and pastries. Their coffee was very smooth and more-ish. The venue is and excellent example of industrial conversation with a perfect blend of cafe/working business. It's nice to sit in the same place where the coffee I'm drinking has also been roasted. There's also an abundance of space.

Name: SAAZAA Coffee
Author: Stefan Nicorescu
Review: Great coffee if you’re looking for a local business with a great coffee, wasn’t sure what was the difference between a large and a medium size, they both looked the same but different serving cups, would come back for sure though

Name: SAAZAA Coffee
Author: bahareh vafaei
Review: Saazaa Cafe offers delightful Saazaa coffee beans, perfect for everyday brewing. The cozy shop now boasts a charming indoor area with good vibes, ideal for enjoying a cup of their quality coffee.

Name: SAAZAA Coffee
Author: raida yusuf
Review:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
"I had an amazing experience! The food was delicious, the coffee was exceptional, and the customer service was perfect. I will definitely be coming back!"

Name: Padre Coffee Brunswick East
Author: T P
Review: Good coffee, as expected. Breakfast selection limited and a bit expensive. Tasty toasties. Great space with room to spread out.

Name: Padre Coffee Brunswick East
Author: Farisa Noviyanti
Review: We love this place! The staff are friendly and the atmosphere is really cozy. Our favourite is lemonade and almond croissant. We bought one package of ground coffee here, it was perfect (the name is Daddy’s Girls - with hazelnut and creamy flavour). There are some books about coffee that we can read, and kids also can enjoy it too (they have babycino with marshmallows and some books for kids)

Name: Padre Coffee Brunswick East
Author: Dan B
Review: I've been coming here for a couple of years now and never go anywhere else. The coffee is great of course, but what I like best is the unpretentious vibe, the light space and most of all the staff who without exception are lovely - it's clearly a nice place to work judging by how happy and relaxed they always seem. The cute dogs and kids are a bonus - I bring both of mine here and they love it ❤️

Name: Padre Coffee Brunswick East
Author: arian t
Review:
Nice cafe, very friendly staff and good service. I had an Ice latte and tried their coffee roll for the first time, it was alright but wasn't the best roll that I've had in terms of freshness and taste, so I'd give it a 4 out of 5.
And as for the coffee, it was more on the Arabica side I think, I could taste a lot of fruity and acidic flavors which was interesting. I personally like my coffee more chocolaty and Nutty but it was fun to try a different coffee profile but that doesn't mean the coffee wasn't good, just a matter of taste and preferences. Bottom line, I enjoyed my time. Thanks guys

Name: Padre Coffee Brunswick East
Author: Mel B
Review:
Delicious soy latte and amazing pastries from Via Porta. Very smooth tasting coffee beans which have been roasted well. Staff are super friendly and I can tell they remember their regulars.

You can pick up your coffee beans to make at home at this store.

Name: Coffea Coffee
Author: donna cotterell
Review:
Great coffee,  fast service and delicious breakfast.
Best scrambled eggs I've had in years.

Name: Coffea Coffee
Author: Jinrui Ma
Review: I have bought this Bialetti pot from them last week, the pot lost colour after I cleaned it. This is absolutely a scam

Name: Coffea Coffee
Author: Kei Onuma
Review:
A great Melbourne coffee shop. The coffee is a Special store blend which was the most delicious of all the  Coffee’s I have had in Melbourne. I am staying in here for 7 days already returned 4 times.

Most disappointing thing about this place is that is closed Monday

Name: Coffea Coffee
Author: Avi
Review: Nice coffee, but average breakfast for the price. Would only comeback for the coffee

Name: Coffea Coffee
Author: K PR
Review: Awesome atmosphere, great coffee and lovely people 😊😊😊

Name: Brown Sugar Cafe
Author: Eddie Khays
Review:
Nestled in the heart of Melbourne, Brown Sugar Cafe is a true hidden gem that combines a cozy atmosphere with outstanding food and impeccable service. From the moment you step through the doors, you're greeted with a warm, inviting ambiance that feels like a home away from home. The rustic decor, with its charming wooden tables and ambient lighting, sets the stage for a relaxed dining experience.

The coffee at Brown Sugar Cafe is nothing short of exceptional.  The baristas here clearly know their craft, and the result is a consistently excellent brew that keeps patrons coming back for more.

But what truly sets Brown Sugar Cafe apart is the service. The staff (Alba and Rin) are friendly, attentive, and genuinely care about ensuring a pleasant dining experience for every guest. Their warm hospitality makes you feel like a valued friend rather than just another customer.

In summary, Brown Sugar Cafe is a must-visit for anyone in Melbourne looking to enjoy great food, excellent coffee, and top-notch service in a welcoming environment. Whether you're a local or just passing through, this cafe will quickly become a favorite spot.

Name: Brown Sugar Cafe
Author: sina najarha
Review: Brown Sugar Cafe offers a delightful experience across the board. The atmosphere is warm and inviting, perfect for a casual meal or a cozy catch-up. The service is attentive and friendly, making you feel right at home. As for the food, it's absolutely delicious! Each dish bursts with flavor and is prepared with care. The portion sizes are generous, ensuring you leave satisfied without breaking the bank. Overall, Brown Sugar Cafe strikes a perfect balance of quality, quantity, and affordability, making it a must-visit for anyone craving a memorable dining experience.

Name: Brown Sugar Cafe
Author: Travel Gen
Review: Brown Sugar Cafe in the Block Arcade offers a delightful coffee experience in beautifully renovated premises. The ambience combines modern comfort with the historic charm of the arcade, making it a perfect spot to relax. The coffee was excellent, brewed to perfection, and the service was warm and welcoming. Whether you're looking for a cozy place to enjoy your coffee or just passing through the arcade, Brown Sugar Cafe is well worth a visit.

Name: Brown Sugar Cafe
Author: Pia Linn Theissing
Review:
Lovely cafe in Melbourne, definitely worth a try!
It’s located in a cute laneway at the Block Arcade right in Cbd. The staff is super friendly, amazing coffee and cozy atmosphere.
I tried the Risotto of the day and it’s one of the best vegetarian risottos I’ve ever had!!
Don’t miss out on that!

Name: Brown Sugar Cafe
Author: Talei K
Review: The baked polenta and mushroom dish was lovely- polenta was creamy and smooth with a crunchy edge. There was a mountain of kale...I like kale but couldn't get through half of it. Hubby enjoyed the French toast, not so much the coffee. The coffee was not to my liking at all, but exchanged for tea that was fine. When paying the bill realised they charge the tax on top, so another $7. A pleasant cafe

Name: Coffee Plus
Author: Paige Palmieri
Review:
Fantastic local spot with impeccable service and the BEST coffee you can find.

The coffee served here is clearly a labour of love as every coffee I've been served at this establishment has been as delicious and smooth as the very first that sucked me in.

The staff are very well liked by the local community and you can easily see why upon visiting and I have no doubt you'll be hooked on the first sip as well.

The food made in house is absolutely delicious, simple fare perfect for a light breakfast or lunch. Pictured is the house granola with apple and honey and it is a phenomenal way to start your morning.

If you're in Ivanhoe, I would make a visit to coffee plus for an invigorating brew the first stop of your day.

Name: Coffee Plus
Author: Two Sheds
Review: This is a nice tucked away spot and clearly very popular with locals.  The coffee was nice as was the toast and vegemite.  This seems like a popular spot with some nice banter between customers and staff today

Name: Coffee Plus
Author: Paige Christo
Review: Best coffee in Melbourne!!!! Amazing service and very cute atmosphere. Can’t wait to go back

Name: Coffee Plus
Author: Neil A
Review: One of the best coffees in Ivanhoe. Out of the way and easy to miss. Good service and good coffee.

Name: Coffee Plus
Author: Timothy Buhov
Review: Fantastic experience at Coffee Plus. The customer service was impeccable and the coffee was top notch.

Name: The League of Honest Coffee
Author: Sonja Pitt
Review:
Pretty abysmal food and coffee on a Saturday morning. Thought they’d have more specialty coffee type stuff, but coffee served in a bowl certainly isn’t it… Did someone forget to wash the mugs or cups??

Only a few toasties left at 10am, no kitchen…. Had one tomato and cheese and one ham and cheese croissant. Gas station quality. Basically no ham/cheese/tomato in them at all. Not nearly enough for breakfast for one person. Some thick cut tomatoes and less stingy on the ham and tomato would have made up for the lack of quality elsewhere.

Filter coffee was OK, but probably under extracted. Lighter roast or better selection would have been better.

Maybe come here if you’re after just a quick to-go coffee, will probably be better than a takeaway elsewhere… But pretty sad for dine-in.

Name: The League of Honest Coffee
Author: Abizer Mohmedi
Review:
Had an honest “bowl” of Flat White to start my day, with a delicious Chk-Avocado-Spinach toastie
The coffee was well made with perfectly steamed and textured milk. The bean flavour was coming through so well 🤎😍
The toastie had some peppercorn kernels adding the right amount of heat at intervals. With the cheese and spinach, it was very flavoursome. I could have done with more avocado though- it was hard to find/taste.
This is my second trip here, while visiting the city and it’s becoming my go-to café! ☕️
Well done, guys ✨🌟

Name: The League of Honest Coffee
Author: Lucy Bui
Review: Delicious hot chocolate, a little rich for my liking but was very well made. We ordered a chicken sandwich which was also great, a little salty but very enjoyable. I was disappointed that they did not have any matcha but the lovely staff and cozy atmosphere definitely made up for it. Excited to return and try out more of the menu!

Name: The League of Honest Coffee
Author: John Domingo
Review:
Ordered an oat mocha, iced latte, mushroom toastie, and cheese & tomato toastie. The cup for the mocha was really cute! The coffee was amazing. The toasties were pretty filling.

The iced latte was on the smaller size, but would probably just need to order a bigger cup next time.

Name: The League of Honest Coffee
Author: IL Deko
Review: This cafe is just opposite to the hotel so it’s the most convenient when you need that caffeine in the morning. It’s super duper busy with the working crowds coming in for their cuppa and you would think the coffee must be great. Unfortunately NOT the case, the long black is under extracted and taste awfully sour. Definitely won’t drop in again.

Name: Traveller Coffee
Author: Warren Malone
Review: Great coffee and pastries cool vibes inside with music. 10/10 would breakfast again

Name: Traveller Coffee
Author: Alex Friedberg
Review:
Unassuming alley coffee shop serving some delicious top notch coffee. I had an iced latte for $6.00 and it blew pretty much any latte I've had so far out of the water.

Absolutely worth a visit, highly recommend.

Name: Traveller Coffee
Author: Joao Pimentel
Review: Great little coffee shop with top notch coffee.  I had a long black which was really nice.  Service is relaxed and casual.

Name: Traveller Coffee
Author: A W
Review:
Great cafe hidden away on a cute little laneway.

Coffee was solid, great taste, strong but smooth and made with care.

Didn't have any of their food but the baked goods looked great, and the menu looked fun, especially the Korean fried cauliflower!

Whilst you wait for your drink there are some lovely little boutiques to explore and an Italian restaurant/shop that sells Italian treats.

We'll be back!

Name: Traveller Coffee
Author: Kati Keksi
Review:
Gorgeous coffee from a tiny little laneway store. Just enough seats to have a place to rest a moment, more ideal if you're on your own.

Proper thin foam layer on the flat white and super tasty beans from Seven Seeds.

There's a range of cakes and muffins but nothing labelled and all close together so even if something is gluten free it's going to be a high risk for cross contamination.

Never the less I'm here for the coffee and it's certainly worth coming back for. Lovely friendly staff too!

Name: Anda cafe
Author: May Yap
Review: Highly recommend the Philly Cheesesteak and Chai latte here. It is my usual order! I tried their new tomato soup and cheese toastie recently and can see why it’s so popular. Service is always friendly and helpful.

Name: Anda cafe
Author: Lilgem
Review: Coffee is excellent, food amazing! Takeaway window is quite busy, a testament that it’s a favourite for the Melburnians early morning coffee fix. Will definitely be back.

Name: Anda cafe
Author: Maryanne Young
Review:
The best egg and bacon muffin I've ever had,  the special sauce is special! I added my hashbrown into the muffin after I took the photo.

Mum said her toasted ham and cheese croissant was very nice.

Both of our coffees were pretty good

The 4 for atmosphere was just because it was super noisy when we were there

Name: Anda cafe
Author: Cher
Review:
Place gets really crowded but managed to get a table for breakfast.

Tried their chai latte & sausage and egg muffin.

And i must say i didn’t expect much as I was thinking of the one from Mcd, but wow the sausage and egg muffin is AMAZING. the sauce was yummy and the sausage was so juicy and tasty, while the egg was cooked with the yolk being abit raw so it was runny (love runny yolks).

Chai Latte was also really enjoyable, they put alot of spices and the milk and spices balanced really well.

Would recommend!

Name: Anda cafe
Author: Natalie Thaivaranone
Review: I tried sausage and egg muffin as I read through all the reviews here, turned out as great as everyone had mentioned. To my experience, hash brown was sooooooooooo good that I regretted ordered only one! (as I took a delivery). Vanilla bean iced latte was impressive! (I added extra shot of coffee, perfect to my liking! not too strong, not too sweet, love it. 😊👍🏻🙏🏻

Name: Philoçoffee Espresso Bar
Author: Erin
Review: Some of the best coffee in Melbourne, strong but smooth. Also dog friendly, treating pups that come to the window to order.

Name: Philoçoffee Espresso Bar
Author: M M
Review: Coming here is my favourite part of my work days! Not only is it the absolute best coffee.. and the absolute BEST cookies (dark chocolate chip & cranberry + macadamia), but it is the most awesome service. Not only do I get a good dose of cookie and coffee, but a good laugh and chat with the beautiful workers who work here everyday. Exactly what I need to break apart my long days of being glued to the laptop. I would give Philo 10 stars if possible.

Name: Philoçoffee Espresso Bar
Author: Olivia Clark
Review: Such a beautiful coffee and the most beautiful staff. I always love my morning visits to Philocoffee. Thanks guys, keep being awesome :)! Much love Liv ! Thanks for my oat latte ;)!!

Name: Philoçoffee Espresso Bar
Author: Jasmine Shepherd
Review: Such great coffee for takeout, lovely barista SHES amazing and makes such beautiful coffee !! Makes their own beans which are a beautiful blend

Name: Philoçoffee Espresso Bar
Author: Natasha Wolvers
Review: My favourite local cafe, friendly service every time, great food & coffee.

Name: Pellegrini's Espresso Bar
Author: Monike San
Review:
Nestled on a bustling street in Melbourne, this Italian-origin coffee shop is a true gem. From the moment you step inside, the warm, inviting ambience captures your senses, offering a perfect retreat from the city's rush. The aroma of freshly brewed coffee, strong and bold, permeates the air, promising a caffeine experience like no other.

Their menu is a delightful blend of Italian classics and local favorites, ensuring there's something for everyone. The cakes are a standout—each slice a masterpiece of flavor and texture, reminiscent of authentic Italian patisseries. Paired with their robust coffee, it's a match made in heaven.

Beyond the sweets, the food is equally impressive. Each dish is crafted with care, bursting with fresh ingredients and rich flavors that transport you straight to Italy. Whether you're here for a quick coffee or a leisurely meal, this coffee shop delivers consistently on quality, making it a must-visit in Melbourne.

Name: Pellegrini's Espresso Bar
Author: Alice Burgum
Review:
Some of the best food I’ve eaten in Melbourne. Easy lunch & amazing service. It feels like you are eating in a family home. Sometimes a wait to get a table but worth the queue- you know in Melbourne if there’s a queue it’s going to be amazing!

Try anything and everything you can- you can’t miss.

Name: Pellegrini's Espresso Bar
Author: Hans-Joachim Rosehr
Review: Visited Melbourne's oldest Italian bar during lunchtime and found it bustling with activity. The menu offered a variety of well-presented, flavorful dishes. The long black, made with Vittoria coffee, was excellent. A great spot for authentic Italian flavors and a lively atmosphere!

Name: Pellegrini's Espresso Bar
Author: Yusra Kaiser
Review: Cute little old cafe. A great place of coffee for anyone who prefers a laidback simple experience. Lovely photos of owners/family on display. Overall has a very old unchanged homely feeling. Good coffee. Outdoor and indoor seating. Good if you’re alone or with a few people- might have seating issue if too many people

Name: Pellegrini's Espresso Bar
Author: Natasha Cooper
Review:
Felt like I walked into Little Italy! We loved the whole vibe of this place - find a seat in the narrow two sided diner style seats and read the menu off the wooden board.

Food came out in under 5 minutes, given a good chunk of bread while you wait. We both scraped our plates clean. Carbonara and their special bolognese gnocchi were out choices for this night. Was very delicious!

So reasonable in price, both meals were $22 each. Will come back to try their lasagne! It looked mouth watering.

Name: Plus Coffee & Co
Author: Weiqi Wang
Review: The size is quite generous; even my boyfriend was full after one serving. The sandwich bread is crispy, and the fillings are very flavorful, with the mushroom one being stringy. The special blend coffee is also good, and the tiramisu is sweet but not cloying, with a very smooth texture. The wait time for the food wasn't long either, making it well worth trying.

Name: Plus Coffee & Co
Author: Foodie Luwee
Review:
Plus Coffee Co on Flinders Lane may be small in size, but it has a warm, cozy atmosphere that makes it a perfect little spot for a quick coffee break. It’s a bustling place with people constantly coming and going for their takeaway orders, and it’s easy to see why. Their sangas and sandwiches are not only tasty but also reasonably priced, making it a great option for a quick, satisfying meal.

I tried their black sesame latte, and it was absolutely delicious! The flavor was rich and unique, with just the right balance, and the froth was perfectly smooth. I found myself wanting another cup because it was that good!

Name: Plus Coffee & Co
Author: Jessica Ni
Review: I love their Reuben sandwich! The pastrami was so soft and flavourful! Truffle mushroom was also our fav. A great choice to go in CBD for lunch

Name: Plus Coffee & Co
Author: Xiaoqing Sheng
Review: Visited this café and was impressed by the delicious sandwiches and beautifully presented pastries. The coffee was rich and smooth, with stunning latte art. The atmosphere was cozy and inviting, making it a perfect spot for a relaxing meal. Highly recommended!

Name: Plus Coffee & Co
Author: Melbourne Diaries - Judy
Review:
A little cosy spot serving tasty toasted sandwiches in the city as well as specialty drinks.

We tried:
🥪 Beef and Kimchi
🥪 Spicy Tuna Melt
🥪 Miso and sesame eggplant
🍰 Tiramisu
🍵 Black Sesame Latte
🍵 Strawberry and Matcha

For the sandwiches, my top pick was the beef and kimchi - perfect combination of flavours and it also had the right amount of each ingredients. The miso and sesame eggplant was surprisingly really good too👌 Their sandwiches were comforting and delicious to have for lunch and cost about $13-$15 each. Definitely a great weekday lunch option if you work around here! Their drinks were also nice, I liked the strawberry and matcha I got 🍓🍵

They offer both takeaway and dine-in, although their seating is very limited and it can be busy during peak hours.

Name: Icarus Coffee
Author: Matt
Review:
Excellent local cafe with great coffee and delicious food. The owner is a top bloke who clearly takes pride in his business.

Some pics below are from when I returned for takeaway coffee and pies, which I tuned up in the home oven.

Name: Icarus Coffee
Author: Yaren Ugur
Review: Beautiful little local cafe! We had the vegetable roti wrap, toasted - it was absolutely delicious. Coffee & matcha was on point as well, sweet treats were great as well! This was our second time, and we will definitely be back!

Name: Icarus Coffee
Author: Hannah McCann
Review: We love this coffee shop, the owner is so friendly and they definitely make the best babycinos in the area! Great snacks and nice teas for sale too. This coffee shop makes the area feel like a community and not just a place to live 🙏🏼

Name: Icarus Coffee
Author: Claire Dunne
Review: I love coming to Icarus coffee, this cafe is small enough that it offers individual and attentive customer service, but large enough that there are numerous and varied coffee and (delicious!) food options. I love that it also supports local businesses by offering their products in store as well.

Name: Icarus Coffee
Author: Sam Eads
Review: I can't say enough good things about this place. The coffee is perfect, the food is delicious and interesting and the staff are so incredibly friendly. The best bit of it all is the owner, he is always so friendly and greets everyone with a smile. Love walking down here in the morning, a perfect way to start the day.

Name: Coffee Society
Author: Simon Dormer
Review:
Second time here in a week, great interior and place to catch the morning sunshine.

Great range of food too.

For those wanting to work, plenty of space and sockets under the seats for you power. Toilets are near the lifts.

Name: Coffee Society
Author: Rahul Rao
Review: Nice little cafe with very good coffee. I ate the breakfast burger, which was just perfect. The service is very quick and friendly.

Name: Coffee Society
Author: Tailini Diehl
Review:
Really good coffee and food.
Staff friendly and the atmosphere is comfortable and calm.
Nice music/relaxing

Name: Coffee Society
Author: Jora Shen
Review: The place was quite busy, but the food was the absolute best. The serves were huge and I could not even finish. The lady who served me was very friendly and helpful.

Name: Coffee Society
Author: Greek Aliti
Review: Cool little place, great flavours and friendly atmosphere. Very easy to miss if you’re driving past searching for something to eat or drink

Name: BENCH COFFEE CO. Lt Collins
Author: jenny chawla
Review:
I recently visited Bench Coffee, a highly-hyped cafe, with high expectations given its premium pricing. Unfortunately, my experience did not meet these expectations.

I ordered the Cloud Float, priced at $10, which was supposed to feature coconut water and coconut foam. However, I couldn't detect any coconut flavor, and instead of sage, it had rosemary on top. The coffee itself was decent, but the overall drink was disappointing.

The location and ambiance of the cafe are excellent, but the quality of the drink did not justify the price. Overall, I did not enjoy my experience as much as I had hoped.

Name: BENCH COFFEE CO. Lt Collins
Author: Adorable Adventurous
Review:
A little cute Cafe tucked away in the corner of a lane way. I did miss and walked right past it.
Staffs are superb, very nice and friendly. I had the white ice and my friend had the coffee and tonic. Very unique experience with the cold drip and they tastes good.
They have a small selection of dessert, however they all delicious. This is a perfect example of doing small however you perfect it.

Name: BENCH COFFEE CO. Lt Collins
