Author: Jonathan Aumonier-Ward
Review: Friendly staff. Nice location. ☕️ was ok. But honestly a bit weak. The predominant taste was just milk. I think their serving size is too big. I’ll try it again and ask for way less milk next time (maybe bringing my own 6 ounce cup). And if it is better will up my review. #secondchances

Name: BENCH COFFEE CO. Lt Collins
Author: Kam Choy Lan
Review:
Finally tried their seasonal drink - Komorebi... well is overrated in social media... milk over pour
Dacquoise...buttoned-down
Fell undesired by a korean male staff while he taking my order...thought he don't speak well english so he just starring at me (awkward) without any introduce/recommend their house special...but he does while he greeting the rest of the customers...
Water was not serve while my seat they all have it...
But a white boy serve me water at the very end while i was getting to leave

Name: BENCH COFFEE CO. Lt Collins
Author: Wen Min Liew
Review: The aroma of the coffee you can smell it once you pass by the coffee shop. Walked in without thinking of buying anything, but end up getting beans & latte to go ☕️ Very modern & minimalist design, love it.

Name: Otherside Coffee
Author: Scarlett H
Review: A cozy gem with a welcoming vibe! The bright smiley coffee cups add a cheerful touch, perfectly matching the rustic, relaxing atmosphere. Great spot to unwind with a delicious brew surrounded by thoughtful details like potted plants and exposed brick.

Name: Otherside Coffee
Author: Benjamin Lewis
Review: Cute and cool little cafe spot in Melbourne cbd, totally shocked and very happy with the prices. So affordable right now. No extra charges for alternate milks or decaf 😌🚶🏻‍♂️ thank you, spreading the word and will be back

Name: Otherside Coffee
Author: Joweli
Review: A cozy joint for a quick coffee break. What sets this apart from the many other cafes in town is their very reasonable prices without compromising on quality or quantity.

Name: Otherside Coffee
Author: Brittany C
Review: Superb, cheap coffee in Melbourne. Who knew that was a thing! So excited to see a cafe that had cheap coffee AND it wasn't cheap and bad! Great coffee, happy wallet and happy staff

Name: Otherside Coffee
Author: Chris Price (Pricey)
Review:
This needs to be more of a PSA than a google review but the coffee here is very good and ONLY $3.40 for a small plus $0 for alternative milk! What a steal, thank you Otherside for keeping it real.
There also an extensive selection of health grab and go food options available, what a little gem 💎

Name: Industry Beans Lt. Collins
Author: Thomas Zulliger
Review:
Always worth a visit.
Nice flat white.
Love when they have free sparkling water to compliment your coffee drink.
Ceramic cup instead of paper cup upon request is perfect.
Sufficient seating which is nice on a rainy day in Melbourne.
Industry Beans should be on your list.
Recommended!

Name: Industry Beans Lt. Collins
Author: Felicia Evelyn
Review: Tried their ice latte and i think it’s more into the sweeter side (coffee’s not too strong). Brownies were good. Limited seating.

Name: Industry Beans Lt. Collins
Author: Tan T
Review:
Not too bad for ice coffee but not sure if it’s just me but coffee bean didn’t felt the best. It was good to try but not maybe once in a while thing. The cafe set up is quite simpler and nice. Service was okish not very friendly.

Good for one time try.

Name: Industry Beans Lt. Collins
Author: Gigi T
Review: Very disappointing customer service. As a well-known coffee shop in the city, I expected above-average service. When we walked in, the barista, who was also handling the counter, rudely asked us to wait. Although we were not in a rush and waited patiently, she didn't make eye contact or greet us kindly when it was our turn to order. The store wasn’t busy, and while she seemed friendly with her co-worker, her attitude towards us, as customers, was unacceptable. This negatively impacted my coffee experience and the taste of my latte. Bad service and a bad impression of Industry Beans, which is supposed to be a must-try coffee spot. 😢

Name: Industry Beans Lt. Collins
Author: Andreymae Meora
Review: My partner and I love flat white coffee, and they nailed it. Their pastries taste freshly baked—the almond croissant was crisp and had the right amount of filling; the peanut butter brownie was gooey.

Name: Brother Baba Budan
Author: Alice
Review:
The decor is awesome. Only one large square table inside and some outdoor seating space available.
They have single origin beans (extra $1) available, remember to ask for it when you order your coffee.
The latte was surprisingly on the more milky side.
I guess because the cafe is always busy, the staff was as friendly as other cafes around the CBD.

Name: Brother Baba Budan
Author: Sheila C
Review: Hip Melbourne cafe serving amazing coffee and pastries. Loved my cup of Finca Rosma! They use beans from popular roaster Seven Seeds which you can also purchase from the cafe. Space is quite small with only a few seats inside. Service is topnotch as with most cafes in Melbourne.

Name: Brother Baba Budan
Author: jenny chawla
Review:
Another over-hyped cafe I visited was Brother Baba Budan. The interiors were unique and impressive, and the staff was friendly.

The cafe offers a wide range of coffee options, albeit at a high price point. I decided to try the coffee based on the positive reviews I'd heard. I ordered an almond cappuccino, and my friend had a batch brew. Although the prices were steep, the quality of the coffee was exceptional. The flavor profiles were spot-on, and we thoroughly enjoyed our drinks.

Overall, despite the higher cost, the quality of the coffee made it worth the visit.

Name: Brother Baba Budan
Author: Kwan Jason
Review:
The flat white is so good, smooth and creamy.

Banana bread ok.

The space there a bit tight, recommend to sit at tall table.

Name: Brother Baba Budan
Author: Marcel Tornow
Review: Had a fantastic experience at this café! The coffee was absolutely delicious with Seven Seeds beans, especially the single origin and the cold brew, both rich in flavor. The décor is unique and quirky, with chairs hanging from the ceiling. Despite being quite busy, the wait time was surprisingly short. The mushroom toastie was a standout! Definitely worth a visit :)

Name: Coffee Exchange
Author: Abizer Mohmedi
Review:
Below average coffee. Ordered a flat white- the milk was not steamed properly, leaving a thinly textured cup with minimal forced latte art. The espresso tasted over extracted (bitter)
Ordered a toasted salmon bagel- again it was just a very dry bagel with salmon stuffed inside. Absolutely not worth the expensive pricing. Avoid.

Name: Coffee Exchange
Author: Ryan W
Review: On my journey for a good value ham cheese croissant, came across this cafe. $10 for this one. Taste was ok and passable, but compared in both size and contents, other cafes are better suited in terms of size and cost.

Name: Coffee Exchange
Author: Annaliza Vergara
Review: So I'm usually not much of a coffee snob, but something like a simple cappuccino shouldn't be terrible as the one I ordered from this cafe. It was so awful that I had to order it again from another cafe (one that tasted like real cappaccino). Also, one of the staff members who served me wasn't very friendly, and when I asked for sugar, she had a very annoyed look on her face. A bare minimum standard of customer service and coffee quality should at least be met, especially if you're based in Melbourne and honestly I would think the $2 coffee from 7-Eleven would meet that than this one. I'm sure this cafe is loved by locals, which is why I want to put up this review to hopefully enable some improvement in the future for their business.

Name: Coffee Exchange
Author: Celestine
Review: It was not crowded when we ate there. Their croissant was good, I had 2 of them. The toastie was ok. Their ham was delicious. Coffee was not bad and the chai was not the instant kind.

Name: Coffee Exchange
Author: Jo SB
Review:
I've come here a few times for breakfast, and this time I ordered The Hipster ($23.6) served with poached eggs, smoked salmon, grilled halloumi, and sriracha may on sourdough.

Great presentation, and it tasted good. They have decent coffee.

Name: Little Rogue.
Author: reham abdullah
Review: Little Rouge Café offers a cozy atmosphere with friendly service, perfect for a casual coffee break. I enjoyed their chocolate banana bread, which was moist, rich, and had the perfect balance of sweetness. Paired with a smooth flat white, it made for a delightful treat. The café is a great spot for a quick snack or a relaxed coffee moment. Would definitely recommend for anyone looking for a little comfort food and great coffee!

Name: Little Rogue.
Author: A S
Review:
I find myself returning to this cafe, day after day, time after time.
The staff are so friendly and take time, when asking about the coffee.
The place is usually full and yet I always find a place to sit and read or write and look outside to the spectacle of life.
Young and old, walking along the small alley this cafe is located in.
Dogs waiting for their owners and dog owners waiting for their matcha latte: modern poetry.

Name: Little Rogue.
Author: Tan P
Review:
Hidding in small corner that we nearly miss it. I believe if you really want to find, you will with your ❤️.

Love their matcha and flatwhite.

Food is good too. We had ham & cheese toasted croissant & matcha cheese cake. Yummy!

Lovely & friendly vibes tho limited sitting area.

Overall experience was good.

Name: Little Rogue.
Author: Hermione Le
Review: Small but really nice coffee and a small place to chill for working and reading. Limited seats so should only sit for short time. Very busy coffee shop. Their matcha latte and sakuratte were amazing. This shop and the opposite bakery Bakemon belong to the same company.

Name: Little Rogue.
Author: Dita W
Review: The BEST place for matcha latte! Always loved the atmosphere, and they even sell their own matcha powder to take home. Plus, the bakery right across is a fantastic bonus. Only few seats available which is not really an issue as we normally do takeaway.

Name: Puzzle Coffee
Author: L. Juan M.S., Ph.D.
Review: Puzzle Cafe is such a unique coffee spot in Australia! When I first visited, I tried their white latte served in an edible cup. While the cup wasn't the most delicious, the concept of an edible cup was truly fascinating! I'm looking forward to my next visit to try the dirty matcha; it sounds like a must-try!

Name: Puzzle Coffee
Author: Derek Rylah
Review:
Puzzle Coffee on Swanston St has lightning fast service by very friendly staff.
The 'Dirty Macha' was really popular so I thought I'd give it a go. It tastes like an odd mix of coffee and green tea with milk. Be warned, it's an acquired taste that I don't wish to try again but glad I've experienced it.
Next time I'll play it safe with a single origin batch brew.

Name: Puzzle Coffee
Author: Pema I'Anson
Review: Dropped into Puzzle Coffee today and wasn't disappointed! Had a lovely cinnamon bun and white coffee, they have a discount for bringing your own reusable cup which I always appreciate. Staff are very friendly. Prices a little steep and there is no seating otherwise it would be 5🌟!

Name: Puzzle Coffee
Author: jayne maree
Review: Matcha & Latte ✅✅ my fussy daughter loved Matcha here (says not all places get Matcha right but liked this version very much )  so comes regularly & as I’m in town took me.  Coffee was nice flavour & hot. Cute design & nice staff. Will be back.

Name: Puzzle Coffee
Author: Yvonne Luk
Review:
I got a latte in an eligible cup! The shop smells so good with coffee, and so does the drink. The eligible cup needs an extra AUD$3, but it is worth a try. It's not as tasty as I thought it would be, but I appreciate the creativity and environmental impact of it.
The moment I drink the coffee, it makes me wanna buy the beans, but too bad they are out of stock. I managed to find it in another branch in Melbourne CBD.

Name: Market Lane Coffee - Collins St
Author: N J
Review: Coffee is so good. My latte is very round and balanced. The aroma is so nice, and their brown sugar is so on point. It’s go along well with the coffee taste. Perfect!!

Name: Market Lane Coffee - Collins St
Author: Shirin AF
Review:
I had a great coffee experience over there.
The aroma of Market Lane’s coffee is inviting.
For those who appreciate the art of coffee, Market Lane is a destination that consistently delivers excellence in each cup, making it a top choice for both seasoned connoisseurs and those looking to explore the world of specialty coffee.
They also offer a relaxed and welcoming atmosphere that encourages to savor coffee and engage in meaningful conversations.
It is high recommended.

Oat Latte 5/5

Name: Market Lane Coffee - Collins St
Author: Maximillion L
Review:
Just love this place. The staff were really friendly, coffee were made with care and absolutely to my taste, superb. Baguette and croissants were excellent too!

The effortless minimalist design of the place just makes you feel great being there.

Name: Market Lane Coffee - Collins St
Author: IRMA JANAMEJOY MADROÑERO
Review: This is the best customer service in all Melbourne, amazing staff, amazing coffee, good location. Thank you so much!

Name: Market Lane Coffee - Collins St
Author: L. H.
Review: The coffee beans are really nice but minor figures oat milk does not suit the espresso flavor. Makes it really acidic! With another oat milk brand would be 5 stars

Name: MAKER Lt Bourke
Author: Pouria Aryan
Review: I absolutely loved the decor and the location of this spot. I stopped in briefly for a coffee, and I can confidently say it was amazing—some of the best I've had. The latte art was beautiful, and the overall vibe was warm and inviting. I wish I had more time (and room!) to try other menu items. Kudos to the team for creating such a wonderful experience!

Name: MAKER Lt Bourke
Author: Jay W
Review:
Nice clean open area cafe located on a bustling street.

As a tourist visiting from Auckland, the coffee was a bit on the weaker side for a flatwhite i am used to.

The staff were friendly and the service was great where the staff even brought around water for us.

The cafe environment was cosy and a pleasure to be in.

Name: MAKER Lt Bourke
Author: Andre
Review: Awesome coffee, friendly staff. Gets packed but the still churn out the cuppas speedily. And it’s nice that they don’t charge extra for milk.

Name: MAKER Lt Bourke
Author: Cybill Getgood
Review: Exceptional Coffee!!! As a marketing executive, I appreciate the well-branded cups & merchandise too! Will definitely be back next time I’m in Melbourne!

Name: MAKER Lt Bourke
Author: Michelle Ngn
Review: The store has a cute modern vibe. The drink was good as well and i got an iced latte but it was only 6AUD, much cheaper compared to Brother Baba Budan just near that area. I will definitely comeback again!

Name: Coffee Geek Brewers
Author: Subramaniam Jeeshan
Review: A specialty coffee place for coffee lovers. Very friendly barista, great coffee and for someone like me who prefer hot latte/magic with a sweet note of strawberry and cocoa in a cup, this would be the perfect place. Would def recommend magic with soy milk. No bitterness or acidity at all.

Name: Coffee Geek Brewers
Author: Two Sheds
Review: The name of this place alone commands attention if you’re on the lookout for top coffee.  We’ve got a gorgeous space here.  Sophisticated, warm and inviting.  The soft guitar music in the background added to the allure. I was after my now customary long black.  I was well advised including on optimal brew size.  Service overall was genuinely affable and friendly..  cold water was delivered to my table as soon as I arrived.  The coffee came on a nice little board.  I love it when you get the mini sparkling water too as they gave me here.  It’s classy.  The coffee was absolutely delectable.  Mighty impressive place.  Loved it.

Name: Coffee Geek Brewers
Author: Johannes
Review: Great coffee, super friendly service. Definitely a must visit when in Melbourne!!

Name: Coffee Geek Brewers
Author: J Davis
Review: Busy with locals this is the best coffee by far across several blocks. Flat white on the footpath (x2) just what I needed on a chilly Melbourne morning in December. Friendly folk, not the too cool variety you find in some places. Delicious coffee. Great little side street position too to sit and watch the trams go by.

Name: Coffee Geek Brewers
Author: Mel B
Review:
Delicious coffee at this window shop coffee hidden behind the busy road. Staff are super friendly!

Got an almond flat white which was very smooth and creamy. 2 shots in a small coffee cup with dairy alternative for $4.80 - pretty good deal in this time of inflation haha

Name: Parlance Coffee
Author: Larissa Linnell
Review:
I had my fist coffee here yesterday and ordered drinks for my family. My coffee a large latte was bitter - even with added sugar. The barista was very nice however she didn’t offer to remake. A shame I was really hoping to like this place!
I think that the machine was probably dirty after a busy day. I won’t go back here for coffee.

Name: Parlance Coffee
Author: eggCellent
Review:
A hidden gem on the pathway from Elizabeth St into Emporium!
Nice coffee, pastries and sweets :) Super cool futuristic interior design - a marriage of Klein Blue and Metallic bench! Also tried their hot pressed sandwich at another store on A’Beckett st yummmm 🤤🥪

Name: Parlance Coffee
Author: Projects By Jomar
Review:
Escape the hustle and bustle and sneak towards the strand and hide away with a cuppa from Parlance Cafe. Discreetly tucked away in the strand towards the exit of emporium is this pleasant cafe serving an option of sweets, cakes, croissants and more!
The coffee is above average and served with a polite and friendly smile!
Once you have received your delightful order snuggle onto the centre’s cushioned seats available in front of the cafe as seatings is not available in the cafe, it is dedicated and designed for take out only.

Name: Parlance Coffee
Author: Nguyen Lam
Review: I gave this place a few tries because it’s near my place but tbh it’s very hard to love it… The matcha latte is bland, the oat milk tastes so weird, … Ordered an $8 danish just for it to turn out mushy and 0% flaky, feels like it’s been left for days.

Name: Parlance Coffee
Author: Ava Cado
Review: Love the coffee here. Removed a star because there is a male barista who is always on his phone - It's unprofessional and unhygienic (he touches his phone then the food)

Name: Axil Coffee Roasters Melbourne Central
Author: Paul Prakongsunt
Review: Excellent coffee as usual. We have been fans of Axil since they opened in Hawthorn years ago. Customer service is also fantastic. Good vibe, prime location. Highly recommendable 👍

Name: Axil Coffee Roasters Melbourne Central
Author: Steven Haas
Review:
Both my friend and I were very impressed by our visit here today at Axil Coffee Roasters at Melbourne Central, after having meaning to visit this venue for quite some time.

Both service and food offered were incredible, in which we learned that the menu offered was a fresh menu that had just been released earlier in the week for the winter.

Oat porridge was personally chosen as a winter warmer myself, whilst my friend chose the smashed avocado (the most avocado either of us had seen on such a dish). Beverages tried whilst at the venue included traditional such as almond lattes as well as specials such as the house special ‘New Orleans cold brew’ and ‘strawberry iced chai’ (both highly recommended), as well as the chilli hot chocolate, which was terrific on a cool day.

Atmosphere and ambience was great for a terrific conversation, but left a little to be desired when it came to keeping warm (a lot of glass at the venue makes it difficult to maintain the heat, I suppose). Otherwise, absolutely great - looking forward to return and try out other items on the menu!

Name: Axil Coffee Roasters Melbourne Central
Author: ksf 2000
Review: Pancake is huge and delicious. The environment is so big and leisurely. Coffee is delicious. Staff is nice.

Name: Axil Coffee Roasters Melbourne Central
Author: Jolin Chang
Review:
My first visit with friend was amazing! All the food have great presentation, portion and worth the value.

Gotta go back and try different things next time

Name: Axil Coffee Roasters Melbourne Central
Author: Tash Marion
Review: Beautiful presented and the best services. Coffee was strong and very nice. You have to pay more for the premium experience of course.

Name: Impresso Cafe
Author: Harinduni Kannangara
Review:
This is a great little spot to grab a coffee!

The staff are super friendly, and it’s so convenient that they open at 6:30 am on Saturdays.

The coffee is smooth and delicious.

Name: Impresso Cafe
Author: Rachel Ovalle
Review: Very good coffee. Nice atmosphere inside with ample seating. A good place to meet a friend or work colleague for coffee.

Name: Impresso Cafe
Author: Ercole Vergara
Review: I love how the barista is passionate with pouring different latte art designs on coffees. I’m a barista myself and I love it here. If you’re looking for satisfying coffee arts, this is your place.

Name: Impresso Cafe
Author: Vivian William
Review: Lovely little cafe to stop for a break, friendly staff, coffee was good, latte art was beautiful and chocolate croissant was very nice.

Name: Impresso Cafe
Author: Kerry Tan
Review: Strongest coffee I've had in Melbourne and one of the best I've had, and that's saying something since Melbourne has good coffee at almost every corner. The egg and bacon bun was great too. Best of all, the service especially that of the lady barista who was so friendly. You made my day on a cold rainy morning. Thanks!

Name: Jasper Coffee
Address: Highpoint Shopping Centre, Unit 2552/200 Rosamond Rd, Maribyrnong
Author: Kat & Dav Adventures
Review: Decent coffee but a bit over-priced. Fine by shopping centre standards, but you can probably do better.

Name: Jasper Coffee
Address: Highpoint Shopping Centre, Unit 2552/200 Rosamond Rd, Maribyrnong
Author: Ni
Review: Jasper has nice coffee beans however coffees from this shop have been quite inconsistent which is disappointing. Been here a few times on the weekends, coffee from last Sunday in particular was very good. Medium FW with 2 shots, first sip was full of flavour, right temperature. The one from today was super milky, no coffee taste, didn’t finish it. Coffee spilled around the takeaway cup, sticked all over the hand. Bought a matcha latte, also had coffee spilled, like they transferred coffee shots around hence my weak coffee and reused the cup that had coffee for the matcha. Paid $5.50 for a cup of warm milk with a few drops of coffee. Just disappointing.

Name: Jasper Coffee
Address: Highpoint Shopping Centre, Unit 2552/200 Rosamond Rd, Maribyrnong
Author: Judy Frazer
Review: Disappointed.  I did not finish my Smashed Avo. The avocado was browned and tasteless, (should have been discarded) and the too-lightly  ‘toast’ was warm bread. Only the lightly cooked cherry tomatoes and my coffee were fine.. Not value for the cost at all.

Name: Jasper Coffee
Address: Highpoint Shopping Centre, Unit 2552/200 Rosamond Rd, Maribyrnong
Author: Leigh Ann Mahady
Review: I eat here regularly.  Always enjoy food and coffee.  Today however I saw a young Vietnamese worker berate a colleague near my table.  This was upsetting.  If the young girl was a manager of the Cafe she needs more training  in how to support her colleague rather than tell her off. I wonder if the new staff member has had a good enough induction? Maybe management  need to think about this.

Name: Jasper Coffee
Address: Highpoint Shopping Centre, Unit 2552/200 Rosamond Rd, Maribyrnong
Author: Katherine Chiu
Review:
The coffee was really bad. I've had better at Middle child and Poyntons. The milk that was used in my soy latte was not soy but full cream. I have been to Jasper before during pre Covid and it was much better then!!!!
The vanilla slice I have had better at Poyntons. This one was dry
The workers didn't want to be there. I didn't really want to write this review but thought I would warn the actual lactose intolerance people. Luckily I can handle full cream but for someone with a sensitive stomach this would be a no go for you!!!
The only saving grace was the coffee tonic which I liked

Name: Proud Mary Coffee
Author: Siren (Siren)
Review:
Great little Cafe with a warm vibe. It feels full but you always have space to move and feel comfortable. The decor adds to this
Food was fast and delicious. The Hash just needed a touch more salt.

Name: Proud Mary Coffee
Author: manooch
Review: If you want to have different experiences with morning coffee definitely you need to get there and get them a go. They have got a specific menu just for coffee that brings you knowledge about the variety of coffee beans.  Also, they got delicious breakfast and very helpful staff.

Name: Proud Mary Coffee
Author: Tristan
Review: My experience at Proud Mary Coffee was a bit underwhelming, especially after hearing so many high compliments. While they seem to know their coffee well, my soy flat white was disappointing—the foam on top ruined the taste and didn’t look appealing either. The kimchi avo on toast tasted great, but the kimchi wasn’t the traditional Korean spicy kind, and while the toppings were delicious, the bacon felt overlooked. I admire their experimental twist on classic brunch dishes, but the execution still needs some polishing. Considering this place is a bit expensive, I did expect the dish to be perfect. I’ll give this place another chance.

Name: Proud Mary Coffee
Author: Derek Hum
Review:
Great place for coffee and a meal. Dishes are unique and delicious. The tiramisu hotcakes was a nice dessert which we shared - might've been too much for one I think.

Piccolo was good and the cold drip was refreshing, if a bit small.

Name: Proud Mary Coffee
Author: Simon T
Review: Excellent spot for coffee and breakfast. Be sure to ask for today's coffee recommendations. Always busy and vibrant

Name: Three Drops Coffee & Sandwich
Author: Phillip Janicki
Review: A small little cafe with minimal outdoor seating only, hence the lower score for atmosphere but the service was good and helpful. Most importantly, the food was unreal and reasonably priced. We got the bacon K-Sandwiches and they are seriously unreal. The flavours are amazing. I only wish I got double bacon to take it to the next level but that’s hindsight for you and still can’t really fault it. Definitely recommend checking this little beauty out.

Name: Three Drops Coffee & Sandwich
Author: Payel Gayen
Review:
Absolutely delightful!
This spot serves some of the best sandwiches in Melbourne and is the perfect place to satisfy your breakfast cravings.

We ordered the classic bacon sandwich, the roasted chicken sandwich, and mocha. The mocha was quite good, and the chicken sandwich was perfectly prepared and tasted fantastic. However, the true standout was the classic bacon sandwich, which was mouthwateringly delicious and filled with fluffy eggs. Simply lovely!

There are some outdoor seating options, and the service was quick and prompt. I highly recommend this place to everyone!

Name: Three Drops Coffee & Sandwich
Author: Daniel Symons
Review: Delicious Korean sandwich, excellent coffee. Lovely service. Well worth a visit if you're after something unique or just plain yummy!

Name: Three Drops Coffee & Sandwich
Author: Lollie Pop
Review: Their bulgogi sandwich is light, flavourful …. dances in your mouth type of texture and flavour. It’s not something I’ve tasted before and it’s absolutely moorish and delightful. Generous portion also. It’s more of a takeaway shop. They do have tables on the walkway next to the road and you can’t sit there when it rains. That’s the only downside. Otherwise, it’s an excellent little cafe.

Name: Three Drops Coffee & Sandwich
Author: Liam
Review:
Cool hole in the wall establishment. I had the ham and cheese toasties, which were delicious and reasonably priced. I’d recommend for anyone going through the city trying to find a bargain!

They also had a few tables on the side of the street, where I parked up for a bit. Good if you’re travelling and looking for a bit of a pit stop.

Name: ONA Coffee Melbourne | Cafe Brunswick
Author: Cambell Charrier
Review: Such an amazing experience all around!!  Delicious food, even better coffee, and the best people.  I attended a latte art class with Victor and he was a great teacher.  Super patient and knowledgeable, and taught a really fun class!  Devin was also super nice and helpful when selecting coffee and giving recommendations for brunch.  11/10 experience!!

Name: ONA Coffee Melbourne | Cafe Brunswick
Author: Tianzhuo Jing
Review:
ONA Coffee Melbourne is an absolute gem in the heart of Brunswick’s iconic café district. ☕️I recently visited and was blown away by the incredible food and coffee. I ordered the beef burger, which was exceptional. The beef was top-notch, cooked to perfection, and came with crispy chips. My partner had the smoked salmon, and the salmon was fresh and flavorful.

for drinks, I went with the customer favorite from the menu, and it did not disappoint! The special blend, which combines the unique flavors of  pistachio coffee, was outstanding. The barista crafted an iced coffee that was truly one-of-a-kind. The flavor was rich and smooth—something you won’t find anywhere else. 🥳

The atmosphere is also fantastic, surrounded by lush plants 🌱 with a cozy outdoor seating area that’s perfect for relaxing.

I highly recommend this place for anyone looking for great coffee and food in Melbourne!

Name: ONA Coffee Melbourne | Cafe Brunswick
Author: Andy Tran
Review:
Beautiful and spacious Cafe in Brunswick.

Food was really tasty and with an open kitchen was able to see the chef work their magic and the food coffee was really nice.

Highly recommend if you are in the area !

Name: ONA Coffee Melbourne | Cafe Brunswick
Author: Pesco Palate
Review:
Love this cafe! The staff is always really nice. Spacious, and nice decor and menu too!

Booked a few times for large groups of around 7-8 by emailing. Service is always efficient despite our group size or how busy it is.

Didn’t take photos but their latte art is great. Especially loved the poodle! Potato rosti and kale were particularly good.

Highly recommend you stop by and try something from their tasty and interesting menu.

Name: ONA Coffee Melbourne | Cafe Brunswick
Author: Liz L
Review: Spacious warehouse turned cafe with bright decor. The service was prompt and friendly. The coffee was great, was impressed with the variety available and really enjoyed my drink. The food was delicious, the flavours and textures all blended well together. It was a nice place for a brunch catch up.

Name: Perfect Coffee
Address: P09 Dandenong Market, 16 Cleeland St, Dandenong
Author: Huoy
Review: The coffee and hot chocolate taste good. Hot matcha also taste better than the other places. Just the stuff never smile and not friendly at all. The croissant tastes ok. Give a try.

Name: Perfect Coffee
Address: P09 Dandenong Market, 16 Cleeland St, Dandenong
Author: D Yas
Review:
Our go-to place for coffee whenever we are at the market. Good coffee with all the alternative milk options, short wait times and so so many pastries, pre-packed cakes & goodies to choose from in the fridge display. They have their own little seating area just infront of the shop.

I'm glad they're here.

Name: Perfect Coffee
Address: P09 Dandenong Market, 16 Cleeland St, Dandenong
Author: Nanny Moo
Review: Awesome beautiful friendly helpful staff, as quick as I ordered a coffee it was getting made, ordered my iced coffee it was getting made, the 3 staff on are awesome and know how to work together, it wasn't busy so instead of doing nothing they did what they do and as quick as I paid it was ready to go...Tuesday 27/8/24 .I have been here twice now and nothing has changed, that's because nothing needs to....Keep up the good work girls.👌only lost a star due to atmosphere, noisy, open dining with people walking all around you....

Name: Perfect Coffee
Address: P09 Dandenong Market, 16 Cleeland St, Dandenong
Author: Cassidy Trousdale
Review: It really is perfect coffee! The pastries here are also always fresh and delicious. Very friendly and efficient service - would definitely recommend!

Name: Perfect Coffee
Address: P09 Dandenong Market, 16 Cleeland St, Dandenong
Author: donna maree
Review: Right in the heart of the fresh food precinct in Dandy Market this cafe delivers exactly what it promises-promptly made coffee at market prices= perfect coffee. Drinks are freshly made to order made via ticketed queuing system. Taste matched price point. Loads of premade cakes on offer. Takeaway or sit to enjoy the atmosphere!

Name: First Love Coffee Collins St
Author: Barnaby Lim
Review: This is a regular spot for good coffee and a croissant. There's a nice lawn outside to sit on on a nice sunny day. There's even sparkling water on tap! Conveniently located in front of the W hotel. Don't forget the point card that gets you free 10th coffee!

Name: First Love Coffee Collins St
Author: Michiel Harberts
Review: Coffee was pretty good. Service a little slow, people in front of me chose another place. Double shot latte hit the spot.

Name: First Love Coffee Collins St
Author: Sambo Da Cambo
Review:
Check out how artistic the barista is over here in their lattes, can you see the Swan 🦢  and the Lilly in these photos?
Came to check out this Cafe cause I needed a Matcha Latte afternoon hit and I was satisfied 😋 ☕
There is sparkling and straight tap water available for customers.

Name: First Love Coffee Collins St
Author: XZ C
Review:
Wonderful place for a test after busy work. Tasty coffee, cozzy music and friendly staff make my day.

Share with my friends this gorgeous store in heart of Melbourne CBD under W hotel in Collin St.

Absolutely great experience and would come back. Strongly recommend!!!

Name: First Love Coffee Collins St
Author: Steve Hui (IFLYFLAT)
Review: Perfect indoor and outdoor ☀️ spot with great coffee ☕😍.

Name: Vacation Coffee CBD
Author: Tina Spencer
Review: This is the best coffee in this area. Not too strong but with a nutty flavour. Big fan! A lot of people came in before me and they got all of our coffees through quickly. I was surprised! The food looks incredible too! I’ll be grabbing breakfast here soon.

Name: Vacation Coffee CBD
Author: daniela rodriguez
Review: Very nice place, totally worth a visit. The atmosphere and the service is very friendly and quiet. The cinnamon roll was very tasty and the latte had a spectacular flavor.

Name: Vacation Coffee CBD
Author: molly
Review:
Today I took a friend from Sydney to Vacation, having read the Urban List Review. Coffee was great. GF ricotta, banana & chocolate serving was very small. Chocolate was chocolate sprinkles. Would never have asked for that.
My real gripe is that I asked to use a ladies room. They said I’d have to walk to Collins Place. They also voluntarily advised they had a key to the apartments next door, but only staff could use it. I advised that I had Crohn’s disease and could present a commonwealth government card explaining that I am entitled to use any toilet available. They declined. I had to leave quickly. They then checked with the manager who also advised I was not able to use the facility. NOT GOOD ENOUGH

Name: Vacation Coffee CBD
Author: Greig Stuart Torpey
Review: A solid brunch spot but not the best in Melbourne. Fun little cafe with a range of coffees but limited food options. We had the banana chocolate toast and the strawberry peanut butter toast. Both were tasty but expensive for what it was and the bread was a little underwhelming. Coffee was tasty and views were good. Overall 4 stars seems reasonable.

Name: Vacation Coffee CBD
Author: Maximillion L
Review:
Went there for breakfast. It was busy with lots of customers doing takeaway. It’s more like a coffee stop rather than a cafe.

The coffee was way too smooth for my taste not enough depth, but I can see how some people like this style of coffee.

The croissants were a bit stale though the chicken sandwiches was quite nice.

Nice design of the shop but the walls are looking a bit grubby.

It was a nice experience but slightly disappointing.

Name: The Coffee Club
Author: tyra delaney
Review: Got takeaway and the girls were awfully rude! As a hospo worker myself I couldn’t believe the service. Waited 20 minutes for a croissant and coffee - milk was burnt in coffee and croissant was burnt (waitress pretty much threw croissant at me too!)

Name: The Coffee Club
Author: Carmen
Review:
The Veggie Big Breakfast is delicious! Coffee was perfectly balanced too 👍🏽

You're greeted with smiles and friendly faces as soon as you enter, the decor and vibes are beautiful in this space, I loved the seating arrangements and colour scheme.

Clean space, offering freshly cooked meals and perfectly balanced coffee!

Although it's inside the shopping centre, we sat at table number 1 (back corner) and it felt like I couldn't hear the outside noise!

Great location for friends to catch up and continue the day shopping thereafter.

Name: The Coffee Club
Author: Collette G
Review:
Firstly there is 2 coffee clubs inside the werribee plaza. This feedback is for the restaurant style Cafe and not the pop up store near Kmart.

There's a couple of issues concerning your chef Vesna which myself & my husband have had occur while this particular chef has cooked our food over the past year we have been attending the coffee club in Werribee

There has been a few things been cooked/done incorrectly over this past year however today I felt the need to make you aware of these recurring issues as they are starting to feel more like a personal issue between Vesna and myself.

For the majority of the time the orders come out for my husband right however I do understand it gets busy at times and the wrong order comes out. Around 6-8 months ago I ordered my husband's omelette and mentioned he has a dairy allergy and cannot have cream or milk in the eggs the lady who served us repeated it to us "dairy free omelette our 3 extras were Mushrooms, spinach and tomatoes.

After taking one or two bites my husband noticed the omelette had cheese and a lot of it cooked into the omelette which chef Vesna had prepared.

It's very lucky his allergy is only mild and not a serious one has anaphylactic shock is a serious and life threating medical issue which requires an EpiPen on the spot to reverse.

After getting this issue resolved with the supervisor on duty. My husband had decided to not eat at the coffee club again with me. He has had prior issues with orders coming out wrong previously and I think the lack of seriousness in this order took its toll for him.

I myself do enjoy coffee club so I continued to eat there. The next time I ate there I brought my 7 months old son and asked for my poached egg well done not runny as I'm breastfeeding feeding.

Getting my egg I cut it open to find it runny and popped open on my plate

Chef Vesna got the egg I returned threw the same egg in the boiling water then threw it back into the plate in pieces exploded open and soaking wet on the plate. I am not too sure I've ever seen such an poached egg a chef would serve like thar in my life.

Today I ordered spinach in my omelette and halfway through i realised I was eating soggy lettuce salad mix cooked thoroughly into my omelette ( picture attached)

I made one of the lady's aware and she offered a refund which I appreciated. The lady mentioned Vesna had left right after cooking my omelette so she could ask her why lettuce was cooked in there not to mention my stomach feeling nauseous since eating that omelette not sure what else was cooked in there.

I don't like thinking things are personal however I have caught Vesna giving myself and my husband but manly me very nasty looks when we come in and stand at the counter to order and while sitting which makes an extremely uncomfortable atmosphere.

It feels more than accidental when these looks are given its highly uncomfortable and feels a little personal and the repeated "mistakes" that happen when we eat there, at the end of the day we go there to enjoy food and coffee and Instead are meet with these issues everytime chef Vesna is there.

Name: The Coffee Club
Author: Jin Lee
Review: Worst coffee quality ever. Visited here at off peak time and ordered 2 flat whites for drinks. We've got spilled coffees with not enough milk foam on top and ugly latte art. Noticed this to the server but she smiled and back to work without any mention. Thanks for the terrible coffee and service. I will never come back.

Name: The Coffee Club
Author: Sadek Ali
Review:
The quality of the food and coffee is too low

The coffee club got over 300 locations around Australia and this one is one of their worst in terms of food quality even coffee is under my expectation

* our vegetarian big breakfast was a regular plate sized ( halloumi cheese was overcooked and dry, grilled mushroom cut very thin and dry, tomato relish was definitely a canned tomato sauce.. apart from that rest of ordered dishes were big disappoint.

Service was good no complaint.

Plenty seatings……

Name: Spin Coffee.
Author: manooch
Review:
Beautiful coffee, friendly service, and a lovely spot to sit and enjoy both.
Tasty croissant and coffee. Fair price .

Name: Spin Coffee.
Author: Kaz Bar
Review:
Beautiful coffee, friendly service, and a lovely spot to sit and enjoy both.
Tasty Turkish rolls and pastries too 👏

Name: Spin Coffee.
Author: Janina Nowak
Review:
Update 22june22:
Two years since my review! Still quick and friendly service. Today had lunch of Chicken Schnitzel with broccoli and herbed new  potatoes. I asked for a wedge of lemon but they did not have any! Was not interested in the gravy. All somewhat a bit dry °though a generous serving. $14.90 as it was a main and 2 sides (see menu board photo).
The Tuna sandwich as takeaway was delicious.
○○○○○○○○
Quick,  friendly service.  Food is fabulous.

Name: Spin Coffee.
Author: Jo Gamble
Review:
I'm in Melbourne for work and instead of paying the insane $35 for breakfast at the hotel,  I decided to try Spin Coffee.

Absolutely delicious Bacon and Egg Roti and Amazing Soy Flat White for only $14.50!!!

Great service and beautiful coffee!  I'll be back tomorrow 👌

Name: Spin Coffee.
Author: Nicole poetic Bloomfield justice
Review: staff an coffee excellent love the atomosphere make my morning an days better thank you

Name: The Original Cafe
Author: Richard Jackson
Review: Classic week-day breakfast + lunch place. This is our dependable local and we've been going every week for five years so you know it's good. Unpretentious atmosphere, staff do not wear leather aprons, piercings or tattoos. However coffee is one of our favourites in the area + the egg + bacon turkish rolls are always on point. Takeaway lunch special is one of the best value offers around.

Name: The Original Cafe
Author: Vahid Shahani
Review:
Lovely coffee with very friendly staff. We were just passing the cafe but the advertise for special lunch took us in. We were full enough to order food, so, we ordered coffee and taste was amazing. Definitely will return to try some of their food soon.

Thanks 😊

Name: The Original Cafe
Author: Thomas L
Review: Great service and great coffee. They use Roasting Warehouse beans and the barista has good chat and banter.

Name: The Original Cafe
Author: Mitchel Bailey
Review:
One of the best value for money lunch spots in Melbourne.
$10 Lunch with great variety. Don't expect gourmet dining but you'll be impressed.

Name: The Original Cafe
Author: Andrew Bandick
Review: Only had the bacon and eggs with a cup of coffee. Staff very friendly and a bit cheeky too. They obviously want to have a bit of fun in their job. Good on them. Here for the weekend so will be back with the 20 others in my group. Glad to see a personality behind the coffee Dennis.

Name: Manchester Press
Author: Amy (thehungrymelbgirl)
Review:
Nestled deep inside Rankins Ln, Manchester Press draws a consistent crowd with its bagels and warehouse vibes.

The last time I dined here was 7 years ago. The chorizo bagel I had back then was well put together and very tasty. The lack of customer service was, however, quite off-putting.

We recently tried Manchester Press again, and we can't fault the warm staff at all. Unfortunately, it was the food that didn't quite hit the mark this time.

We ordered the Bacon and Maple Bagel ($24.50), which on paper sounds like the perfect combination of sweet and savoury. However, it was actually quite difficult to get all the flavours together as it was quite textural. We also weren't sure whether the blueberry bagel was the best flavour to compliment this creation.

We fared a bit better with the Philly Steak Bagel ($23.80), although the chef may have gone a bit overboard with the chipotle mayo and the overall result was not that aesthetically pleasing.

Hojicha Latte ($6.50) is on its current special menu for those wanting to try something different.

It was overall a slightly underwhelming experience, but I am hoping it was only because we went on a bad day...

Name: Manchester Press
Author: Pauline
Review:
It’s been many years since I visited Manchester Press.

🥯🫐 I’ve had very fond memories of their blueberry bagel served with the best mascarpone cream mixed with berries and pistachio, so I decided to visit them again after at least 5 years. It most definitely did not disappoint even though the way they whip the cream is now a little different.

I highly recommend the mixed berry bagel if you want a sweet brunch.

🍳 We also had their Egg Benedict and it was superb with a generous serving of bacon.

🍯 Another bagel we tried was their honey ricotta toast, I would have preferred they served a bigger portion of ricotta rather than thinly spread it on the bagel. I wouldn’t mind paying more for it too. I suppose this highlights the bagel rather than the topping.

Name: Manchester Press
Author: Emily Holm
Review:
This place used to be one of the *it* places in Melbourne. And now…. It’s fine?
Our coffee was still pretty good actually. But our bagels didn’t look or taste nearly as exciting and they used to.
Don’t get me wrong… they were absolutely fine. Just nothing special. The smoked salmon looks unhappy and tasted like supermarket salmon, and the blueberries were sparing.
There was nothing super wrong, but also nothing super right.

Name: Manchester Press
Author: Ariaan
Review: Service and atmosphere is amazing here! Staff is always so friendly and it’s so cozy inside. I ordered an oat mocha and ham and cheese toasted sandwich takeaway. Oat mocha tasted bland and watery. Toasted sandwich was crunchy and buttery however there wasn’t a lot of cheese and the ham was very salty. The other sandwiches on their menu look amazing though so definitely recommend trying one of those instead! Bagels looked delicious!

Name: Manchester Press
Author: Cindy Ramli Chang
Review:
Brunch date with the one and only on our wedding anniversary day ❤️
That day I decided that I do not like bagels 🤣
The sandwich was beautiful
Bagels topping was good but I just figured that I do not like that texture. So dense 🤣
But apparently “Good bagels are denser than most bread,”
Well guess I just don’t like it 🙈

The place was buzzing with people enjoying their food. Young, old, kids, adult, they all enjoying the vibes there.
Will come back for more sandwiches 😁

#bulgogi_n_rendang

Name: Cafe Belt Melbourne
Author: Tamara K
Review: Came to the cafe to sit in and have a coffee. I had the Kenya cold brew and the Ethiopian espresso and both were lovely. I would recommend the cold brew for those who are in the mood for a mellow and comfortable flavour profile. They served the espresso with soda water which I really appreciated. Staff were friendly and very sweet. The cafe itself had a very calm and welcoming vibe with their coffee on display. They also had a map on the wall with the coffee origins and flavour notes which I really enjoyed. I would happily visit again.

Name: Cafe Belt Melbourne
Author: Joy H
Review: One of the best cafes in the city for some “me” time! Great coffee, cozy vibe, and welcoming staff. You won’t regret it!

Name: Cafe Belt Melbourne
Author: Gabrielle G
Review: This is the best coffee shop owner I've ever met!! A lovely Korean couple! The coffee is at least in the top 10 in Melbourne! The taste is balanced and smooth, and there is absolutely no burnt coffee bean taste. They are very nice and warm, and even on busy weekends, they will personally deliver the coffee to you if you are waiting outside of shop. I had the best coffee experience here. Today I chatted with barista for a while, and the lovely girl gave me a rose. My good mood starts from now! 🩷 I love you!!

Name: Cafe Belt Melbourne
Author: Sam Arora
Review:
Was hard to believe that a place can't have all 5 star reviews but the coffee here is sensational and that's coming from someone who is quite fussy about coffee.

It's a small cozy space so inside is fairly quiet

Name: Cafe Belt Melbourne
Author: 张家威
Review: If you live in fast pace and wanna slow down a bit, please come through their magic door and have a dine in coffee. Warm and welcoming vibe, cozy space! You will 100% not go wrong!

Name: Cathedral Coffee
Author: Jay W
Review:
DELICIOUS FLAT WHITE!

This cute cafe is set in a cathedrals setting just off the main busy road.

The flat white here was one of our top coffees here.

The service was quick and friendly!

Will recommend here :)

Name: Cathedral Coffee
Author: Boo C
Review: Not easy to find a coffee shop still open after 5 but here it is. Both flat white and magic are nice! Very friendly staff.

Name: Cathedral Coffee
Author: Quincy Malesovas
Review: Go-to meeting spot in the city because they serve coffee late and it’s always good. Can get busy though so arrive early if you need to save a spot.

Name: Cathedral Coffee
Author: Phil Malone
Review: Quaint little cafe in a quiet arcade location off Flinders Lane. Yummy cookies and croissants and good quality coffee and chai. Definitely worth a visit and has a Parisian feel about it.

Name: Cathedral Coffee
Author: Kyle Rich
Review: Wine and coffee - great pairing in the cutest arcade! I loved it here. One of the only spots serving good coffee till late! Great vibes here nestled just off of the busy street.

Name: Pascoed Coffee
Author: Xiaohan Pan
Review: Excellent service with 5-star coffee! Latte art is absolutely impressive! Will come back for more 🥰

Name: Pascoed Coffee
Author: Leena H
Review:
Nice modern style local cute cafe just opened 4 months ago.

Great customer service and attentive. Detailed coffee and fresh salad 🥑🥗

☆5 Beautiful Humbler Proud Mary fresh coffee beans. Perfect ratio of long black. It comes in 3/4 amount in a normal size cup for a long black. Light to Medium, smooth, clean, no bitter or acidity, touch of sweetness and fresh after taste. 👍

For latte, I would prefer 3/4 amount for stronger coffee  to my liking😋

I really enjoyed my long black ☕️😊

Name: Pascoed Coffee
Author: WK S
Review:
Awesome coffee, food and very lovely staff.

As a new mum who recently just moved to the area, I’ve always hoped to find a good cafe locally to brighten up those postpartum days!

Owner Barry is very lovely and not only does he make good coffee, he and the staff always go above and beyond to serve us. Very friendly and welcoming. Also it’s spacious enough for prams.

Have definitely found my go-to cafe! Can’t wait for bubba to try his first babycino when he’s old enough :)

Name: Pascoed Coffee
Author: The Ghost
Review:
Great coffee and cool people

Ordered a menemen, the best menemen I’ve tasted!

Will try the menemen with Sujuk and cheese as suggested by chef

Name: Pascoed Coffee
Author: India Ross
Review:
Brand new coffee shop (only about 2 months old)
Great smiles, great coffee, what more do you want....maybe amazing doughnuts?... Which they have!

Do yourself a favour and pop in for a doughnut and a coffee or their iced lemon tea - it was too good that I finished it before taking a photo.

Name: Indus Specialty Coffee
Author: Hagop Kojadelian
Review:
Beautiful coffee, delicious jaffle! Ajay gave me a taste of their new batch brew as well and it was amazing.

My partner and I moved around the corner. I needed something to eat and a coffee as we're getting everything into the house.

I'll definitely be back, thanks guys!

Name: Indus Specialty Coffee
Author: Rachel Kennedy
Review: New destination coffee in St Kilda. Had one of the brightest, most interesting espressos I have tasted in a long time. Beans are also great. The fact that the owner is so friendly on top of the quality makes this cafe a fast favorite in Melbourne. He took over quite recently so the selection is different from what was previously at this location. The coffee is in a league of its own.

Name: Indus Specialty Coffee
Author: Priyanka Pendam
Review: Great coffee, staff is always happy to serve, owner is also friendly. They’re offering 3$ coffees until end of September, which is a great deal!

Name: Indus Specialty Coffee
Author: Helene Barrow
Review: Easily the best specialty coffee in the broader area! Run by a friendly Q-grader using Zest beans - they make smooth, flavourful, nuanced, delicious coffee with top notch, clean equipment (never a burned or ashy taste, just perfect extraction). Aje and his crew will not let you down. If you want real coffee, or to impress someone with real coffee, this is your spot!

Name: Indus Specialty Coffee
Author: Tristan
Review: Medi Coffee House is a charming little cafe next to a barber shop, easy to miss if you're walking too fast. They have limited seating but a convenient takeaway window. I thoroughly enjoyed my coffee, which had a delightful blend of fruity, acidic, and bitter notes that paired perfectly with my chosen milk. It's definitely not a coffee you drink and forget.

Name: Golden Mug Cafe
Address: Ground Floor of Novotel Hotel, 399 Little Lonsdale St, Melbourne
Author: Elaine V. Reglos
Review:
Went to dine in with my grandma and auntie as they were on a tourist visa. We spotted this cozy cute cafe and tried some of their dish.

The amount of the food is huge and delicious. Coffee is very good as well, we have experience the best customer service as well her name’s Vanessa. Will definitely come back for another experience!

Name: Golden Mug Cafe
Address: Ground Floor of Novotel Hotel, 399 Little Lonsdale St, Melbourne
Author: Jennie
Review: The steak at this restaurant is an unforgettable delight. Each bite of the steak is tender, juicy, and perfectly grilled, with a delicious flavor. The prawn salad is refreshing and delicious, with fresh prawn complemented by crisp lettuce and salad dressing, bringing a refreshing seafood flavor. As for the garlic cheese bread, it's a wonderful combination, with a soft texture and a surface sprinkled with rich cheese and garlic flavor, each bite is a delight. Overall, this meal is satisfying and a feast for the taste buds.

Name: Golden Mug Cafe
Address: Ground Floor of Novotel Hotel, 399 Little Lonsdale St, Melbourne
Author: Hara
Review: I had high expectations, but my experience was disappointing. I ordered the bacon eggs benedict, and the eggs were too sour. Is this really bacon? There wasn't even a hash brown! The place felt busy but didn't deliver on quality. I won't be coming back

Name: Golden Mug Cafe
Address: Ground Floor of Novotel Hotel, 399 Little Lonsdale St, Melbourne
Author: Yosy Christy Natalia
Review:
Perfect spot to grab your late afternoon coffee cuz they open until 6pm!

Comfortable sitting table on the Ground floor of Novotel hotel. They have so many various of foods (breakfast, lunch and dinner menu). Coffee is also great!

Highly recommend!

Name: Golden Mug Cafe
Address: Ground Floor of Novotel Hotel, 399 Little Lonsdale St, Melbourne
Author: Sylvia Liu
Review: We had a delightful brunch at this spot, enjoying a variety of dishes including chili scramble, egg royal, seafood penne pasta, and a fresh fruit bowl. The drinks were also standout, with both the chai latte and iced taro being refreshing and flavorful. Everything was delicious.

Name: Project 281 Coffee Roasters
Author: Mary Borjal
Review:
Head here for a quick brunch before our flight. Service was great even though it was very busy we had our coffee and food taken straight away. The coffee is very strong though! We were a bit shocked. Very delicious food! It came quickly as well. They even stored our luggage.

Thank you very much team! Definitely recommend this place!

Name: Project 281 Coffee Roasters
Author: Gopi Bala
Review:
I ordered the apple crumble French Toast.

Aesthetics wise, it looked great. However, the sourdough bread was lacking the usual French toast taste.

The rhubarb sauce and custard went well with the toast.

Overall a very nice place to dine in and with excellent service.

A great find in Brunswick area.

Name: Project 281 Coffee Roasters
Author: Max Weichelt
Review: I have been here for brunch 5 times, and I can't keep coming back! The Eggs Benedict is the best I've had in the city, the ingredients are slightly unorthodox but balance perfectly with the overall flavours of the meal. The coffee is freshly roasted, the staff are fantastic and I can't wait to visit next time!

Name: Project 281 Coffee Roasters
Author: Lauris Jones
Review: Great cafe for a brunch catchup. We shared the prawn okonomayake which was very nice.  Serving sizes are generous, well presented and staff were attentive.

Name: Project 281 Coffee Roasters
Author: Sophie Chan
Review: Ordered the chicken and waffles. There were only two tiny pieces of chicken, quite dry and burnt as well, very disappointing. The waffle was soggy and very sweet. The dish with the sauces was very sweet in general so it was not very enjoyable. Came here with quite high expectation, nice vibes but food wise, I do not recommend.

Name: Dukes Coffee Roasters
Author: Trevor “tangowhisky37” Warren
Review:
Grab yourself an excellent cup of coffee before you head off to work. The size of the line outside was a bit unnerving but it moved really quickly and the service was great. The place isn't too big, but the baristas know their job, keep up the pace while delivering a great brew.

Calling all coffee aficionados, all roads lead to Dukes on Flinders lane!!!!

Name: Dukes Coffee Roasters
Author: 뜌니Dduny
Review: I come to Melbourne and have 2-3 cups of coffee a day as a standard. I think I know why Dukes Coffee is famous for its beans. The beans taste clean and taste strong. Milk foam is also soft and delicious. I drank flat white and overall it's a good combination. But I'm a little disappointed that it lacks a bit of savory taste.

Name: Dukes Coffee Roasters
Author: MazCal
Review:
Flinders lane - the line outside was a good indication that this place must be good, so we thought we’d give it a go.
There should be more Certified organic coffee roasters in Melbourne ~ this was good to see.
Was a little surprised that they only offered 2 types of milk - skinny & oat 🤷🏻‍♀️ being an almond milk drinker I opted for neither, but my husband had a skinny & he thought it was great - rich & flavoursome, fragrant & not too bitter in taste👌🏽

They also stock a small range of desserts.

Service is fast!

Name: Dukes Coffee Roasters
Author: Jay W
Review:
Busy Ambient cafe within Melbourne CBD.

There were alot of customers here and it looked like a spot where people would sit down and read books as they sipped away at morning coffee.

The staff were friendly and the service was quick.

Unfortunately our oat flat white had splitting of the oat milk, which made it quite unpleasant to drink. The cows milk flat white was okay but was quick milky.

The service ans the atmosphere was so great that i would be willing to go back and try the coffee again!

Name: Dukes Coffee Roasters
Author: Cynthia (Cynful Recommends)
Review:
ig/tiktok: @cynfulrecommends

🤍 𝐅𝐥𝐚𝐭 𝗪𝐡𝐢𝐭𝐞 - AUD 5⁣
Went with Dukes’ organic blend which was creamy, nutty and had notes of milk chocolate and cherry. A pretty decent cuppa!⁣
⁣
🖤 𝐁𝐥𝐚𝐜𝐤 - AUD 5⁣
My partner went with the organic blend as well. Without the milk, we got notes of red apple and caramel, where the black coffee was crispy, and vibrant. ⁣
⁣
🥐 𝐂𝐫𝐨𝐢𝐬𝐬𝐚𝐧𝐭 - AUD 4.50⁣
⁣A pretty standard croissant that served its purpose in keeping up filled till lunch
⁣
Located in the CBD, near Flinders Street station, it’s definitely a convenient and decent coffee spot to grab your morning coffees. ⁣

Name: Veneziano Coffee Richmond
Author: Mish
Review:
Not the best vibes. When I think brunch I think happy smiling staff and a hustling bustling atmosphere. This wasnt it - wish I read the more recent reviews before we came in.

Service wasn’t great. The place was half empty on a weekend. Many staff around who conveniently ignored us standing at the please wait to be seated sign. Finally managed to flag down a staff member and she told us to feel free to pick a table anywhere ourselves.

At our table, same story, kept waiting for staff to come take our order. The guy who gave us menus seemed so annoyed. Same story with another staff when we ordered desserts and when we went to pay our bill. Overall we found service was very slow, inattentive and unwelcoming.

Food was decent but portions are a bit small for the prices.

Name: Veneziano Coffee Richmond
Author: CHLOE
Review:
I really enjoyed this spacious café with an on-site roaster—it’s a clear sign the coffee is top-notch, and it definitely lived up to expectations!

We visited for brunch on the weekend and tried both the white and black coffee, which were excellent. The food was also delicious.

I’ll definitely be returning and highly recommend this place.

Name: Veneziano Coffee Richmond
Author: M G
Review: This cafe has a beautiful decoration and a spacious setting. Their raspberry crescent is an absolute delight—it’s a treat for your taste buds. Pair it with a cup of coffee, and you’re in for a delightful experience.

Name: Veneziano Coffee Richmond
Author: Michael Gay
Review:
Aspire Coffee 8.6
Elevate 9.4
Tried Elevate last visit and ding ding ding we have a winner,
Rich coffee with a slight back taste of a good chocolate and not overly strong.
Definitely worth revising the Stars from 3 to 5 and could easily be the best coffee in Richmond let alone Melbourne.

Original.
100mts from home so i persisted after having a few different blends and not finding the right match for me.
The 'Aspire Organic' is a real winner, nice middle road blend that makes you wish it was bottomless

Name: Veneziano Coffee Richmond
Author: Deshal Patel
Review: Relaxing and spacious atmosphere! One of the best coffees one can have in Melbourne! I highly recommend trying the blend 'Soar'.

Name: Best Bean Best Cup Coffee Roasters
Author: Natalie Veljanovska
Review: `The coffee you make is delicious and keeps me driving down to you guys daily for my coffee fix. You clearly take pride in your craft, and  I really appreciate the friendly, attentive service you provide which makes my visit very enjoyable… During most of my visits I come with my toddler and jimmy and crew always look after me , and ensure I am able to order and receive my coffee quickly, not only that , they go out of their way to bring me my coffee to my car even if they are super busy ! I can’t speak highly enough of their service and recommend for anyone to try their coffee !

Name: Best Bean Best Cup Coffee Roasters
Author: Leena H
Review:
Best Bean Best Cup Coffee Roasters located inside the industrial areas.

Their coffee is full strength without adjusting to extra shot long black. Large size long black was rich, bitter cocoa, touch of acidity and fresh after taste.
Latte comes with 3/4 glass and perfect ratio of milk and coffee 👌😋

Name: Best Bean Best Cup Coffee Roasters
Author: Nate Kenny
Review: The owner is the nicest bloke in the world, and makes a good coffee in addition. The beans are also the ones i usually use at home and they make good pour over.

Name: Best Bean Best Cup Coffee Roasters
Author: Gianni Renda
Review: I just bought two bags of coffee from here and to say it's like crack is an understatement. The Vietnamese roast in particular is so good! The service is exceptional and their focus on details with the roasting and blending is impeccable. Serious hidden gem here.

Name: Best Bean Best Cup Coffee Roasters
Author: Ellie Sadrinajafi
Review: Great local, specialty coffee. True Melbourne-quality standard! Love having these guys in the area, and so friendly!

Name: Oli & Levi Cafe
Author: Kristy
Review: Cute little place. The staff are friendly.. I tried a cheddar and jalepeno egg tart, a cream cheese sesame seed Bagel and an OJ. Good prices and delicious.

Name: Oli & Levi Cafe
Author: Leandra C
Review: Cute cafe with awesome coffee. Haven’t tried their food items yet though but looks good.

Name: Oli & Levi Cafe
Author: A Bat
Review:
I stumbled into this cafe while on the hunt for breakfast during early morning, and I am so glad they are open because I was starving.

I ordered a small latte and a hashbrown wrap, and they both came pretty quickly. Admittedly, the latte tasted pretty weak, then again, I am used to stronger coffees. The hashbrown wrap, however, has the very right balance of flavours, the thick slice of eggs, the hash potatoes, the bacon, and the thin layer of barbecue sauce woke my stomach up very well.

Both items came together around AUD12, it was a nice start of the morning

Name: Oli & Levi Cafe
Author: Hiromi
Review: I had a delicious sandwich(chicken, mayo and avo on Grain) and latte.  Cozy cafe with nice music. Friendly staff.

Name: Oli & Levi Cafe
Author: Ye Ma
Review: Walked in right after trading hours, which was my fault of not checking properly. The owner made my coffee anyway and let me stayed as long as I needed. She's even kind enough to offer me a few blueberries for free. As a traveller to Melbourne, I felt very walm welcomed. A pleasant experience indeed.

Name: Juliette Coffee & Munch
Author: manooch
Review: I had coffee there. 2 times. Coffee was nice and warm. Also the staff are polite. Not a big one but one of the good one.

Name: Juliette Coffee & Munch
Author: Ajin Francis Anto
Review: A very simple yet pleasing cafe experience. I casually dropped in during which I think were their busy hours and was surprised to find their Barista single handedly dealing the cafe with utmost patience. Thank you Neha for your customer service, especially for your suggestions on my cappuccino. Cheers!

Name: Juliette Coffee & Munch
Author: Kimberley Grace
Review:
This has become our daily coffee stop on our morning walk. The new owners are so nice, always remember our names and orders, and they love our little pup!

The coffee is great, we drink a long black so there's no where to hide if the coffee isn't good!

Name: Juliette Coffee & Munch
Author: Pebble_ -
Review: This is a great little hidden cafe. Great vegetarian options and delicious coffee. Always good service from the fella that runs the place.

Name: Juliette Coffee & Munch
Author: G M
Review:
Juliette Bakery has always been a favorite spot for me due to its efficient service and wide variety of delicious offerings. However, my recent visits have left me disappointed.

Since the change in ownership, there has been a noticeable decline in both the quality of the food and the service. The coffee, once rich and flavorful, now lacks the same appeal. The food quality has also suffered, and the once extensive variety seems to have diminished.

The service has become exceedingly slow. On multiple occasions, my orders took an unreasonably long time to be prepared. Additionally, there seems to be a lack of basic skills among the staff; they struggled with simple tasks like toasting a sandwich properly.

It genuinely feels bad to leave such feedback for a small business, but I hope this review serves as constructive criticism. Juliette Bakery has the potential to return to its former glory with some necessary improvements in both service and food quality. I truly hope to see positive changes soon

A reply to the owner:

Thank you for your feedback. While I disagree with the notion that leaving negative comments makes someone a sad person, I believe this platform is meant for providing honest reviews about businesses. I have also left positive reviews for places that I found truly amazing and continuously improving.

You have the right to disagree with negative feedback about your business, but I believe that acknowledging and addressing constructive criticism is essential for growth and improvement. Julian’s team (the previous owners) set a high standard for Juliette.

I hope you can see this feedback as an opportunity to enhance the quality of your services.

Name: Puzzle Coffee
Address: Southern Cross, Spencer St &, Collins St, Docklands
Author: Janelle
Review: Tried the iced cappuccino here which was recommended by the staff, it's SO GOOD! Liked how it has a layer of cream on top, plus their own Riddle coffee blend, overall tasted so smooth and it's just awesome!!🤍 Also got to try their Strawberry Matcha, Strawberry Yogurt and Yuzu Espresso Sparkling (seasonal specials), love the sparkling yuzu espresso the most, it was very refreshing but also not overpowering the taste of coffee. Will definitely come back again for their coffee🤤

Name: Puzzle Coffee
Address: Southern Cross, Spencer St &, Collins St, Docklands
Author: Oracle Chow
Review:
good place for a quick caffeine fix! very conveniently located, with friendly staff 💓

ordered:
✦ iced strawberry matcha ($8.00)
✦ hot hojicha ($5.50) + edible grain cup ($3)
✦ ham & cheese croissant ($7.50)
✦ peanut butter & choc cookie ($5.99)

Name: Puzzle Coffee
Address: Southern Cross, Spencer St &, Collins St, Docklands
Author: Elle
Review:
Amazing coffee and service!! Great place for a pit stop and reasonably priced too!
Not to mention the yummy snacks

Name: Puzzle Coffee
Address: Southern Cross, Spencer St &, Collins St, Docklands
Author: 서동훈
Review: The cafe was really blusting, so I wasn’t expecting much in terms of service, but the manager and staff were super friendly and quick. In addition, the coffee tasted good enough.

Name: Puzzle Coffee
Address: Southern Cross, Spencer St &, Collins St, Docklands
Author: Julie Egitto
Review:
good coffee and have an option of edible cup.
was a good experience with the cup. just wish it the cup comes on different flavours, like cinnamon and honey or a chocolate one.

Name: Puzzle Coffee Melbourne Central
Author: Leon Chai
Review: This coffee shop is an absolute gem! The interior decor is very distinctive, blending modern minimalism with vintage elements, making you feel incredibly comfortable as soon as you step in. The variety of coffee is impressive, catering to all tastes whether you prefer a robust espresso or a refreshing cold brew. I especially recommend their pour-over coffee, which has a delicate taste and a rich aroma, allowing you to savour the pure essence of the coffee beans. The desserts are also a highlight, each one meticulously crafted and delicious, particularly their cheesecake, which is creamy and melts in your mouth. Moreover, the staff are warm and attentive, always greeting you with a smile, making you feel right at home. Whether you're looking to relax alone or catch up with friends, this place is an excellent choice. In short, this coffee shop is well worth a visit and won't disappoint!

Name: Puzzle Coffee Melbourne Central
Author: Jen
Review:
☕️COFFEE FIX☕️

Been meaning to try puzzle coffee for so long and it did not disappoint. I enjoyed all the drinks, but surprisingly I really liked their yuzu sparkling! The strawberry matcha wasn’t as strong as I would like and quite subtle in flavour. Overall a good spot to get a pick me up - I also got their white coffee which was perfect. 👌

Featured:
🍵 strawberry matcha
✨ yuzu sparkling
☕️ white coffee

Thank you for the invite.

Name: Puzzle Coffee Melbourne Central
Author: Larc Justine Chan
Review: Poor matcha taste. Almost tasteless. Even worse is their customer service. No enthusiasm and didnt feel like we were welcome.

Name: Puzzle Coffee Melbourne Central
Author: Eat with Josephine
Review:
I'm huge fan of puzzle coffee for when I want to indulge in an iced beverage because their variety is always good.

Having previously tried their strawberry matcha, I was eager to order it again. However, this time, there seemed to be a shortage of strawberries, throwing off the overall balance a bit.

On the other hand, hojicha, a personal favourite of mine, had a nice roasted flavour throughout. The yakult sparkling drink proved to be incredibly refreshing, hitting all the right spots, especially on the day we visited when the temperature soared to 35 degrees. To round off the experience, the croissant was light and crispy but fell into the category of being quite average, lacking any standout qualities.

I'd suggest giving their drinks a try since that's their specialty, despite some occasional inconsistency, they are still quite good! With multiple locations around Melbourne too.

Puzzle coffee was kind enough to invite me out to try out their menu.

Name: Puzzle Coffee Melbourne Central
Author: Luong Gia Linh
Review: Served by sweet Audrey! Best service ever

Name: Twenty & Six Espresso
Author: Kiah W
Review: I don’t think I’ve ever taken pictures of food before this moment. 10/10 fish tacos and 10/10 limited iced strawberry matcha latte. Also fam’s latte art was exceptional

Name: Twenty & Six Espresso
Author: Hiromi
Review: Pulled pork benny and latte were delicious. Cute latte art made me happy.  Cozy and nice atmosphere. Friendly staff. Highly recommended.

Name: Twenty & Six Espresso
Author: Hanmo Li
Review:
Brunch at @twentyandsix from a few months ago: good brunch staples in a nice and cosy shop!
.
Zucchini and corn fritters with bacon: yummy fritters!.
Smashed avocado with poached egg
Matcha latte

Name: Twenty & Six Espresso
Author: Habibah Ghazali
Review: Love this cafe. The coffee always good and not overpriced. The food that we have tried are all yummy! The owner and his staff are very nice and friendly too! The best coffee in North Melbourne.

Name: Twenty & Six Espresso
Author: Olga Balaganskaya
Review:
It's one of my favourite cafes. I love this place for their delicious and healthy dishes and a nice, quiet atmosphere.
Place features:
- Food and drinks.
- Breakfasts and lunches.
- Original serving.
- Moderate prices.
- Dogs friendly.
- Indoor and outdoor areas.
- Friendly stuff.

Name: Overlay Coffee
Author: ShengJin
Review: The store is not big but clean and tidy. The peanut latte is especially delicious. I drank it several times and the concentration of peanuts was different each time. But overall it is still very good.

Name: Overlay Coffee
Author: Kwan Jason
Review:
The drinks are fabulous!

Peanut Cream Latte is too much so try the peanut cream matcha, really good, trust me.

Limited space, can takeaway and city walk

Name: Overlay Coffee
Author: Harinduni Kannangara
Review:
I wanted to see if this place lived up to the hype, and if you go for the Peanut Butter Cream Latte, I'd say it definitely does! It was my first time trying peanut butter in coffee, and it was creamy, delicious, and so satisfying.

The place was packed—everyone was there to try their coffees—but the service was impressively fast. Even with more than 10-12 people waiting, I got my coffee in under 4 minutes. Definitely worth trying the Peanut Butter Coffee!

Name: Overlay Coffee
Author: Gwen Lin
Review: Very popular cafe with warm staff and amazing peanut butter latte with oat milk for me. Usually wait a long line on the weekends, but not a long wait to get your cafe and breaks ! I would love to visit every week when I am in the CBD, highly recommended 👍

Name: Overlay Coffee
Author: Bảo Hân Phạm
Review: The first time I drunk peanut cream latte about two-three weeks ago that was totally perfect. The peanut was so condensed and the peanut taste was so obvious. But I feel like the recipe is quite different today and some previous days. I feel the cream was too washy and maybe less peanut? Although it is still good, I’m quite disappointed. I really miss the old taste 😭 it is not good as the first time.

Name: Top Notch Coffee
Author: Patompon Putpipat
Review:
coffee is so cheap. small flat white is just 4.
staff are so good. so friendly.
the cafe vibes also nice. near the tram stop so easy to just stop and grab coffee!

Name: Top Notch Coffee
Author: Mauro Miglino
Review: With infinite charm and an amazing vibe, Top notch coffee,is without a shroud of doubt,one of the best coffee shops in Melbourne,hands down. Enveloped by sheer opulence and impressive aesthetics, as well as offering the highest level of standards and professionalism,as well as pertinacity, the lovely and hard working staff members that work in this outstanding coffee shop,are passionate about their work, and will  incessantly go out of their way to ensure that they consistently provide the best coffees and food that you will ever experience in Melbourne. This high level of proficiency and standards as well as being pertinacious and diligent about their work and how they consistently serve their respective customers with the pinnacle of superlative skills and sublime professionalism, is in essence conducive in enhancing as well as elevating an amazing experience at coming to Top notch coffee to purchase their absolutely amazing food and sublime coffees. The staff that work at Top notch coffee are always so warm, friendly and accommodating, in fact so much so,that they even address their customers by their names, will always welcome them with a warm smile. In conclusion to my review, and in the interest of truth and veracity, if you are in dire need of experiencing some of the best coffees you'll ever have the pleasure of tasting in Melbourne, then I both highly and vehemently encourage that you try Top notch coffee, I can assure you will not be disappointed, the coffees here are on another level, and the staff are so sweet and friendly, not to mention some of the most proficient, passionate, diligent, and hard working people I have ever met,try this outstanding coffee shop, you will be so glad you did ❤️❤️❤️❤️❤️🙏🙏🙏🙏

Name: Top Notch Coffee
Author: Beau Tanton
Review: By far the best coffee in Moonee Ponds. Never too hot, smooth blend, service is impeccable, so friendly and welcoming. Food and treats are of the highest quality. Highly recommend.

Name: Top Notch Coffee
Author: rebekah
Review:
I came here because of the high reviews, I'm a chai lover and saw some people commented on how nice it was. the shop is so cute and clean, the staff are really friendly and knew names of customers. the whole place just had a friendly atmosphere to it

I was really let down by the drinks though, the chai didn't have much flavour to it, I wouldn't come back. my dad loves his coffees but also didn't really enjoy it from here

seems like a lovely store, if you're not too picky with your drinks it's nice to visit, but otherwise I'd recommend going elsewhere

Name: Top Notch Coffee
Author: Sydney Scott
Review: Kevin is a legend! A coffee wizard if you will🤭 thanks so much for serving up some of the best coffee in Melbourne!

Name: Path Melbourne
